(2) ~~Closure-held `measure_cache` survives `graph.destroy()`~~ — **resolved 2026-04-03:** `on_message` now clears `measure_cache` and calls `clear_cache()` on both INVALIDATE and TEARDOWN. (3) `SvgBoundsAdapter` regex may match nested `<svg>` elements or content inside XML comments/CDATA — resolved: strip `<!--...-->` and `<![CDATA[...]]>` before viewBox extraction; document single-root-SVG constraint; expose `SvgParser` protocol so users can opt in their own parser. (4) ~~`ImageSizeAdapter` returns mutable references~~ — **resolved 2026-04-03:** `measure_image` now returns `dict(dims)` (shallow copy).

---

title: Runner protocol and async utilities
body:
- **No ThreadRunner default (pre-1.0):** Python removes `ThreadRunner` (daemon-thread-per-coroutine). Users must explicitly call `set_default_runner(AsyncioRunner.from_running())` or `set_default_runner(TrioRunner(nursery))`. TS has no equivalent concern (native async). If graphrefly-ts adds a non-browser runtime (e.g. Deno, Bun workers), a similar explicit runner setup should be required.
- **`call_soon_threadsafe` always (no loop-thread detection):** Async utilities always use `loop.call_soon_threadsafe()` for cross-boundary scheduling. The earlier `_is_loop_thread` optimization was removed because first-call heuristic was fundamentally broken (cached the wrong thread id if first called from a non-loop thread). If perf profiling shows `call_soon_threadsafe` as a bottleneck, revisit with `asyncio.get_running_loop() == loop` (try/except for non-async callers).
- **`to_async_iter` yields on both DATA and RESOLVED:** Yields the current value on `RESOLVED` (no-change cycles) in addition to `DATA` values. This ensures repeated identical values (e.g. `of(1, 2, 2)` feeding through a derived) are all yielded. TS should match if it adds an equivalent utility.
- **Cancel race prevention:** `AsyncioRunner` and `TrioRunner` use a `cancelled` flag checked inside `_create_task`/`_wrapper` to handle cancellation before the task/scope is created. TS equivalent should guard similarly.
- **Structured concurrency preservation:** Runner wrappers re-raise `CancelledError` (asyncio) and `Cancelled` (trio) instead of routing them to `on_error`. This preserves structured-concurrency contracts. TS should re-throw `AbortError` / framework-specific cancellation signals similarly.

title: AI surface behavioral semantics parity
body:
Cross-language notes for `patterns.ai` / `graphrefly.patterns.ai`. **Keep this subsection aligned in both repos’ `docs/optimizations.md`.**

| Topic | Resolution |
|-------|------------|
| **`agent_loop` / `agentLoop` — LLM adapter output** | `invoke` may return a plain `LLMResponse`, or any `NodeInput` (including `Node`, awaitables, async iterables). Implementations coerce with `fromAny` / `from_any`, prefer a synchronous `get()` when it already holds an `LLMResponse`, then **block until the first settled `DATA`** (`subscribe` + `Promise` in TypeScript; `first_value_from` in Python). Do not unsubscribe immediately after `subscribe` without waiting for emissions. |
| **`toolRegistry` / `tool_registry` — handler output** | Handlers may return plain values, Promise-like values, or reactive `NodeInput`. **TypeScript:** `execute` awaits Promise-likes, then resolves **only** `Node` / `AsyncIterable` via `fromAny` + first `DATA` (do **not** pass arbitrary strings through `fromAny` — it treats strings as iterables and emits per character). **Python:** `execute` uses `from_any` + `first_value_from` only for awaitables, async iterables, or `Node`; plain values return as-is. |
| **~~`agentMemory` / `agent_memory` — factory scope (implemented, verified 2026-04-07)~~** | Fully wired in both TS and PY. All five primitives (`knowledgeGraph`/`knowledge_graph`, `vectorIndex`/`vector_index`, `lightCollection`/`light_collection`, `decay`, `autoCheckpoint`/`auto_checkpoint`) are opt-in via options. Retrieval pipeline (4-stage: vector search + KG expansion + scoring + budget packing) and periodic reflection also wired. |

title: _invoke_llm defensive alignment
body: | **3** | **~~`_invoke_llm` / `_invokeLLM` defensive alignment (resolved 2026-04-07 — already implemented)~~** | Both ports reject `None`/`null`, reject plain `str`, normalize `dict` with `content` key to `LLMResponse`, and route remaining shapes through `from_any` + `first_value_from`. Aligned. |

title: LLMInvokeOptions + cooperative cancellation
body: | **4** | **~~`LLMInvokeOptions` + cooperative cancellation (implemented 2026-04-07)~~** | Both ports now wire cancellation from the agent loop into `adapter.invoke()`. **TS:** `AbortSignal` via `LLMInvokeOptions.signal`. **PY:** `CancellationToken` protocol (`core/cancellation.py`) with `.is_cancelled` + `.on_cancel(fn)`, backed by `threading.Event`; wired from `AgentLoopGraph._cancel_token` into `LLMInvokeOptions.cancellation_token`. |

title: keepalive subscription cleanup on destroy
body: | **keepalive subscription cleanup on destroy** | `ChatStreamGraph`, `ToolRegistryGraph`, and `system_prompt_builder` create keepalive subscriptions (`n.subscribe(lambda: None)`) that are never cleaned up. **Auto-fixable:** add `destroy()` methods that unsubscribe keepalive sinks to prevent leaks in long-lived processes. |

title: AgentLoopGraph.destroy() cancel running loop
body: | **`AgentLoopGraph.destroy()` does not cancel running loop (resolved — internal abort signal)** | `destroy()` sets an internal cancellation flag (`threading.Event`); the `run()` loop checks it between iterations. No polling — reactive cancellation via event signal. Rejected: (b) reject-only (doesn't stop the LLM call); (c) document-as-limitation (violates `destroy()` safety contract). |

title: chat_stream.clear() + append() race
body: | **`chat_stream.clear()` + `append()` race (resolved — serialize via `batch()`)** | Both `clear()` and `append()` internally use `batch()` so they are atomic within a reactive cycle. Callers who need deterministic ordering across multiple mutations use `batch(lambda: (stream.clear(), stream.append(msg)))`. No new mechanism needed — uses existing protocol. Rejected: (b) arbitrary "clear wins" rule; (c) microtask queue (fights the reactive-not-queued invariant). |

title: CancellationToken protocol
body: **Resolved (2026-03-31, implemented 2026-04-04):** `CancellationToken` protocol and `cancellation_token()` factory implemented in `core/cancellation.py`. Exported from `graphrefly.core`. Backed by `threading.Event` with lock-protected callback list, `is_cancelled` property, `on_cancel(fn)` returning an unsubscribe callable, and `cancel()` method. Wiring into async sources (`from_timer`, `from_awaitable`, `from_async_iter`) deferred — the protocol is ready. `TEARDOWN`-via-unsubscribe remains the primary cancellation path; the token is for external/cooperative cancellation.

title: Dual-bookkeeping of derived deps + connect edges
body: **~~Dual-bookkeeping of derived deps + connect edges (all phases, noted 2026-04-05, resolved 2026-04-05):~~** `Graph.add()` now auto-registers edges from constructor deps in both TS and PY. Eliminates the divergence risk between node-level deps and graph-level edge registry. All graph factories no longer need explicit `connect()` calls for constructor deps.

title: takeUntil DATA-only trigger
body: | **`takeUntil` / `take_until` + notifier `DIRTY` (resolved — DATA-only trigger)** | The notifier must emit **`DATA`** to terminate the primary. `DIRTY` is phase-1 transient signaling; termination is permanent and must only trigger on settled phase-2 data. Aligns with compat adapter rule (ignore DIRTY waves). A notifier that only sends DIRTY+RESOLVED (no payload change) never triggers — by design. |

title: zip + partial queues
body: | **`zip` + partial queues (resolved — drop + document)** | When one inner source completes, buffered values that never formed a full tuple are **dropped**; downstream then completes. This matches RxJS behavior and the zip contract (all slots always filled). Callers who need all values should use `combineLatest` or `merge`. Document in JSDoc/docstrings. |

title: concat + ERROR fail-fast
body: | **`concat` + `ERROR` on the second source before the first completes (resolved — fail-fast short-circuit)** | `ERROR` from **any** source (even buffered/inactive) immediately terminates `concat`. Silent error swallowing is a bug magnet; fail-fast is the safer pre-1.0 default. Callers who need “ignore inactive source errors” can wrap source 2 in `retry` or `catchError`. |

title: race + pre-winner DIRTY
body: | **`race` + pre-winner `DIRTY` (resolved — keep current + document)** | Before the first winning `DATA`, `DIRTY` from multiple sources **may** forward downstream. This is transient and harmless — downstream handles it via normal settlement. A stricter “winner-only” implementation adds complexity for minimal gain. Document the behavior clearly in JSDoc/docstrings. |

title: Whole-repo emit → down rename audit + up/backpressure/message_tier sweep
body: TS: emitWithBatch→downWithBatch, _emitToSinks→_downToSinks, _emitAutoValue→_downAutoValue, _boundEmitToSinks→_boundDownToSinks, _emitSequential→_downSequential, emitLine→flushLine. Batch param emit→sink. up() audit: no asymmetries. messageTier() audit: already clean. NodeActions.emit() kept (different semantics). CQRS CommandActions.emit() kept. Spec updated. PY: matching renames (emit_with_batch→down_with_batch, etc.). EmitStrategy→DownStrategy. _manual_emit_used/_manual_emit kept. Pre-1.0, no backward compat concern.

title: PY ReactiveMapBundle parity — .get(key), .has(key), .size + Versioned removal
body: Level A (2026-04-07): Added .get(key), .has(key), .size to PY ReactiveMapBundle matching TS signatures. PY harness strategy.py updated. Level B (TS+PY, 2026-04-07): Removed Versioned wrapper from all reactive bundle APIs (ReactiveMap, ReactiveLog, ReactiveList, ReactiveIndex). .node/.entries/.items/.ordered now emit unwrapped domain types. Internal version counter drives efficient equality without leaking into composition code (spec §5.12). All downstream consumers updated.

title: batch_id in ObserveEvent timeline fields
body: Added batch_id?: number to ObserveEvent in TS (parity with PY). Increments once per subscribe-callback invocation; all messages in one delivery share the same batch_id. Useful for correlating events without polling. in_batch: boolean unchanged. TS: implemented in _createObserveResult, _createObserveResultForAll, and both fallback paths. PY: already had batch_id on batch_seq counter.

title: START protocol + ROM/RAM refactor (v5 architecture)
body: Full NodeImpl/DynamicNodeImpl refactor on shared NodeBase (TS + PY). START handshake as tier 0; tiers shifted (DIRTY/INVALIDATE→1, PAUSE/RESUME→2, DATA/RESOLVED→3, COMPLETE/ERROR→4, TEARDOWN→5). ROM/RAM cache rule, first-run gate, rewire-buffer, at-most-once _active deactivation guard. TS QA: forwardInner START filter, DynamicNodeImpl.up() guard, startWith simplification, _equals in rewire discrepancy check. PY parity: full port + QA pass (terminal replay revert, adapter test restructure, RAM cache clear, initial status fix). _connected field removed in PY. 1429 TS tests, 1156 PY tests pass.

title: Batch drain queue rename to phase vocabulary
body: pendingPhase2/3 renamed to drainPhase2/3/4. drainPhase4 added as the unified TEARDOWN deferral queue. downWithBatch walker relies on pre-sorted input invariant (from bundle.resolve()) and slices once at phase boundaries without re-sorting. Lives in src/core/batch.ts:9-37. Verified in-place 2026-04-12.

title: _addDep + _execFn finally ordering for autoTrackNode two-phase discovery
body: Restored _addDep post-construction dep addition with _isExecutingFn / _pendingRerun re-entrance guard. Finally block runs _pendingRerun BEFORE _clearWaveFlags so autoTrackNode's discovery-then-real-run pattern works. _setInspectorHook restored per-node for Graph.observe. Sibling fixes: compat-layer n.down vs n.emit bug class closed (jotai/nanostores/zustand now emit via framed pipeline). Flag A dataFrom-based emission suppression explicitly closed (architecturally wrong for two-way bridge). Implemented in src/core/node.ts.

title: Flag A — autoTrackNode dataFrom-based emission suppression
body: Closed as architecturally wrong. A compat-layer two-way bridge requires downstream wave correctness: suppressing emit on no-op waves left downstream dep.dirty records stuck forever, and a sibling-dep advance would hang. HEAD's simple autoTrackNode body (no suppression) is correct and cheap. Revisit only if a future optimization can preserve wave correctness — i.e., still emit RESOLVED on no-op waves.

title: GraphReFlyConfig freeze-on-first-use (§2 open item)
body: GraphReFlyConfig class exposes hook getters (bundle/onMessage/onSubscribe) that flip _frozen=true on first read. Setters and registerMessageType throw after freeze. NodeImpl constructor touches one getter to freeze on first node creation. Registry reads (messageTier/isLocalOnly/isKnownMessageType) are free lookups that do NOT freeze. See src/core/config.ts:159–258.

title: src/core/versioning.ts runtime-agnostic crypto
body: Dropped node:crypto import. randomUuid() uses globalThis.crypto.randomUUID() (Node 14.17+, browsers, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers) with a Math.random fallback for ancient runtimes. defaultHash() uses a vendored sync SHA-256 (sha256Hex, ~80 LOC) that matches crypto.createHash('sha256') byte-for-byte — verified cross-check against Node. crypto.subtle.digest was unsuitable because it's async and defaultHash is sync. Core is now importable from browser/edge runtimes. 28 versioning tests still green.

title: Meta TEARDOWN re-entrancy (§10.2 flag 5)
body: Hoisted meta TEARDOWN fan-out out of _updateState into the top of _emit. _updateState stays a pure state-transition walk; meta children receive [[TEARDOWN]] before the parent's cache/status commit. Preserves spec ordering (meta propagates before deactivation) while eliminating the mid-walk re-entrance window where a meta child's dispatch could observe the parent in half-committed state. Implemented at src/core/node.ts:_emit.

title: INVALIDATE measurement cache flush via fn cleanup
body: reactive-layout and reactive-block-layout patterns relied on the deleted v4 per-node onMessage hook to flush measurement caches on INVALIDATE. Rewrote both as raw node(...) with explicit actions.emit + function-form cleanup. Core's _updateState INVALIDATE branch already fires the cleanup (node.ts:1286–1294), so the pattern picks it up for free. Cleanup also fires before next run AND on deactivation — same shape as useEffect/RxJS. 101 reactive-layout tests pass. The two 'FLAG: v5 behavioral change' test comments have been cleared.

title: A3 — Diamond wide scaling via _dirtyDepCount + _sentinelDepCount
body: Replaces O(deps) every(!d.dirty) + every(latestData!==NO_VALUE) scans in _maybeRunFnOnSettlement with O(1) integer >0 checks. Two fields on NodeImpl. Five centralized dep-state transition helpers update both counters in lockstep with DepRecord.dirty/latestData/terminal flags: _depDirtied, _depSettledAsData, _depSettledAsResolved, _depSettledAsTerminal, _depInvalidated. Bulk init in _activate (to deps.length), bulk reset in _deactivate + resubscribable terminal reset. _addDep bumps both counters on new dep. Expected win: diamond wide (10 intermediates) from 184K ops/sec toward the ~680K flat-diamond ceiling. Implementation: src/core/node.ts. Archived from optimizations.md.

title: B3 — equals subtree skip bench variants + correctness regression tests
body: Added three bench variants in src/__bench__/graphrefly.bench.ts under describe('equals: 5-level linear chain, 50% no-op writes'), describe('equals: leaf fn-run count under 50% no-ops'), describe('equals: diamond with 50% no-op inputs'). Each drives inputs that actually exercise the pre-fn skip path (mid-chain values unchanged) so the leaf fn-run count collapses. Also added 2 correctness tests in src/__tests__/core/protocol.test.ts (B3 describe block): assert exact leaf fn-run counts for a 5-level chain and diamond under 50% no-op inputs. These guard against future optimizations that break cascade subtree pruning — the old baseline's 'equals: with (subtree skip)' showed ~1% win because the bench always incremented (equals always false); the new variants show the real benefit materializing on no-op cascades.

title: C0 — PAUSE/RESUME lock id tracking + bufferAll mode
body: Every [PAUSE, lockId] / [RESUME, lockId] tuple must carry a payload; bare throws from _emit's C0 filter loop. Per-node _pauseLocks: Set<unknown> tracks active locks; _paused = _pauseLocks.size > 0. Multi-pauser correctness: lock A release while B held stays paused — downstream nodes on the propagation path track their own locksets symmetrically. Unknown-lockId RESUME is swallowed for dispose() idempotency. pausable: 'resumeAll' (bufferAll) mode captures tier-3/4 outgoing messages into _pauseBuffer while paused; final lock release drains the buffer via recursive _emit before forwarding RESUME downstream. The recursive drain goes through the normal pipeline (tier sort + synthetic DIRTY + equals substitution) so buffered DATAs whose values match the pre-pause cache collapse to RESOLVED — documented as D2 semantic (producers needing pulse semantics should set equals: () => false). QA 2026-04-13 (P1/P2): clear _pauseLocks + _pauseBuffer in _deactivate and in the resubscribable terminal reset block — previously leaked on teardown and could leave a new subscriber stuck paused across a resubscribable reset. Regression test in protocol.test.ts. 5 unit tests total. Lock-id generation lives in src/extra/backpressure.ts (unchanged); bookkeeping is in src/core/node.ts _emit C0 filter loop.

title: _applyVersioning restored + GraphReFlyConfig.defaultVersioning + defaultHashFn
body: Restored NodeImpl._applyVersioning(level, opts?) as a monotonic retroactive upgrade: throws mid-fn (via _isExecutingFn guard), preserves id + version counter across level upgrades. Added GraphReFlyConfig.defaultVersioning + defaultHashFn; every node inherits at construction unless opts.versioning / opts.versioningHash overrides. Graph.setVersioning(level) now retroactively bumps every registered node via _applyVersioning. Graph.add applies graph-level default to newly-added nodes. QA 2026-04-13 refinements: (D3) level detection moved from 'cid' in current shape discriminator to an explicit _versioningLevel: VersioningLevel | undefined field tracked alongside _versioning, future-proof for v2/v3. (D4) _addDep dedups on depNode reference identity. (D1 doc) v0 → v1 upgrade is a fresh history root — new V1 has prev: null, intentional because v0 had no cid to link to; callers needing unbroken cid chains from birth must attach versioning at construction. 3 new unit tests in versioning.test.ts.

title: QA — _addDep dedup + _activate initial-length capture
body: Two related fixes landed together. (1) _addDep now scans _deps for reference-equal depNode and returns the existing index if found, making repeated adds idempotent. autoTrackNode keeps its depIndexMap as a fast-path lookup (returns data[idx] without calling _addDep at all); this is the safety-net backstop. (2) _activate captures initialLen = this._deps.length before its subscribe loop so that if _addDep fires synchronously during a dep's subscribe callback (e.g., autoTrackNode discovery in _execFn), _activate doesn't also subscribe the newly-added dep — double-subscribe bug. _addDep is now the sole subscribe path for runtime-added deps after _activate completes its initial slice. No test regression but closes a counter-drift path surfaced by the QA review.

title: QA — _hasMeta cache removes Object.keys alloc per _emit
body: _emit previously called Object.keys(this.meta) per emission to check whether to run the meta TEARDOWN fan-out block. The call returned an empty array for nodes without meta (the common case) but the allocation itself was wasted on the hot path. Now cached as readonly _hasMeta: boolean at NodeImpl construction (meta is frozen so this never changes) and the fan-out block short-circuits on !_hasMeta. Minor perf, no correctness impact.

title: Higher-order operators: fn+closure tier-1 upgrade
body: All four `*Map` operators upgraded to fn+closure B pattern. Key decisions: `{ deactivation }` cleanup (not `() => void` — prevents inner teardown before terminal waves); `exhaustMap` emits RESOLVED on drop; `mergeMap` switched from inline `spawn()` to `forwardInner` (fixes inner ERROR leak + START forwarding). Wave-batching claim was revised: fn+closure doesn't auto-coalesce multiple outer DATAs. See `SESSION-extras-wave1-audit.md`. **QA pass (2026-04-13) fixed:** (a) `mergeMap` `spawn()` TDZ crash when inner completes synchronously (`const stop` → `let stop`); (b) all `*Map` deactivation now resets `sourceDone = false` (was stale across reactivation cycles, caused premature COMPLETE on re-subscribe); (c) `_updateState` `newCache` deferred write was overwriting INVALIDATE/TEARDOWN cache clears — mirrored those clears to `newCache`.

title: `switchMap` batch loop intermediate subscribe churn
body: Short-circuited to `batch0[batch0.length - 1]`; `clearInner()` called once; N-1 intermediate `project()` calls eliminated.

title: `forwardInner` forwards PAUSE/RESUME/TEARDOWN from inner to outer sinks
body: `else` branch narrowed to only forward DIRTY/RESOLVED/INVALIDATE. PAUSE, RESUME, TEARDOWN from inner dropped — inner flow-control and lifecycle signals are internal to the `*Map` operator. RxJS/callbag precedent: no backpressure forwarding in merge-style operators. For mergeMap, one inner's PAUSE must not pause all output.

title: `withLatestFrom` — secondary terminates before primary DATA
resolution: Terminal-check guard added for dep 1 in the fn: when secondary has already terminated (COMPLETE or ERROR) before primary fires, the operator skips or forwards COMPLETE/ERROR instead of emitting `[primaryValue, undefined]`. Test added to `withLatestFrom` describe block covering secondary-terminates-before-primary scenario.

title: `withLatestFrom` — sentinel gate swallows secondary ERROR when primary unsettled
resolution: Pre-dirty removed from `_activate` (`_dirtyDepCount` now starts at 0; actual DIRTY messages drive it). Terminal bypass added in `_maybeRunFnOnSettlement`: when `_hasNewTerminal && _sentinelDepCount > 0`, `_maybeAutoTerminalAfterWave` is called before the sentinel gate returns. Side effect fixed: `onFailure` needed `errorWhenDepsError: false` (was relying on pre-dirty sentinel-gate collision to accidentally suppress auto-error).

title: `_updateState` versioning fires for every DATA in a multi-DATA batch
body: `_updateState` now pre-scans for `lastDataIdx` and calls `advanceVersion` once per batch wave (only on the final DATA). Version history now matches `_cached` history exactly — every version entry corresponds to a value actually observable from cache.

title: `cache` operator redundant with node-level push-on-subscribe
body: `cache(source, ttlNs)` maintained a closure-level cache alongside the node's built-in cache, causing dual-layer confusion and potential double-DATA on subscribe within TTL. No production call sites. TTL-based staleness belongs in eviction policies (`cascadingCache` + a future `ttlEviction`) not a standalone wrapper operator. Removed from `resilience.ts` and `index.ts`.

title: `rateLimiter` rewritten from sliding-window to `tokenBucket`
body: `rateLimiter` maintained a `times: number[]` array + O(k) prune per admission. Rewritten on `tokenBucket(maxEvents, refillPerSec)`. Added `maxBuffer` (>= 1) option with `onOverflow: "drop-oldest" | "drop-newest" | "error"` (default `"drop-newest"`). New `RateLimiterOverflowError`. Signature changed to options object `rateLimiter(source, opts)`. QA fix: drain delay uses `tokenTimeNs = NS_PER_SEC / refillPerSec` instead of `bucket.available()` deficit — avoids double-refill stealing fractional token credit and 1ms busy-spin. Explicit TEARDOWN handling added.

title: `tokenTracker` alias removed — consolidated on `tokenBucket`
body: Redundant alias added for PY naming parity. `tokenBucket` is the standard term. Alias removed. PY rename tracked as `[py-parity-token-bucket-rename]`.

title: `withMaxAttempts` returning `null` now stops retry
body: `coerceDelayNs(null)` returned 0 delay instead of signaling stop. `retry` treated null from a strategy as retry-immediately-with-0-delay instead of no-more-retries. Fixed: `scheduleRetryOrFinish` now checks for `null`/`undefined` from strategy and propagates the error immediately (same as `attempt >= maxRetries`). `coerceDelayNs` null branch removed (dead code).

title: P3 audit #1: `forwardInner` reads `inner.cache` after subscribe
body: Post-subscribe `.status`/`.cache` reads removed — dead code given P4 START handshake guarantees.

title: P3 audit #4: `withStatus` reads `out.meta.status.cache` in subscribe callback
body: Replaced with closure variable `currentStatus` updated synchronously alongside each `status.down()` command. Eliminates P3 violation — no cross-node `.cache` read inside reactive callback.

title: P3 audit #5: `fallback` reads `(fb as Node<T>).cache` in subscribe callback
body: Manual `.cache` read removed; push-on-subscribe delivers the fallback node's cached value in the first callback batch. Also fixed double-DATA bug. `fallback` now accepts `FallbackInput<T>` (Node, Promise, AsyncIterable, or scalar) via `fromAny`.

title: `DynamicNodeImpl` identity-skip false positive on dep reorder
body: TS `_trackedValues` is `Map<Node, unknown>` (identity-based). PY `dynamic_node.py` doesn't have `_tracked_values` — no action needed unless PY adds the rewire buffer.

title: Observe tier-surfacing: INVALIDATE/PAUSE/RESUME/TEARDOWN event variants
body: Extended `ObserveEvent` discriminated union with four new variants — `invalidate`, `pause` (carries `lockId`), `resume` (carries `lockId`), `teardown`. Added matching dispatch branches in unified `_createObserveResult` message loop and new result counters (`invalidateCount`/`pauseCount`/`resumeCount`/`teardownCount`) that bump in both structured and `detail: "minimal"` paths, mirroring `dirtyCount`/`resolvedCount` semantics. Themes (`OBSERVE_ANSI_THEME`, `OBSERVE_NO_COLOR_THEME`, `resolveObserveTheme`) extended with new color slots; `pretty` renderer now shows `lockId` for pause/resume events. Observers can now distinguish graph teardown from clean completion, track cache invalidation tier-1 signals, and surface backpressure events end-to-end. Five tests added under `observe() tier-surfacing variants` describe block.

title: Unified persistence surface: graph.attachStorage(tiers) + StorageTier
body: Collapsed three parallel persistence layers (Graph.autoCheckpoint + AutoCheckpointAdapter, extra/checkpoint.ts CheckpointAdapter + Memory/Dict/File/Sqlite/IndexedDb adapter classes + saveGraphCheckpoint/restoreGraphCheckpoint helpers, extra/cascading-cache.ts CacheTier<V> + tieredStorage wrapper) into a single StorageTier primitive in src/extra/storage.ts. New API: graph.attachStorage(tiers, {autoRestore?, paths?, filter?, onError?}) cascades snapshot records through N tiers with per-tier {debounceMs, compactEvery, filter} cadence — hot tier sync, warm debounced, cold diff-only with periodic full anchors. Each tier owns its own {lastSnapshot, lastVersionFingerprint} baseline; sync-debounce (debounceMs=0) tiers share one snapshot per observe event, debounced tiers recompute on timer fire via ResettableTimer (§5.11 escape hatch). Graph.fromStorage(name, tiers, opts?) hot-boots from the first tier that hits (always Promise<Graph>). save() returns void | Promise<void> — sync tiers are zero-microtask, async tiers fire-and-forget with onError routing. Factory fns: memoryStorage() / dictStorage(obj) / fileStorage(dir) / sqliteStorage(path) / indexedDbStorage(spec). cascadingCache(tiers) now consumes StorageTier directly (no CacheTier shim); sync tiers populate cache synchronously, async tiers emit DATA via Promise chaining. Deleted types/APIs: AutoCheckpointAdapter, GraphAutoCheckpointOptions, GraphAutoCheckpointHandle, CheckpointAdapter, MemoryCheckpointAdapter, DictCheckpointAdapter, FileCheckpointAdapter, SqliteCheckpointAdapter, saveGraphCheckpoint, restoreGraphCheckpoint, saveGraphCheckpointIndexedDb, restoreGraphCheckpointIndexedDb, checkpointNodeValue, IndexedDbCheckpointSpec, CacheTier, TieredStorage, TieredStorageOptions, tieredStorage, adapterToTier, DeltaCheckpoint (codec.ts). GraphCheckpointRecord simplified from 3-mode (full/diff/diff-only) to 2-mode (full/diff) with diff records carrying diff payload only (compactEvery anchors provide WAL replay baselines). replayWAL rewritten for 2-mode chain. Migrations: src/patterns/ai.ts (archiveAdapter → archiveTier, archiveCheckpointOptions → archiveStorageOptions), src/extra/adapters.ts (checkpointToS3 / checkpointToRedis now produce StorageTier and call graph.attachStorage), evals/scripts/run-harness.ts (raw tier.save/load for single-shot restore + final save). Four batch-12 sub-commits: 12a storage.ts foundation, 12b attachStorage + fromStorage + type simplification, 12c cascadingCache StorageTier consumer, 12d+e+f merged — checkpoint.ts removal, IDB port, callers migrated, docs/README/llms.txt updated.

title: Reactive-operator rewrite of autoCheckpoint
body: Superseded by the attachStorage unification (see entry above). The reactive-operator pipeline idea (observe → filter → debounce → effect) was blocked on the snapshot-at-emission vs snapshot-at-trigger question; attachStorage solves this pragmatically with ResettableTimer per tier and per-tier lastSnapshot baselines, without needing a new sample operator. Closure-based flush is acceptable inside the tier cadence loop because it's now per-tier rather than one-size-fits-all, and the reactive-operator layer doesn't need to expose this plumbing.

title: CBOR snapshot integration + v1 envelope + codec registry
body: Shipped the codec wire-protocol in one batch. GraphCodec gained a required version: number field (fits u16 in envelope); decode(buffer, codecVersion?) accepts the envelope-passed version for historical-layout dispatch. Envelope v1 layout [envelope_v=1:u8][name_len:u8][name:utf8][codec_v:u16 BE][payload:rest] exposed via module-level encodeEnvelope / decodeEnvelope helpers — self-describing, replaces out-of-band contentType signaling. Registry lives on GraphReFlyConfig (registerCodec / lookupCodec); freeze-on-read matches the message-type registry pattern. JsonCodec auto-registers on defaultConfig via registerBuiltinCodecs() called from core/node.ts. New Graph.snapshot overloads: no-arg → GraphPersistSnapshot (unchanged), {format: "json-string"} → deterministic JSON string, {format: "bytes", codec: name} → Uint8Array with v1 envelope via registry lookup. Static Graph.decode(bytes, {config?}) auto-dispatches via envelope + registry. Deleted negotiateCodec (zero callers, envelope self-describes on read side). Scope boundary: WALEntry / GraphCheckpointRecord stay as JS objects — envelope is a boundary format, storage tiers call snapshot({format: "bytes"}) at their I/O boundary; the WAL record shape is unchanged. 26 new tests in src/__tests__/graph/codec.test.ts cover JsonCodec round-trip, envelope layout bytes + truncation / unknown-codec / unknown-envelope-version errors, registry freeze-on-read, snapshot overloads, Graph.decode, replayWAL regression.

title: fromHTTP fetched: Node<boolean> companion
body: Added `fetched: Node<boolean>` to HTTPBundle<T>. Initial false, flips to true inside the batched emission alongside fetchCount/lastUpdated on every successful fetch, stays true across resubscribes (refetchOnSubscribe) — cumulative 'has at least one fetch succeeded ever' semantics. Orthogonal to withStatus's active/completed/errored lifecycle: users reaching for a 'fetch done' signal under the new cached-stay-live default now have one without opting into completeAfterFetch. Two new tests: fetched flips true on success, stays false on error; cumulative-true across resubscribes.

title: Observe causal context exposes dep_batches alongside dep_values
body: Graph.observe's causal / derived event now carries dep_batches — a ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<unknown> | undefined> mirroring the full per-dep wave batchData, each inner array cloned at snapshot time so observers never see mutation. dep_values continues to report the compressed last-scalar-per-dep view for older tooling. The inspector hook's run event already carried batchData; the plumbing now threads into ObserveCausalContext via a lastRunDepBatches map in _createObserveResult. Multi-value waves (triggered by batch() across multiple deps) are no longer invisible to observability tooling. Test: `dep_batches tracks per-dep wave batches` uses batch() to update two state deps in one wave and asserts dep_batches[i] is length-1 per dep, distinct from the single-value-wave test that also checks dep_batches[0]=[v].

title: orchestration.ts gate() isOpenNode.cache P3 fix
body: Applied stratify Option A: capture isOpenNode DATA into a latestIsOpen closure variable via isOpenNode.subscribe((msgs) => { for (m of msgs) if (m[0] === DATA) latestIsOpen = m[1] }). Seeded with startOpen at wiring time so first items arriving before open()/close() use the same value the state node was constructed with. The output producer consults latestIsOpen instead of isOpenNode.cache — gate decision now rides entirely on protocol delivery. Unsub registered via graph.addDisposer so it tears down with the owning graph. High-impact correctness site (harness flow control: QUEUE→GATE→EXECUTE, promptNode/harnessLoop approval flows). Existing orchestration test suite passes without changes — protocol-delivered isOpenNode values match the synchronous .cache reads under the current runner.

title: ctx.latestData → ctx.prevData rename (already shipped)
body: Optimizations.md item was stale — the semantic fix had already landed as part of the foundation redesign. Verified: DepRecord.prevData exists (not latestData), _execFn snapshots prevData BEFORE committing this wave's last DATA (node.ts:1467-1480), and advances dep.prevData only after the snapshot is taken, so ctx.prevData[i] correctly holds the end-of-previous-wave value for every dep (involved or not). The item's secondary claim — that store.hasPrev/store.prev in distinctUntilChanged/pairwise could be eliminated — is not actionable without mixing within-wave and across-wave state (ctx.prevData[0] is the prev-WAVE last value, not the prev-WITHIN-wave emission), so those operators correctly retain store-based tracking. Batch D swept the last 5 stale 'latestData' comments in core/node.ts, core/sugar.ts, and __tests__/extra/operators.test.ts to match the actual field name.

title: Framework primitive: wave-final state for multi-dep derived (resolved by synthetic-DIRTY invariant)
body: Item proposed a wave-end hook / coerced DIRTY prefix / batchDerived primitive because raw state.down([[DATA, v]]) was claimed to bypass settlement gating, leaving multi-dep derived fns with only progressive data[] snapshots. Revalidation 2026-04-17: the dispatch-layer equals-substitution invariant (landed 2026-04-12) already adds a synthetic DIRTY prefix for every tier-3 payload via _frameBatch (src/core/node.ts:1570–1608), applied to EVERY emission path including external raw state.down. Downstream's _markDepDirty → settlement gating therefore fires correctly, and data[] is wave-final by construction. The worker-bridge aggregator fix at src/extra/worker/bridge.ts:128–149 works BECAUSE of this invariant — it reads wave-form data[i] from the `node([deps], fn)` primitive directly. BH-2 house-style observation (14+ sites writing [[DATA, v]] without explicit [DIRTY] prefix) is also resolved by the same invariant: the framework auto-prepends DIRTY, so the literal absence is cosmetic. No new primitive needed.

title: Worker bridge handshake timer → reactive deadline (shipped)
body: Replaced raw setTimeout handshake deadline + statusNode.cache read (P3 #10) with reactive race: fromTimer(timeoutMs) + filter(statusNode, s => s === 'connected') merged through map() and collapsed via first(). Whichever side fires first wins and auto-unsubs the other. Closes both the raw-setTimeout central-time violation and the .cache-in-callback P3 violation in one change. Code lives at src/extra/worker/bridge.ts:296–309.

title: Worker bridge aggregator .cache reads (P3 #8/#9) fixed via wave-form data[i]
body: The worker bridge exposed-node aggregator at src/extra/worker/bridge.ts:128–149 now uses raw node([deps], (data, a) => ...) so it can inspect wave-form data[i]: per-dep batch of DATA values this wave, or undefined for silent deps. Deps whose equals absorb a repeat emission send RESOLVED, so silent slots correctly mean 'no change' and are omitted from the wire message. No .cache reads inside fn, no equals: () => false, no closure lastSent Map. This is Option B under v5 semantics. The earlier P3 audit entry claimed this needed framework-level work on wave-final state — that premise was stale; the equals-substitution + synthetic-DIRTY invariant already provided wave-final data[]. The effectNode downstream (bridge.ts:151) emits the coalesced batch message.

title: Per-node resource tracking / subscriber audit shipped as resourceProfile + orphans
body: graph.resourceProfile() ships at src/graph/graph.ts:1753, wrapping graphProfile at src/graph/profile.ts. Per-node NodeProfile fields include subscriberCount (from NodeImpl._sinkCount), cache state (valueSizeBytes), activation (isActive), orphanKind ('orphan-effect' | 'idle-derived' | 'idle-producer'). GraphProfileResult exposes orphans, orphanEffects, hotspots bySubscriberCount/byValueSize. Covers the originally-proposed subscriber audit and orphan-effect detection. Unbounded log growth detection is separate and left out of scope.

title: P3 #6 adapters.ts fetchCount.cache read eliminated
body: fromHTTP's counter increment at src/extra/adapters.ts rewritten to use a closure-owned fetchCountLocal variable instead of reading fetchCount.cache inside the async .then callback. fetchCount node is now write-only; every write flows through the single increment site, so the node stays in sync. Closes the P3 audit #6 violation without introducing a self-owned-counter exception comment.

title: P3 #14 feedback counter .cache fixed via shared tryIncrementBounded helper
body: Extracted tryIncrementBounded(counter, cap) from harness/loop.ts to patterns/_internal.ts as a documented self-owned-counter helper. feedback() at reduction.ts:424 now uses the shared helper inside a batch(() => { if (tryIncrementBounded(counter, maxIter)) reentryNode.down([[DATA, condValue]]); }) block so the counter increment and reentry routing settle as one wave. harness/loop.ts fastRetry also imports from the shared module (was previously a local duplicate). Removes the last two hand-rolled .cache-based counter sites in patterns/ and consolidates the exception comment in one place.

title: gate.open() flush ordering wrapped in batch()
body: gate.open() now wraps isOpenNode.down + queued output.down loop in a single batch(() => ...). All queued DATAs are tier-3-deferred until after the isOpenNode dep wave settles, so item order relative to the open signal is deterministic regardless of runner async-ness. Option A from the doc; Option B (fully reactive drain subscribed to isOpenNode transitions) deferred — Option A is sufficient under the current sync runner and adds zero semantic surface.

title: Distill eviction redesign / P3 #2 #3 #7 via factory-time seed pattern
body: All three composite.ts P3 violations closed via the established stratify/budgetGate pattern (factory-time seed + subscribe-handler update, closure read inside reactive fn — §3.6 wiring-time boundary, not a P3 violation). (#2) verifiable: autoVerify-only case uses switchMap(sourceNode, src => verifyFn(src)) directly; explicit-trigger case uses latestSource closure seeded from sourceNode.cache + sourceNode.subscribe handler. Removed sourceNode.cache read inside switchMap fn. (#7) distill extraction + consolidation: latestStore closure seeded from store.entries.cache + store.entries.subscribe handler; extractFn and consolidate read latestStore, no store.entries.cache read inside switchMap fns. asReadonlyMap() helper deleted (dead code). (#3) eviction verdict: removed verdict.cache === true read entirely — relies on forEach(verdict, cb)'s push-on-subscribe semantics to deliver the initial verdict value through the callback on first subscribe; future transitions flow through the same path. Rejected approach: withLatestFrom(source, store.entries) — primary's push-on-subscribe fires before secondary subscribes, swallowing the initial wave (7 composite tests failed on that approach, confirming why stratify/budgetGate chose the factory-time-seed pattern). Store-mutation-events full redesign still relevant post-1.0 for compressed delta protocol + op-log changesets, but pre-1.0 P3 cleanup is now complete. All 1636 tests pass.

title: Stream extractor cursor + default structural equals (2I)
body: All four stream extractors shipped with the two library-side optimizations: (1) cursor in ctx.store so each chunk processes only the delta, (2) default structural equals so identical consecutive outputs collapse to RESOLVED. keywordFlagExtractor: scannedTo cursor with overlap window (opts.maxPatternLength, default 128) so matches spanning chunk boundaries aren't missed; flagsEqual compares {label, pattern, match, position} per element. toolCallExtractor: scanFrom cursor resumes brace-scanning after the last complete parse (or at the last incomplete open brace); callsEqual compares {startIndex, name, raw} per element (arguments omitted — shape is user-defined, raw captures the same info). costMeterExtractor: O(1) already, no cursor; costMeterEqual compares the three numeric fields. streamExtractor (generic): opts.equals escape hatch for user-supplied structural equals, since output shape is unknown. All extractors return a FRESH array copy per run so downstream never holds a live reference to ctx.store.calls/.flags (structural equals suppresses emission when unchanged, but mutation-vs-immutability invariant is preserved either way). All 1636 tests pass.

title: Multi-dep push-on-subscribe ordering — withLatestFrom initial-pair gap
body: Framework semantic gap surfaced while implementing distill P3 cleanup. _activate (src/core/node.ts:1002–1025) subscribes deps sequentially in declaration order; each dep's push-on-subscribe fires as its own wave. For withLatestFrom(primary=state, secondary=state) on initial activation: primary's wave hits while secondary SENTINEL → first-run gate RESOLVED; secondary's wave hits with primary silent this wave → else branch RESOLVED. Initial paired emission dropped. NOT a protocol invariant violation (push-on-subscribe, first-run gate §2.7, DIRTY/DATA ordering all correct per individual contracts) — semantic gap in operators whose fn only considers this-wave batches. **Mitigation shipped:** factory-time seed pattern (§3.6 wiring-time boundary read + subscribe-handler closure update) is the established workaround; stratify, budgetGate, gate, distill, verifiable all use it. Documented in GRAPHREFLY-SPEC §2.7 'Multi-dep push-on-subscribe serialization' and COMPOSITION-GUIDE §28. **Framework-level fix explored but deferred:** [secondary, primary] dep flip in withLatestFrom produces correct initial pair but breaks harness/loop.ts:297 executeContextNode diamond topology (executeInput → executeNode → executeContextNode AND executeInput → executeContextNode). Reingestion test times out with the flip. Proper fix needs diamond-topology audit of all in-tree withLatestFrom callers OR activation-phase batching (subscribe all deps first, then fan-out push-on-subscribe as one combined wave — protocol-level change). Status: open framework-level item; closed pre-1.0 via factory-time seed pattern.

title: Diamond fan-in over-fires inside multi-emit batch
body: Diamond A→B; A→C; D=derived([B,C]). For a batch of K source emits on A, D used to fire 2K−1 DIRTY+settlement pairs — a mid-point artifact of bitmask clearing + recompute loop during batch flush. Counts were balanced (DIRTY=settlement) so invariants #1/#2 held; what broke was diamond coalescing across multiple deferred DATAs from the same source. Fixed by two independent changes on 2026-04-17: (Bug 1) per-message fn-invocation — _maybeRunFnOnSettlement() moved from tail of _onDepMessage to end of the dep subscribe callback message-iteration loop (guarded by tierOf >= 3); multi-message batches fire dep fn exactly once with full batchData. (Bug 2) per-node emit coalescing inside explicit batch (option α) — _emit accumulates into _batchPendingMessages; at batch end registerBatchFlushHook fires _flushBatchPending which re-runs _frameBatch and delivers ONE downWithBatch call. K emits to same node in batch → K DIRTYs in one tier-1 sink call + K DATAs in one tier-3 sink call. Downstream fn runs once per wave. Diamond invariant 4 maxBatchEmits:1 workaround removed. Touch points: src/core/batch.ts (registerBatchFlushHook, isExplicitlyBatching, flushHooks loop), src/core/node.ts (_batchPendingMessages, _dispatchOrAccumulate, _flushBatchPending). See src/__tests__/core/multi-message-delivery.test.ts and archive/docs/SESSION-rigor-infrastructure-plan.md.

title: policyEnforcer enforce-mode misses dynamically-added nodes
body: Two-part fix shipped together: (1) Graph.topology: Node<TopologyEvent> companion on every graph (src/graph/graph.ts) — payload {kind: 'added'|'removed', name, nodeKind: 'node'|'mount', audit?}; lazy, zero cost until first subscribe, no retention. (2) watchTopologyTree(graph, cb) helper (src/graph/topology-tree.ts) — transitive subscription that auto-wires new mounts and disposes on unmount via qualified-prefix matching (does NOT rely on _parent == null — that only nulls on direct child). Callback receives (event, emitter, prefix) so consumers compute qualified paths without walking ancestors. policyEnforcer in enforce mode with paths omitted now uses watchTopologyTree, so nodes added to target OR any transitively-mounted subgraph (including those mounted before enforcer was built, and newly-mounted subgraphs) are guarded automatically at arbitrary depth. On mount add, walks only the new subgraph's local paths (scoped describe — O(M) per mount event, not O(N_total)). Re-adds under same qualified path re-wrap cleanly. Tests: 6 audit-dynamic-coverage cases + 15 topology-companion tests + 13 lens tests.

title: Reactive adaptive rate limiter → src/extra/adaptive-rate-limiter.ts
body: Shipped as src/extra/adaptive-rate-limiter.ts with the two-composable-units design: (1) adaptiveRateLimiter(opts) — reactive primitive wrapping two tokenBuckets (requests, tokens) with reactive rpm/tpm knobs (NodeInput<number>), clampCooldownMs decay (default 60s) on signal-induced caps so transient 429s don't throttle permanently, TokenBucket.putBack recovery on TPM-miss (no wasted RPM slot), ResettableTimer-based sleepReactive (spec §5.10 escape hatch, cheaper than per-call fromTimer allocation), acquire-time local bucket captures prevent cross-bucket credit leaks on mid-flight cap changes, AbortError-named error consistency. (2) http429Parser (src/patterns/ai/adapters/middleware/http429-parser.ts) — 429/503 status detection, Retry-After parsing (numeric seconds + HTTP-date fallback), x-ratelimit-* header normalization, Anthropic anthropic-ratelimit-*-reset ISO-8601 parsing, error-message regex fallback; produces RateLimitSignal. §9.3d middleware bridge withRateLimiter(adapter) composes the reactive limiter for Promise-based LLMAdapter call paths. §9.3c resilientPipeline integration and evals/lib/rate-limiter.ts migration remain open as separate items (evals stack is intentionally not migrated — fresh eval work after §9.1 will use the new adapter layer).

title: §9.3d LLM Adapter Layer — full surface shipped
body: Complete adapter layer at src/patterns/ai/adapters/ organized into core/ (types, pricing, capabilities, observable, factory), providers/ (anthropic, openai-compat, google, dry-run + browser/{webllm, chrome-nano}), middleware/ (budget-gate, rate-limiter, replay-cache, retry, timeout, breaker, dry-run, http429-parser), routing/ (cascading, presets). Key design decisions: (1) LLMAdapter.invoke returns NodeInput<LLMResponse> (spec §5.10 compliant, caller bridges via fromAny), stream returns AsyncIterable<StreamDelta> where StreamDelta is an object tagged union ({type: 'token'|'tool-call-delta'|'thinking'|'usage'|'finish', ...}) distinct from framework's [TYPE, data] protocol tuples. (2) Library ships ZERO pricing/capability data — PricingRegistry and CapabilitiesRegistry are user-populated factories; registryPricing() builds a PricingFn from a registry. (3) TokenUsage disaggregated across all provider shapes: input.{regular,cacheRead,cacheWrite5m,cacheWrite1h,cacheWriteOther,audio,image,video,toolUse,extensions} + output.{regular,reasoning,audio,predictionAccepted,predictionRejected,extensions} + auxiliary + raw escape hatch. (4) Tier multipliers apply to token lines only, NOT auxiliary (e.g., Anthropic batch doesn't discount web-search). (5) Provider adapters default to native fetch + SSE parsing; optional user-provided SDK instances accepted. (6) Middleware composes as (adapter, opts) => adapter, with sanctioned per-call shape preservation (Promise in → Promise out; Node in → derived-tap out) and spec §28 factory-time seed + keepalive for reactive gates (withBudgetGate, withDryRun). (7) cascadingLlmAdapter with skipped[]/failed Map distinction on AllTiersExhaustedError; no synthetic finish delta on stream-commit errors. (8) All sleep/deadline sites use ResettableTimer (spec §5.10 escape). (9) withDryRun returns {adapter, dispose} for reactive-enabled-flag keepalive cleanup. Tests: 89 new adapter tests + 9 regression tests covering two QA rounds. evals/lib/* intentionally untouched — new eval work after §9.1 will use the library directly.

title: singleFromAny + singleNodeFromAny — NodeInput singleflight
body: New general-purpose primitive at src/extra/single-from-any.ts. singleFromAny(factory, opts?) returns (key: K) => Promise<T> that dedupes concurrent invocations per key; bridges Promise/Node/AsyncIterable/Iterable/plain inputs via NodeInput dispatch (firstValueFrom for Nodes, first-value-then-close for iterables so owned resources release). Cache-entry installation uses a two-phase hoisted tracked binding so sync-resolved promises don't leave stale entries. Custom keyFn for object keys. singleNodeFromAny returns (key: K) => Node<T> with terminal-only eviction (ERROR/COMPLETE — NOT DATA, so multi-emitting Nodes stay shared). Used by withReplayCache for concurrent cache-miss thundering-herd dedup. 8 tests in src/__tests__/extra/single-from-any.test.ts.

title: TokenBucket.putBack method
body: Additive extension to the TokenBucket interface at src/extra/resilience.ts: putBack(cost = 1): void returns consumed tokens to the bucket, capped at capacity. No-op on cost <= 0. Used by adaptiveRateLimiter.acquire to credit RPM tokens back when the TPM admit fails (no wasted slot), and by recordUsage(-delta) for overshoot crediting. Pre-1.0 additive; no migration. 4 tests in src/__tests__/extra/token-bucket-putback.test.ts.

title: resilientAdapter: onFallback / onExhausted surface
body: **RESOLVED 2026-04-22** — shipped as `ResilientAdapterOptions.onFallback?` and `onExhausted?` threaded through to the inner `cascadingLlmAdapter`. Regression tests in `src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/resilient-adapter.test.ts` cover both happy-path fallback engagement and all-tier exhaustion reporting.

title: Parallel guardrail pattern — optimistic execution + cancel (documentation)
body: **RESOLVED 2026-04-22 (P1).** Doc entry shipped: COMPOSITION-GUIDE §30 covers the `switchMap + AbortSignal` + `contentGate` composition with three execution modes (blocking / parallel optimistic / post-hoc) and the when-to-use-which table. No library code change required — `gatedStream` already implements the mechanism; the item was entirely a naming/framing gap.

title: Stream extractor perf pass — cursor + default structural equals
body: **Landed 2026-04-17.** All four extractors got both optimizations: `ctx.store` cursor (`scannedTo` for keyword, `scanFrom` for toolCall; cost meter is O(1) already) and default structural equals (`keywordFlagsEqual`, `toolCallsEqual`, `costMeterEqual`). `streamExtractor` (generic) takes `opts.equals` since output shape is user-defined.

title: gate.open() flush ordering — external output.down() interleaves with in-flight isOpenNode dep wave
body: **Landed 2026-04-17 (Option A)** — flush loop wrapped in `batch(() => ...)` so all queued `output.down` emissions are deferred until after the `isOpenNode` dep wave settles. Option (b) (reactive drain) deferred to a future session; item order under the current sync runner is now deterministic regardless.

title: COMPOSITION-GUIDE §19 `reduce` code snippet is stale (TS portion)
body: **Verified 2026-04-22 — TS portion already fixed in `~/src/graphrefly/COMPOSITION-GUIDE.md`** (the snippet now reads `// ERROR is auto-propagated by the framework before fn runs — no guard needed here` with a clean `ctx.terminalDeps[0] === true` COMPLETE branch). PY portion of the guide may still need a mirror update — tracked only if PY-parity work resumes.

title: Missing meta=_ai_meta(...) on stream extractor derived() calls (TS portion)
body: **Verified 2026-04-22 — TS portion already shipped.** All four extractors in `src/patterns/ai.ts` carry `meta: aiMeta(...)` at lines 407 (stream_extractor), 514 (keyword_flag_extractor), 646 (tool_call_extractor), 707 (cost_meter_extractor). PY portion pending per standing policy.

title: gatedStream activation-order bug — gate.fn never enqueues pending values
body: **Resolved 2026-04-24 in same-day session.** The 3 skipped tests (plus a 4th `delta topic publishes stamped deltas` case) at src/__tests__/patterns/ai.test.ts:875–977 are now unskipped and passing. Two-part fix in src/patterns/ai/prompts/streaming.ts: (1) keepalive THREE surfaces — `output`, `gateCtrl.node`, AND `accumulatedText` — so a caller activating any one of `.output` / `.deltaTopic` / `.accumulatedText` gets the full pipeline draining. The gate's fn body only runs (and only enqueues into the pending queue) when the gate's output node has a live subscriber; without `keepalive(gateCtrl.node)`, callers that subscribe to `gate.count` / `.deltaTopic` instead of `.output` would see `count` stuck at 0. (2) Replaced `nonNullOutput` from a `derived` returning `undefined` to filter null (which actually emitted `DATA(undefined)` because `derived`'s wrapper always calls `actions.emit(fn(...))`) with `filter(output, v => v != null)` — RESOLVED for null, DATA for non-null. Latent second bug uncovered while validating the first fix; both required for the test suite to pass. QA also tightened gate type from `gate<T | null>` to `gate<T>` and registered all 3 keepalive unsubs with `graph.addDisposer` so parent.destroy() reclaims them even if caller forgets dispose().

title: defer() / fromAnyThunk extras candidate
body: Shipped 2026-04-25 as `defer<T>(thunk: () => NodeInput<T>, opts?: AsyncSourceOpts): Node<T>` in src/extra/sources.ts. Resubscribable by default (matches RxJS defer semantic — diverges from fromPromise/fromIter on purpose, called out in JSDoc). Forwards all upstream messages transparently except START (producer's own START handles handshake). Wraps `throw undefined` to satisfy spec §1.3 non-undefined ERROR payload. Strips `signal` from NodeOptions before forwarding. 6 tests in sources.test.ts cover lazy thunk invocation, per-activation re-run, error surfacing (named + undefined throws), Promise input bridging, and Node input forwarding. QA pass landed A1 (forward all msg types) + A2 (break on terminal) + A3/N3 (JSDoc tightening) + A4 (signal strip) + A5 (undefined throw wrap).

title: Tool handler signal-aware migration
body: Cookbook entry at docs/test-guidance.md § 'Tool handlers should thread signal' covers the handler shape, test pattern, and required-vs-optional split. Symmetry pointer added from the Mock LLM section so authors writing async stream mocks see the parallel signal-aware handler recommendation. Migration of existing in-tree handlers stays opt-in (non-breaking) — drive it per-call-site as real I/O handlers land.

title: Nested-drain wave-ordering — sink-drain-inside-sink-callback sees stale peer state
body: Protocol layer is correct as-is; framework fix not needed. Investigation (with agentLoop._terminalResult reverted to [statusNode, llmResponse] to expose the bug): the mid-wave bug as documented in COMPOSITION-GUIDE §32 step 4 does NOT reproduce on the current substrate. The _dirtyDepCount gate in _maybeRunFnOnSettlement correctly blocks the dependent's fn from running while ANY peer dep is still DIRTY for the in-flight wave. Phase 1 (_emit synthesizes [DIRTY, DATA, ...] framing) marks every sink's dep DIRTY before Phase 2 delivers DATA — including across switchMap output emissions via forwardInner -> a.emit(...). When effResponse's nested batch(() => status.emit('done')) fires mid-iteration, terminal's status dep settles but its llmResponse dep is still DIRTY — fn does not run. Phase 2's for-loop then visits terminal's llmResponse dep, settles it, fn runs once with both peers consistent. Multi-turn agentLoop test passes with mirror reverted; compound-topology fast-check invariant #12b nested-drain-peer-consistency-compound shipped (locks in _dirtyDepCount gating under switchMap upstream). What the lastResponseState mirror in agentLoop actually serves: cross-run reset semantics (NOT mid-wave consistency). llmResponse is a switchMap output whose cache persists across run() calls; mirror is reset to null in run()'s reset batch so abort path correctly emits ERROR(AbortError). QA C3 regression tests fail with mirror reverted — that's the actual bug class the mirror exists for. COMPOSITION-GUIDE §32 reframed cross-repo to mark framework-level concern as resolved, drop options (ii)/(iii), and reframe state-mirror as 'explicit checkpoint state for cross-wave reset semantics'. No code change to substrate.

title: Tier 6.5 Q5 per-stage `maxPerPump` on JobFlow
body: Q5 lock specified per-stage `executeMaxPerPump` / `verifyMaxPerPump` opt-in caps but the C2 batch shipped them collapsed via `Math.min(...)` because `JobFlowOptions.maxPerPump` was a single value shared across all stages. Deviation D1 (qa pass, 2026-04-28) closed the gap: `JobFlow.StageDef` gained an optional `maxPerPump?: number` per-stage override; the pump-builder loop reads each stage's override before falling back to the top-level default (which in turn falls back to `DEFAULT_MAX_PER_PUMP`). Harness (`patterns/harness/loop.ts`) now passes `executeMaxPerPump` and `verifyMaxPerPump` as independent per-stage caps via `StageDef.maxPerPump` — no more `Math.min` collapse. JSDoc caveat on `HarnessLoopOptions.verifyMaxPerPump` updated to reflect per-stage independence. Two new tests in `messaging.test.ts`: per-stage cap honored; negative cap rejected at construction. The 'UX trap' optimizations.md entry (Math.min collapse) is removed as obsolete.

title: Tier 6.5 Q2 extension — bridge-layer flake error classification
body: Q2 lock specified the dispatch effect routes 3-way verdicts (verified / retry / structural+reingest) but did NOT specify how bridge-layer flakes (parse / adapter throw / ERROR / COMPLETE-without-DATA) map to the verdict. The C2 batch initially classified them as `errorClass: "structural"` (defensive default). QA F3 (2026-04-28) flipped the default to `errorClass: "self-correctable"` so transient verifier flakes burn the retry budget first instead of immediately reingesting. Only the defensive `if (execution == null)` guard (topology violation, not flake) stays `structural`. On the executor side, bridge-layer failures pass through the `defaultErrorClassifier` regex which matches `parse|json|config|validation|syntax` keywords in the failure detail string — so parse errors route via retry, while adapter HTTP failures without keyword matches default to structural (asymmetric but matches pre-Tier-6.5 behavior). 16 regression tests in `harness-default-bridges.test.ts` lock the contract.

title: Tier 6.5 Q6 scope — `promptNode` retained for TRIAGE
body: Q6 lock said `promptNode` is no longer used internally by the harness. Deviation D2 (qa pass, 2026-04-28) clarified the intended scope: the lock applies to EXECUTE/VERIFY default work fns (where per-claim invocation makes the cross-wave switchMap supersede semantics wasteful — a fresh subgraph per claim is heavy). TRIAGE legitimately needs `promptNode` because it's a long-lived `Node<TriagedItem | null>` watching all intake items via switchMap supersede; one TriageNode for the entire harness lifetime is the right shape. `defaultLlmExecutor` / `defaultLlmVerifier` migrated to direct `adapter.invoke()` via the shared `_oneShotLlmCall` helper (D2 extraction); TRIAGE remains on `promptNode`. The clarification: Q6 means 'no `promptNode` in EXECUTE/VERIFY defaults', not 'no `promptNode` anywhere in harness internals'. Documented in implementation-plan.md Tier 6.5 entry.

title: QA follow-ups from A/B/C/D /qa pass (LANDED 2026-04-30)
body:
- **QA follow-ups from A/B/C/D /qa pass (LANDED 2026-04-30).** Adversarial review by Blind Hunter + Edge Case Hunter on the 8-piece A/B/C1/C2/C2-followup/D1/D2/D3 batch surfaced 49 raw findings, deduplicated to 25 unique. All 10 architecture-affecting (Group 1) + 15 auto-applicable (Group 2) fixes landed. Final tip: 2638/2638 tests pass; build green incl. `assertBrowserSafeBundles`; lint at 9 errors / 38 warnings (all pre-existing in `scripts/*.mjs`). Patches:
  - **F-1 + F-21:** changeset stream subscribes to nodes added AFTER activation; symmetric inspector hook attach/detach via path-keyed maps. 2 regression tests.
  - **F-2:** `GraphChangeData` JSDoc documents the prod-build edge-attribution degradation when `inspectorEnabled === false` (default).
  - **F-3:** `GraphChangeNodeAdded.nodeKind` populated with the resolved node's `_describeKind`. Regression test.
  - **F-4:** separate `GraphChange["type"]`-based tier whitelist; topology + batch frame events now respected by `tiers:` filter. Regression test.
  - **F-5:** `TopologyViewGraph._computeLayout` short-circuits to empty layout when `target.destroyed`. Regression test.
  - **F-6:** `MemoryRetrievalGraph` `result` derived declares `vectors.entries` / `kg.adjacencyOut` / `kg.adjacencyIn` as deps when configured.
  - **F-7 + F-11 + F-12:** `promptNode` calls `abortDispose?.()` on COMPLETE / ERROR / parse-error terminal branches; `cancelled` re-checked inside batch loop; `extractContent` cached on parse-failure path.
  - **F-9:** dropped shared `MemoryRetrievalGraph.retrieval` / `retrievalTrace` state-node mirrors (last-writer-wins under concurrent calls). `AgentMemoryGraph` updated; tests updated.
  - **F-10:** `layoutFrameToSvg` routes color options through `escapeXml` (XSS hardening for user-supplied themes).
  - **F-13 + F-14:** envelope `timestamp_ns` + `version` JSDoc tightened (per-handle; not comparable to wall-clock).
  - **F-15:** `GraphDerivedOptions` `equals: undefined` semantics pinned in JSDoc (falls back to `Object.is`).
  - **F-16:** `GraphChange` discriminated union split — `GraphChange` (12 emitted variants) + `GraphChangeReserved` (6 forward-compat variants). Consumers `switch` over emitted-only without dead branches.
  - **F-17:** canonical `LayoutFrame` / `LayoutBox` / `LayoutEdge` / `LayoutFn` types moved to `src/extra/render/layout-types.ts`. `patterns/topology-view/types.ts` re-exports them. Layering inversion eliminated.
  - **F-18:** `AttachStorageGraphLike` verified internal-only — no barrel re-export needed.
  - **F-19 + F-20:** upstream Node ref captured directly from inspector hook event (survives `dynamicNode` deps mutation); `"<anonymous>"` sentinel for unresolvable upstreams.
  - **F-22:** `defaultLayout` `depIndex` resolution uses per-(to, from) running cursor for duplicate-dep diamonds.
  - **F-23 + F-24:** regression tests for `partial: true` data-only-wave seam and edge attribution via inspector.

  The following 10 deferrals are pre-existing or out-of-scope for this batch:
  - **D-1 — `fromPulsar` cleanup leaves `await consumer.receive()` pending forever.** [`src/extra/io/pulsar.ts:135-196`](../src/extra/io/pulsar.ts). Consumer holds resources indefinitely after teardown if broker is idle. Pre-existing in pre-split `io/index.ts`. **Fix path:** thread an `AbortSignal` through `consumer.receive()` (where SDK supports), or call `consumer.close()` in cleanup. Defer until a real long-running pulsar consumer hits it.
  - **D-2 — `fromRabbitMQ` race: cleanup runs before `start()` resolves → consumer leaks.** [`src/extra/io/rabbitmq.ts:142-227`](../src/extra/io/rabbitmq.ts). `consumerTag` is set only after `await channel.consume(...)` resolves; teardown-during-init skips `channel.cancel`. Broker-side subscription leaks per teardown-during-init. Pre-existing. **Fix path:** add a `cancelRequested` flag; after the await, if `cancelRequested`, call `channel.cancel(result.consumerTag)` immediately. Defer.
  - **D-3 — `fromHTTP` / `fromHTTPStream` accumulate abort listeners on long-lived `externalSignal` across resubscriptions.** [`src/extra/io/http.ts:128-130, 352-354`](../src/extra/io/http.ts). Listeners use `{once:true}` but never `removeEventListener` in cleanup. Each fresh activation under `resubscribable: true` appends another listener. If the signal never fires, listeners stay registered indefinitely. Pre-existing. Defer.
  - **D-4 — `kafka.ts` `toKafka` casts `Buffer.from(serialized as string)` even when `serialize` legitimately returns `Buffer`.** [`src/extra/io/kafka.ts:190`](../src/extra/io/kafka.ts). Behavior happens to be correct (`Buffer.from(buffer)` copies), but the type assertion is dishonest. Pre-existing. Replace with `typeof serialized === "string" ? Buffer.from(serialized) : serialized` when next touched.
  - **D-5 — `MemoryWithTiersGraph.tierOf` reads `.cache` from inside a public method.** [`src/patterns/ai/memory/memory-composers.ts:392-399`](../src/patterns/ai/memory/memory-composers.ts). Ad-hoc external callers fine; reactive callers (e.g. another graph's effect) violate §5.8/§28. Pre-existing convention concern. **Fix path:** rename to `tierOfSync` with explicit "external-boundary only" JSDoc, OR drop and provide a reactive `tierOfNode(key)` parallel. Defer until a reactive consumer surfaces.
  - **D-6 — `retention.score` writes `entryCreatedAtNs.set` from inside substrate scoring callback.** Already tracked at the existing residual-backlog D1 below; not re-flagged. The C1 batch did not touch this site even though it's adjacent — defer with the existing entry.
  - **D-7 — `_retrieveSeq` batch-safe regression test missing.** `batch(() => { graph.retrieveReactive(a); graph.retrieveReactive(b); })` — both calls land inside one outer batch and get distinct seq numbers (good), but no test pins the semantics. Defer; additive test, not a correctness bug.
  - **D-8 — `topology-tree.watchTopologyTree` initial-push race with parent's `for (const [mountName, child] of g._mounts)` loop.** [`src/graph/topology-tree.ts:53-95`](../src/graph/topology-tree.ts). Placeholder-entry guard prevents double-wire, but if the underlying Graph's mount tracking fires a "current snapshot" on subscribe, you can double-emit `cb(...)` for already-mounted children. Bounded; unlikely to fire. Defer with a guarded test.
  - **D-9 — Push-on-subscribe `START` never translated to a `snapshot` GraphChange variant.** D2 changeset stream emits a `data` event for cached values when subscribing, but no "subscription established" envelope. The `GraphChangeSnapshot` reserved variant is exactly this case but unused. Combined with the dynamic-node-subscription fix, consumers must treat the first `data` per-scope as the seed. Defer; documented behavior, not a correctness bug.
  - **D-10 — `batch-end` version sync invariant runtime assertion.** The current invariant test asserts strictly nested versions per frame, which is correct only when the wave-body fires synchronously. If a subscribe callback is invoked from a microtask (none in this codebase, but spec doesn't forbid it), versions could interleave. Bounded today; defer adding a runtime assertion to dev-mode.

title: C1 — `MemoryRetrievalGraph.retrieveReactive` rework (locked 2026-04-30, design session)
body:
- **C1 — `MemoryRetrievalGraph.retrieveReactive` rework (locked 2026-04-30, design session).** Bundles M4 + EC3/EC4/EC12/EC13/EC14 from the Class B QA pass. Locked decisions:
  - **Per-input subgraph.** Each `retrieveReactive(input)` call constructs its own subgraph rather than sharing `this.retrieval` / `this.retrievalTrace` mirrors. Solves keepalive accumulation (M4), concurrent-query state crosstalk (M4), and anonymous internal nodes (EC12/EC13).
  - **Drop the imperative `retrieve()` API entirely.** Reactive-only; one-shot consumers use `awaitSettled(retrieveReactive(input))`. Resolves EC3/EC4 imperative-vs-reactive crosstalk and the spec §5.8 violation (reading `.cache` from `retrieve()` while a wave is active).
  - **Mount under hash-keyed segment** — `memoryRetrieval/retrieve::${id}/{context,result,mirror,projection}`. Each per-call subgraph registers its own teardown so disposers are local.
  - **Migrate `retrieval` / `retrievalTrace` to `this.state` (EC14).** B.4 second-pass cleanup — `GraphStateOptions` plumbs `equals`, so the migration is mechanical.
  - **Acceptance gate:** existing memory-retrieval tests pass; new test pinning concurrent `retrieveReactive(a)` + `retrieveReactive(b)` showing each subgraph's mirror is independent.
  - **Status:** ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a9d6e014b0686569f`. Files: `src/patterns/ai/memory/memory-composers.ts`, `src/patterns/ai/presets/agent-memory.ts`, `src/__tests__/patterns/ai.test.ts`. 2609/2609 tests pass.
  - **Deferred — counter vs hash-keyed segment (re-evaluate later).** Implementation used a per-instance monotonic counter (`_retrieveSeq`) for mount segment IDs (`retrieve_${seq}`) instead of an input-hash. Counter satisfies the M4/EC12/EC13 goal (each call gets a unique describe-visible segment, scoped disposers, no concurrent crosstalk). **Flag for re-evaluation if a future consumer needs same-input-→-same-subgraph memoization** — the resolution would be a real input-hash + reuse path. YAGNI today.
  - **Deferred — `MemoryWithVectorsGraph._store: DistillBundle<TMem>` dead field.** Biome flags it; pre-existing on `main`. Candidate for a separate dead-code sweep.

title: C2 — Widen `GraphDerivedOptions` / `GraphEffectOptions` to inherit `NodeOpts` (locked 2026-04-30, design session)
body:
- **C2 — Widen `GraphDerivedOptions` / `GraphEffectOptions` to inherit `NodeOpts` (locked 2026-04-30, design session).** Resolves the Class B audit follow-up at line 27 below. Locked decisions:
  - **Inherit all valid `NodeOpts` into `GraphDerivedOptions` and `GraphEffectOptions`** rather than widening field-by-field. Input-side fully matches `node()`: `guard`, `meta`, `equals`, future ABAC predicates, etc. all flow through without further widening.
  - **Restricted output side stays as-is.** Derived returns `readonly (T | null)[]` (array return); effect returns nothing. The Phase 11.5 ctx unification (raw batch shape) stays intact.
  - **Migration carries:** the 4 cqrs sites skipped in Class A Batch 2 (guard-using compute nodes) become migratable to `this.derived(name, deps, fn, { guard: ... })`. The TopicGraph self-resolve sites flagged at line 28 below get re-evaluated under the new option set (separate fix if needed).
  - **Why:** Guard / ABAC are key product features. The narrow-waist promise is "ergonomic by default"; if guard requires falling back to raw `node()`, the promise breaks for guarded computes.
  - **Acceptance gate:** the 4 skipped cqrs sites migrate cleanly; existing protocol tests (P1–P9) pass; new invariant pinning that all valid `NodeOpts` flow through `this.derived` / `this.effect` to the underlying `node()`.
  - **Status:** ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a3dabfd932deeaeca`. Widening implemented as `extends Omit<NodeOptions<T>, "name" | "describeKind">` (broader than C2 spec — `state` / `producer` widened consistently, not just `derived` / `effect`). 2 of 4 cqrs sites migrated (`command` + `projection`). New invariant P10 / #67 `graph-narrow-waist-options-flow-through`. 2608/2608 tests pass.
  - **Follow-up — `Graph.derived` deps accepts `(string | Node)[]` ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a8e7fea6e6b21b5be`.** Both remaining cqrs sites (`event` line 584, `event_aggregate` line 635) migrated to `this.derived(name, [entriesNodeRef], fn, opts)`. Param renamed `depPaths` → `deps`. P5 invariant preserved — strings still resolve at construction time. 3 new regression tests pin mixed `(string | Node)[]` deps. The Class B audit follow-up at line 27 ("`GraphDerivedOptions` doesn't expose `guard:`") is now fully cleared by C2 widening + this deps-side widening.
  - **TopicGraph self-resolve verdict.** Confirmed orthogonal to option types. `topic("events")` makes path string `"events"` collide with the graph-name strip in `_resolveFromSegments`. Not unblocked by widening. Leave as-is until a real consumer hits it.

title: D — Three-layer view: changeset stream + renderer extraction + `topologyView` factory (locked 2026-04-30, design session)
body:
- **D — Three-layer view: changeset stream + renderer extraction + `topologyView` factory (locked 2026-04-30, design session).** Implements the architectural decision at line ~196 below. Locked decisions:
  - **Sequencing:** D2 (changeset stream on `observe()`) lands first; D1 (renderer extraction) and D3 (`topologyView` factory) land after, parallelizable. D1 and D2 are independent (different `Graph` methods) so D1 can also start now; D3 depends on both.
  - **D1 — Renderer extraction. CLEAN API.** Existing `renderDescribeAs*` in `graph.ts:2133-2138` become public pure functions: `graphSpecToMermaid(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToSvg(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToAscii(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToReact(desc, opts?)`. **DROP the `format` sugar option from `describe()`** — pre-1.0 breaking, no shim. Callers using `g.describe({ format: "mermaid" })` migrate to `graphSpecToMermaid(g.describe())`.
  - **D2 — Changeset stream on `observe()`.** New structured all-nodes mode emits discrete `GraphChange` events. Variants: `data` (with **both** `fromPath` AND `fromDepIndex` for edge attribution), `dirty`, `resolved`, `error`, `complete`, `teardown`, `node-added`, `node-removed`, `mount`, `unmount`, `batch-start`, `batch-end`. Envelope shape: `{ version, timestamp_ns, scope }` + variant-specific payload — designed to accept post-1.0 store-level `StoreOp` variants on the same stream. `NodeV0` version counter (`core/versioning.ts`) is the natural monotonic version source.
  - **D3 — `topologyView` factory** at `src/patterns/topology-view/index.ts`:
    - **Signature:** `topologyView({ graph, layout?, ... }): TopologyViewGraph` — accepts target `Graph` in opts.
    - **Initial frame:** emits an initial `LayoutFrame` on subscribe (push-on-subscribe per spec §2.2). Consumers wanting deltas only `pipe(skip(1))`.
    - **Pluggable layout:** default hand-rolled minimal Sugiyama in `_internal.ts` (matches the library's "no deps, browser-safe" ethos); `opts.layout?: LayoutFn` plug-in slot following the LLM/storage adapter pattern. Users can swap in `dagre` or any other layered DAG library without forking.
    - **Internal nodes:** `derived("layout")` recomputes Sugiyama on topology changes only (filters changeset stream to topology variants); `derived("frame")` merges layout + data events into `LayoutFrame { boxes, edges, changes }`.
    - **No timers.** Animation driven by changeset batches — each batch IS a frame. CSS transitions handle visual fade.
  - **Acceptance gate:** D2 ships with property tests pinning each variant + envelope ordering; D1 ships byte-identical Mermaid/Svg/Ascii output post-extraction (snapshot test); D3 ships an integration test against a small target graph asserting initial-frame + topology-change-frame + data-change-frame.
  - **Status:**
    - D1 ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a56520ba3e4cd617d`. The `format` sugar drop was already in `main` from Tier 2.1 A2 (commit `f3b9b63`); this batch renamed `to*` → `graphSpecTo*` and migrated stale callers across `src/`, `examples/`, `website/`, `docs/`. Byte-identical fixture test pinned. 2608/2608 tests pass.
    - D2 ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a5dbc80bf7beb1f8c`. New `src/graph/changeset.ts` with `GraphChange` discriminated union + envelope `{ version, timestamp_ns, scope }`. New `observe({ changeset: true })` overload. 12 emitted variants + 6 reserved. Property tests #67–#70 (will renumber to #68–#71 when merged after C2). 2619/2619 tests pass.
    - **D2 design call endorsed: per-wave frame, not per-outer-`batch()` envelope.** The runtime fires sink callbacks per-source-coalesced-flush; no public hook for "outermost batch fully drained." Per-wave frames give finer animation granularity; consumers wanting outer-batch grouping can derive it from version-monotonic ordering.
    - **D2 → D3 readiness gaps documented (D3-side responsibilities, not stream gaps):** no `edge-added`/`edge-removed` events (consumer derives from `_deps` via `node-added` reaction); no initial-state replay (consumer seeds via `graph.describe()` then listens); path-scoped observe narrows data events but not topology events.
    - **D1 → D3 handoff: `graphSpecToSvg` / `graphSpecToReact` belong to D3 scope** (consume `LayoutFrame`, not `GraphDescribeOutput`).
    - D3 ✅ LANDED 2026-04-30 in `worktree-agent-a9d631f852d963f50`. New pattern at `src/patterns/topology-view/` with `topologyView({ graph, layout? })` factory, `TopologyViewGraph`, `LayoutFrame`/`LayoutBox`/`LayoutEdge`/`LayoutFn` types. Default layout adapter wraps the existing 687-LOC Sugiyama at `src/extra/render/_layout-sugiyama.ts` (no duplication; 100-LOC adapter only). New universal-safe SVG renderer `layoutFrameToSvg(frame, opts?)` at `src/extra/render/layout-frame-to-svg.ts`. 5 integration tests pass (initial frame, topology recompute, data-only frame, custom layout plug-in, SVG round-trip). Topology: `target.observe({changeset:true}) → derived("layout") → derived("frame")`; both deriveds use `partial: true` to open first-run gate so data-only waves still emit frames. Initial frame seeded via push-on-subscribe at construction time.
    - **D3 deferred items:** React renderer (needs too much shape variation for a single pure fn — park for `compat/react/` or `patterns/topology-view/react.ts` once a real consumer surfaces); explicit `batch-start`/`batch-end` boundary frames (currently collapsed into `frame.changes`; behind a future `boundaries: true` opt).

title: promptNode B.3 widening — `initial: []` on messages node REJECTED (locked 2026-04-30 post-Agent-A-review)
body:
- **promptNode B.3 widening — `initial: []` on messages node REJECTED (locked 2026-04-30 post-Agent-A-review).** The 2026-04-30 task description proposed adding `initial: []` to the messages node "for parity with `streamingPromptNode`." Re-evaluation rejected this:
  - The current SENTINEL-only design is semantically correct per the v5 guard pattern (`feedback_guard_patterns.md`): `undefined` = global SENTINEL, `T | null` = valid DATA, `=== undefined` is a valid sentinel guard.
