The two-tier semantics are load-bearing — **initial no-input** (no dep DATA'd → first-run gate blocks → outer cache stays `undefined`) is genuinely distinct from **mid-flow no-input** (deps DATA'd then went null → fn emits empty msgs → switchMap dispatches `node([], { initial: null })` → outer emits `null`).
  - Adding `initial: []` primes messagesNode at construction → switchMap fires immediately → empty-msgs branch dispatches its `initial: null` → outer emits phantom `null` BEFORE any LLM call. Re-breaks `harness.test.ts` `queueDepths["auto-fix"]` and `fast-retry exhaustion` (the same regression class that triggered the 2026-04-24 revert at `archive/docs/SESSION-ai-harness-module-review.md:3654`). `withLatestFrom`-paired triggers fire on the phantom null per implementation-plan L4.
  - Streaming/prompt parity is the wrong frame: streaming has no harness queue/retry feedback, so its constraints differ. Forcing parity here breaks load-bearing distinctions.
  - **Decision:** keep messagesNode without `initial:`; do NOT add a parallel "primed" state node either. The describe-parity argument doesn't hold — messagesNode is already named + tagged + visible in `describe()`; only its cache is `undefined` until first dep DATA, which is the correct behavior for first-run-gated nodes throughout the library.
  - **Action item:** update `archive/docs/SESSION-ai-harness-module-review.md` and the implementation plan to remove the `initial: []` ask for messagesNode. Spec-repo `~/src/graphrefly/COMPOSITION-GUIDE-PROTOCOL.md:516, :528` still references `prompt_node::call` — needs alignment to `::response` per the C+D widening rename.

title: Graph narrow-waist Phase 7 (landed) + Phase 9 (REVERTED) (2026-04-29)
body:
- **Graph narrow-waist Phase 7 (landed) + Phase 9 (REVERTED) (2026-04-29).** Per `archive/docs/SESSION-graph-narrow-waist.md` § "Course Correction" sections:
  - **Phase 7 — fast-check properties P1–P7 added** to `src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts` (registry 57 → 64). Names: `graph-derived-sentinel-absorption`, `graph-derived-keepalive-cache-current`, `graph-disposal-completeness`, `graph-batch-atomicity`, `graph-derived-path-resolution-construction`, `graph-narrow-waist-topology-visibility`, `graph-derived-keepalive-equivalence`. **P5 restated** to current contract (paths resolve at construction time + emissions flow during the mount's lifetime); the original "mount removal errors instead of stale ref" form is deferred under the rewire gap (C4). New invariants reference the SESSION doc directly via `specRef`. **Note:** these properties test the LEGACY `graph.derived` fn signature; ~~they will need rewriting in Phase 17 once restricted signatures land~~ **Phase 17 VERIFIED (2026-04-30):** P1–P7 already use restricted signatures, no changes needed.
  - **Phase 9 — closure-mirror migrations REVERTED (2026-04-29 evening, post-§28 reading).** *(Note: Phase 15 subsequently LANDED (scope-reduced) — see line below at "Path forward". Only 2 of ~9 sites were migratable: `composite.ts` verifiable + distill consolidated migrated to `withLatestFrom` reactive edges. Remaining sites are legitimately Level-3 closure-mirrors.)* Two pattern files were migrated from §28 closure-mirror to `graph.derived(..., { keepAlive: true })` then reverted after re-reading COMPOSITION-GUIDE §28 surfaced that the migrations are net regressions:
    - The §28 closure-mirror pattern reads a CLOSURE VARIABLE inside fn body (sanctioned per §3.6 boundary read at construction).
    - The migrated form replaces this with `graph.derived(keepAlive)` + `.cache` reads of the registered mirror node from inside reactive fn bodies. **`.cache` reads of other nodes inside reactive fn bodies ARE P3 violations.** The migration traded one shape (sanctioned) for a different shape (forbidden).
    - §28 also explicitly documents `withLatestFrom` migration as the WRONG direction for state+state-cached deps ("WRONG: withLatestFrom drops the initial pair under state+state deps"). Phase 16 attempted this and surfaced 5 test failures — the documented §28 symptom.
    - **Reverts (2026-04-29 evening):**
      - `src/patterns/ai/presets/agent-loop.ts`: 5 closure-mirrors (`latestTurn` / `latestAborted` / `latestStatus` / `latestMessages` / `latestSchemas`) restored to subscribe-and-capture form per §28.
      - `src/patterns/orchestration/pipeline-graph.ts`: `latestIsOpen` closure-mirror restored.
      - `src/extra/composite.ts` `verifiable` + `distill` (Phase 16 attempts): closure-mirrors were never modified in Phase 9; Phase 16 attempt was reverted in-session before tests went green.
      - `src/patterns/ai/presets/agent-memory.ts` (Phase 16 attempt): reverted.
    - Tests + lint clean post-revert. Comments at the closure-mirror sites cite §28 and reference this reversal.
  - **The closure-mirror visibility problem is real but framework-layer.** The reason §28 exists at all is that `withLatestFrom`'s initial-pair drop (sequential per-dep push-on-subscribe) blocks the more naturally reactive form. The framework-level fix is tracked at lines 812-822 below ("Multi-dep push-on-subscribe ordering — `withLatestFrom` initial-pair gap"). Two paths: (a) diamond-topology audit + dep-order flip, (b) activation-phase batching (coalesce all deps' push-on-subscribe into one combined initial wave). Once shipped, every §28 closure-mirror site can migrate to `withLatestFrom + switchMap + graph.add` cleanly.
  - **Sites NOT migrated (now correctly classified as §28-canonical):**
    - `src/patterns/ai/presets/agent-memory.ts` — §40 reactive-extractFn callback bridge using closure-mirror per §28.
    - `src/patterns/ai/memory/memory-composers.ts` — `latestCtx` closure-mirror; the retention.score read concern is an orthogonal reactiveMap API gap (D1 above), not a closure-mirror issue.
    - All other §28 sites in the library (`stratify`, `budgetGate`, `gate()`, `distill`'s `latestStore`, `verifiable`'s `latestSource`, etc.) — canonical §28, do not migrate.
  - **Verification:** 113 test files, 2605 tests passing post-revert. Property tests (`protocol-invariants.test.ts`) include 7 invariants (registry 65 with the registry-uniqueness assertion). Pre-existing `process.test.ts:789` `noBannedTypes` lint error is unchanged.
  - **Path forward:** ~~Phase 10 (Q2–Q9 design pass on restricted signatures) + Phase 10.5 (activation-phase batching framework fix design session). Phase 15 (library-wide §28 → withLatestFrom cleanup) is blocked on 10.5.~~ **All LANDED (2026-04-30):** Phase 10 (restricted signature design) LANDED. Phase 10.5 (`withLatestFrom` `partial: false` flip — 1-line fix, not a design session) LANDED. Phase 11 (restricted signatures implemented) LANDED. **Phase 11.5 (ctx unification, 2026-04-30):** `GraphDerivedFn`/`GraphEffectFn` first arg changed from unwrapped scalars to raw batch shape (`data: readonly (readonly unknown[] | undefined)[]`); `FnCtxDerived` extends `FnCtx` (gains `terminalDeps` + `store`); `FnCtxEffect` aliases `FnCtx`. `FnCtxProducer` stays `{ store }` only. Resolves Q-D' (same-value re-emit detection) by exposing batch shape natively — `data[i].length === N` for N-emit waves. Output side stays restricted (return array for derived, `up.pause`/`resume` only for effect). Test-local helper `latestVals(data, ctx)` shipped in `src/__tests__/test-helpers.ts` for tests that only care about scalar-per-dep. LANDED. Phase 12 (operators/sources refactored to raw `node()`) LANDED. Phase 13 (patterns refactored to Graph methods) LANDED. Phase 14 (core sugar removal — only `dynamicNode`, `autoTrackNode`, `pipe` remain) LANDED. Phase 15 (closure-mirror cleanup, SCOPE-REDUCED — 2 of ~9 sites migrated in `composite.ts`) LANDED. Phase 17 (P1–P7 invariant verification) VERIFIED. Phase 18 (composition guide split into 4 level files) LANDED. See SESSION doc § "Updated implementation sequence" for the post-§28 phase ordering.

title: Graph narrow-waist Bundle 3 — surface trim + explain/reachable fold (landed 2026-04-29)
body:
- **Graph narrow-waist Bundle 3 — surface trim + explain/reachable fold (landed 2026-04-29).** Pre-1.0 breaking. Per `archive/docs/SESSION-graph-narrow-waist.md` Phase 5 + Phase 6:
  - **Deleted** `Graph.get(name)`, `Graph.setAll(entries, opts?)`, `Graph.removeAll(filter)` — `Graph.get` was 0-caller in `src/`; `setAll` / `removeAll` had 1 test site each. Migration: `g.get("x")` → `g.node("x").cache`; `g.setAll({a: 1, b: 2})` → `g.batch(() => { g.set("a", 1); g.set("b", 2); })`; `g.removeAll(filter)` → inline `for (const n of names.filter(filter)) g.remove(n)`.
  - **Folded** `Graph.explain(from, to, opts?)` and `Graph.reachable(from, dir, opts?)` into `Graph.describe({ explain: {...} })` / `describe({ reachable: {...} })` mode-options. Reactive form: `describe({ explain: {...}, reactive: true })` returns `{ node: Node<CausalChain>; dispose }` (same shape as the old `explain({ reactive: true })`). `explain` and `reachable` are mutually exclusive with each other AND with the topology-snapshot options (`detail`/`fields`/`filter`/`actor`) — runtime throws `TypeError` on conflict. Tier 3.5 reactive-arg carve-out (F.9) preserved: `from`/`to`/`maxDepth`/`findCycle` still accept `Node<...>` in the explain mode.
  - **Kept** the standalone `reachable(graphDescribeOutput, from, direction, opts?)` export — it's the underlying impl that `describe({ reachable })` delegates to; tests exercise the standalone form directly. `explainPath` standalone export likewise unchanged.
  - **Kept** `Graph.trace` + `TraceEntry` + `traceCapacity` — annotation surface is load-bearing for `explain`'s `CausalStep.annotation`.
  - **Migrations:** 174 test-site changes across 18 files in `src/__tests__/` + 18 changes across `demos/` and `examples/`. `LensGraph.why()` JSDoc and `InspectGraph.explainTarget` facade rewired to `target.describe({ explain })`.
  - **/qa pass landed in-batch (2026-04-29):** Two parallel reviewers (Blind Hunter + Edge Case Hunter) surfaced ~28 unique findings (overlap deduplicated). All 5 patches landed: A1 stale `from: from, to: to` JSDoc fix in `examples/inbox-reducer/pipeline.ts:35`; A2 test describe-block label rename in `audit.test.ts:466-467`; A3 bulk-remove test universe derived from `g._nodes.keys()`/`g._mounts.keys()` instead of hardcoded `["a","b","c"]`; A4 `reactiveName` JSDoc note about explain-mode `name`/`reactiveName` aliasing precedence; A5 long-line demo reformat. **D2** (BH1/EC8) tightened reachable-mode `reactive` validation from `!== undefined` to `=== true || === "diff"` so explicit `reactive: false`/`undefined` pass through as no-ops (symmetric with explain mode). **D1** added 19 dispatch-contract spot tests in `src/__tests__/graph/graph.test.ts` covering: SENTINEL routing for explain/reachable, return-shape parity, mutual-exclusion TypeErrors (explain+reachable, mode+detail/fields/filter/actor, reactive in reachable, reactive:"diff" in explain), `name ?? reactiveName` precedence (3 cases). **D3** migrated stale prose in `examples/{inbox-reducer,knowledge-graph,spending-alerts}/README.md` + `website/src/content/docs/blog/42-one-describe.md` + `website/src/content/docs/demos/{spending-alerts,knowledge-graph,index}.md`; regenerated `website/src/content/docs/api/graphLens.md` via `pnpm --dir website docs:gen`.
  - **Verification:** 2598 tests passing (+19 dispatch-contract tests over Bundle 3 baseline 2579); lint clean (10-warning baseline preserved); build green (`assertBrowserSafeBundles` honored); typecheck clean (only pre-existing `_invariants.ts` / `core/hash.ts` errors).
  - **Deferred carry-over:** the Bundle 1 C1–C4 deferrals below (graph.batch throw / keepAlive cache / cross-graph node ownership / path-based derived rewire gap) all still apply post-Bundle 3.
  - **QA-pass deferrals (consumer-driven, lower priority):**
    - **C5: `_explainReactive` `equals` tuple missing `from`/`to` (EC12).** Pre-fold `_explainReactive` bug — when reactive args change `from`/`to` to a path producing identical `steps`/`found`/`reason`, the `equals` deduper suppresses the new emission and subscribers see stale `from`/`to`. Pre-existing; not new in Bundle 3. Fix: include `a.from === b.from && a.to === b.to` in [graph.ts:2878-2883](../src/graph/graph.ts#L2878). Defer until a real consumer hits stale `from`/`to`.
    - **C6: `_explainReactive` audit-meta `from`/`to` snapshot stale on reactive args (EC17).** [graph.ts:2872-2877](../src/graph/graph.ts#L2872) captures `meta.from = resolveExplainPath(from)` ONCE at construction; the closure body re-resolves on each recompute, but the meta snapshot stays frozen. Audit dashboards filtering on `meta.from === "X"` mismatch after a reactive arg change. Pre-fold behavior; the comment at lines 2868-2871 calls it "best-effort provenance." Fix options: (a) document the limit in `GraphDescribeExplainInput` JSDoc; (b) drop `from`/`to` from `meta` when they're reactive (only set when both static strings). Defer until an audit consumer surfaces the gap.
    - **C7: Reachable mode passes no `actor` to inner `this.describe()` (EC11).** Pre-fold `Graph.reachable` had identical limitation — reachable always sees the full unscoped topology. The conflict-rejection at the new dispatch makes it impossible to feed an `actor` in directly. If a consumer wants actor-scoped reachability, the bundle's primary topology surface should expose it: extend `GraphDescribeReachableInput` with an optional `actor?: Actor | Node<Actor>` and pass it through to the inner `this.describe({ actor })`, then call `reachable(...)` over the scoped output. Defer until a real consumer asks.
    - **C8: `actor: null` rejection diagnostic asymmetry (EC9).** Mutual-exclusion check uses `opts[conflicting] !== undefined`. An explicit `actor: null` (or any non-undefined falsy) throws `\`actor\` not accepted` — confusing for callers using `null` to mean "clear it." Either tighten to `opts[conflicting] != null` (treat `null` and `undefined` symmetrically as "absent") or document precisely. Defer to first user report.
    - **C9: `InspectGraph.explainTarget` cast collapses overload set + loosely-typed `describeOpts` (BH8/BH9/EC15/EC16).** The new facade impl casts through `unknown` to a single-overload function and uses `Record<string, unknown>` for the locally-built describe options. Loses overload-resolution safety for future `describe()` mode additions. Lower-risk because the local opts are constructed by the facade itself. Future tightening: extract a typed `GraphDescribeExplainInput`-aware overload signature and use it directly without the cast hop. Defer.
    - **C10: `InspectGraph.explainTarget` doesn't expose `reactiveName` slot (EC16).** Underlying `describe({ explain, reactive: true })` accepts both `name` and `reactiveName`, but the facade's overload signature accepts only `name`. Asymmetry — inspect's wrapper is stricter than the underlying surface. Either deprecate `reactiveName` on `describe({ explain })` mode entirely (keep `name` only) for internal consistency, or add `reactiveName?` to the facade's overloads. Defer with a slight lean toward deprecating `reactiveName` aliasing in explain mode pre-1.0.
    - **C11: Forward `name` collision risk on `GraphDescribeOptions` (EC19).** Today `name` lives at the outer level of `describe({ explain: {...}, reactive: true, name: "..." })`. If `GraphDescribeOptions` ever gains a top-level `name` field meaning something else (e.g., topology-mode tagging), ambiguity is silent. Forward-compat lock: move `name` INSIDE the `explain` object (e.g., `explain: { from, to, name: "..." }`) — eliminates the collision class. Backward-incompatible — but per `feedback_no_backward_compat`, pre-1.0 it's free. Defer until a real new `name` consumer surfaces.

title: Three-layer view model + changeset stream (2026-04-27, design-review)
body:
- **Three-layer view model + changeset stream (2026-04-27, design-review).** Architectural decision: the live topology view decomposes into three layers — **(1) Topology** (structure/edges/types via `describe()` → GraphSpec JSON, fully restorable), **(2) Data** (runtime values/causal flow via `observe()` changeset stream, best-effort replay — sync/async/stream timing is inherently lossy), **(3) Functions** (not serializable by design — the library abstracts fn bodies behind causal chains). Layer 1 + Layer 2 together provide the complete live view; Layer 3 is intentionally hidden. Key sub-decisions:
  - **Renderer extraction (D1 — LANDED 2026-04-30).** `describe()` outputs data only (GraphDescribeOutput / GraphSpec). Rendering lives in `@graphrefly/graphrefly/extra/render` as pure functions: `graphSpecToMermaid(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToAscii(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToD2(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToPretty(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToJson(desc, opts?)`, `graphSpecToMermaidUrl(desc, opts?)`. The original Tier 2.1 A2 extraction (2026-04-27) shipped these under `to*` names with the `format` sugar still on `describe()`; the D1 follow-up (2026-04-30) **dropped the `format` sugar entirely** (pre-1.0 BREAKING — no shim) and renamed the public functions to the explicit `graphSpecTo*` form. Internal callers (`validate-observability`, `demo-shell`), the in-tree examples (`inbox-reducer`, `harness-refine-hello`, `nestjs/order-flow`), the website docs (`recipes/nestjs-integration.md`, `blog/42-one-describe.md` postscript, `blog/45-ai-first-engineering.md`), and the test suite all migrated. `src/__tests__/graph/graph.test.ts` has a byte-identical fixture regression ("D1 byte-identical fixture pins each graphSpecTo* renderer's output") that locks Mermaid / D2 / Pretty / JSON / MermaidUrl output for a known a→b + `c::leaf` mount fixture so any future renderer drift surfaces as a snapshot failure. `graphSpecToSvg` and `graphSpecToReact` are not yet shipped — they were called out in the original D1 sketch but defer to D3 (`topologyView` / `LayoutFrame`).
  - **Changeset stream on `observe()`.** New structured all-nodes mode emits discrete `GraphChange` events instead of accumulated state. Core types: `data` (with `fromPath`/`fromDepIndex` for edge attribution), `dirty`, `resolved`, `error`, `complete`, `teardown`. Topology types: `node-added`, `node-removed`, `mount`, `unmount`. Batch boundary types: `batch-start`, `batch-end` (groups changes into animation frames). Future extensions: `pause`/`resume`, `invalidate`, `trace`, `resubscribe`, `snapshot`. The changeset stream unifies topology + data inputs for `topologyView` into a single subscription.
  - **Reactive animation (no timers).** Animation is driven by the changeset stream itself — each batch of changes IS the animation frame. Renderer highlights edges/nodes based on `data` changes in the current batch; next batch replaces previous highlights. CSS `transition` handles visual fade — no `setTimeout`, no `fromTimer`, no spec §5.10 violation.
  - **`topologyView(graph)` factory.** Thin graph (patterns/topology-view/): `derived("layout")` recomputes Sugiyama on topology changes only; `derived("frame")` merges layout + changeset data events into `LayoutFrame { boxes, edges, changes }`. Pure renderer functions consume `LayoutFrame`. Follows reactive-layout precedent (headless graph → data structure → consumer renders).
  - **Relationship to post-1.0 op-log changesets.** The observe changeset stream (graph-level: "DATA flowed from A→B") and the post-1.0 op-log changesets (store-level: "key X set in MapBackend") operate at different layers and do NOT block each other. However, both share a common envelope shape: `{ version, timestamp_ns, scope }` + variant-specific payload. Design the `GraphChange` type with these envelope fields so the store-level `StoreOp` variants (post-1.0, `§8.7 Delta checkpoints & WAL`) can plug into the same stream later. `Graph.diff()` (`graph.ts:3771`) already proves the change vocabulary (added/removed/changed/version-bumped); the changeset stream is that vocabulary emitted live. `NodeV0` version counter (`core/versioning.ts`, currently unused) is the natural monotonic version source for both layers.
  - **Blocked on:** (a) changeset stream type definition + `observe()` enhancement, (b) ~~renderer extraction PR~~ ✅ landed 2026-04-30 (D1 above), (c) `topologyView` factory. Can be sequenced independently. No dependency on post-1.0 op-log work.

title: Google SDK shape migration `@google/generative-ai` → `@google/genai` (DONE 2026-04-24, AI/harness review tail)
body:
- **Google SDK shape migration `@google/generative-ai` → `@google/genai` (DONE 2026-04-24, AI/harness review tail).** [src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/google.ts](../src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/google.ts) `GoogleSdkLike` interface tightened to the `@google/genai` single-param `generateContent({ model, contents, config })` shape; `abortSignal` lives under `config` (no second-arg `{signal}`). `sdkBackedGoogle` reshapes the fetch-API body into SDK params via a new `toSdkParams` helper that moves `generationConfig.{temperature, maxOutputTokens, thinkingConfig}` and top-level `systemInstruction` / `tools` into `config`, and threads `invokeOpts.signal` as `config.abortSignal`. `generateContentStream` now `await`s the iterable (the new SDK returns `Promise<AsyncIterable<...>>`; awaiting a non-thenable resolves to itself, so legacy direct-iterable shims still iterate). Fetch path unchanged — REST body still uses the `generationConfig` shape. `package.json` already declared `@google/genai ^1.48.0`; the migration was about tightening the duck-type the adapter accepts, not adding a runtime import.

- ~~**`defer()` / `fromAnyThunk` extras candidate**~~ **SHIPPED 2026-04-25** — archived to `archive/optimizations/resolved-decisions.jsonl` (id: `defer-extras-operator`). See [src/extra/sources.ts](../src/extra/sources.ts) `defer<T>()` and [sources.test.ts](../src/__tests__/extra/sources.test.ts) for the 6 regression tests.

title: Wave A Unit 4 Ring 2 trio landed (2026-04-24) — `agentLoop` closure-mirror symmetry, `_runVersion` retirement, `toolExecution` extraction:
body:
- **Wave A Unit 4 Ring 2 trio landed (2026-04-24) — `agentLoop` closure-mirror symmetry, `_runVersion` retirement, `toolExecution` extraction:**
  - **Closure-mirror symmetry (COMPOSITION-GUIDE §28).** `promptInput` in [src/patterns/ai/agents/agent-loop.ts](../src/patterns/ai/agents/agent-loop.ts) now samples `chat.messages` and `tools.schemas` via the same subscribe-and-capture template that `latestTurn` / `latestAborted` / `latestStatus` already used. No more `chat.allMessages()` / `tools.schemas.cache` inline cache reads from inside a reactive fn body — every cross-subgraph sample goes through a seeded `let latestX = ...` mirror. `messagesSub` / `schemasSub` disposers wired into the subgraph teardown so unmount tears them down alongside the other mirror subscriptions.
  - **`_runVersion` retirement.** `_terminalResult` now emits `LLMResponse` directly (was `{response, runVersion}`) and `run()` uses `awaitSettled(_terminalResult, { skipCurrent: true })` — the `extra/sources.ts` infrastructure shipped earlier does the stale-resolution gating entirely at the subscribe boundary. Removed: `_runVersion` field, the `++this._runVersion` stamp, the compound object allocation per emission, the `run()` predicate that compared `v.runVersion`. Subscribe-before-kick ordering is load-bearing: `awaitSettled` subscribes synchronously during the `async` function's initial execution slice, BEFORE `status.emit("thinking")` drains the pipeline — otherwise a sync adapter (all current mocks / offline stubs) races past the subscribe and `skipCurrent: true` swallows the only DATA this run will produce. Comment in `run()` body documents the invariant.
  - **`toolExecution` primitive.** New file [src/patterns/ai/agents/tool-execution.ts](../src/patterns/ai/agents/tool-execution.ts). Signature: `toolExecution({ toolCalls, tools, retryCount?, onError? }): Node<readonly ToolResult[]>`. Lifted from the inlined `executeToolReactively` helper; `agentLoop` now composes it (`retryCount: 1`, `onError: "rescue"` defaults). Re-exported from `patterns/ai/index.ts`. Public surface: `ToolResult`, `ToolExecutionOptions`, `toolExecution`. The primitive throws loudly on empty batches (upstream gate must emit RESOLVED for empty) — matches the pre-extraction assertion so callers that regress the gating get a diagnostic instead of silent no-op.
  - **`ToolRegistryGraph` reactive-only.** QA pass (2026-04-24) removed the imperative `execute(name, args): Promise<unknown>` method entirely — `executeReactive(name, args): Node<unknown>` is now the sole execution path. `executeReactive` was rewritten as a `producer` that owns a per-call `AbortController`, threads `signal` into both `tool.handler(args, {signal})` and the inner source coercion, and fires `ac.abort()` on producer teardown — so `switchMap` supersede in `toolExecution` actually cancels in-flight handler work for signal-aware handlers. Sync handler throws surface as `[[ERROR, err]]` inside the producer (no escape). A new `handlerResultToNode` internal helper preserves the pre-refactor "treat string / array / scalar as single DATA" semantics by routing those shapes through `Promise.resolve(raw)` rather than `fromAny` (which would iterate strings as chars and arrays as elements). Tool handler signature widened to `(args, opts?: {signal?: AbortSignal}) => NodeInput<unknown>`; existing handlers ignoring opts still work. Four tool-registry tests migrated from `await tr.execute(...)` to `await awaitSettled(tr.executeReactive(...))`; one new regression test asserts unsubscribe → `ac.abort()` propagation.
  - **Verification:** 2170 TS tests pass (+4 gatedStream unskips, +1 abort-propagation regression, +1 stale-response regression). Lint clean. Build green including `assertBrowserSafeBundles` — `tool-execution.ts` and `tool-registry.ts` are universal-safe.
  - **Remaining Unit 4 follow-ons:**
    - ~~`toolExecution` has no direct test file yet — coverage comes through `agent-loop.ts` integration tests.~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-24** — standalone unit test shipped at [src/__tests__/patterns/ai/agents/tool-execution.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/agents/tool-execution.test.ts). Covers happy-path multi-call, empty-batch invariant throw, rescue vs propagate error modes, retry on transient failure, and switchMap supersede signal abort.
    - `meta.closureReads: [...]` JSDoc annotation (session-doc Unit 4 Q4) — not landed with this trio; lightweight observability nice-to-have, add when the inspection tooling actually consumes it.


  Unblocks part of the "fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27 — blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks" follow-on that has trailed batches 11–19. Ships a minimal ghost-state capture surface on [src/core/config.ts](../src/core/config.ts) (mirroring the existing `globalInspector` shape — zero-cost-when-unset, no freeze), wires three call sites in [src/core/node.ts](../src/core/node.ts), and lands 4 fast-check invariants (#54–#57) that previously had no runtime counterpart. Deltas:
  - **API.** New `RigorRecorder` interface on [config.ts](../src/core/config.ts) with two methods: `onNonVacuousInvalidate(node, prevValue)` and `onTerminalTransition(node, kind, autoComplete, autoError, hasDeps)`. Attached via `cfg.rigorRecorder = ...` / cleared by assigning `undefined`. Like `globalInspector`, the slot is operational (settable post-freeze, no config-freeze trigger). Production cost: one `cfg.rigorRecorder != null` branch per call site.
  - **Call sites.** Three hook calls in `NodeImpl._updateState`'s message walk (the post-`_frameBatch` state-commit waist — shared by both direct `.down([[INVALIDATE]])` and dep-cascade `_onDepMessage` → `_emit(INVALIDATE_ONLY_BATCH)` paths):
    - COMPLETE branch — fires `onTerminalTransition(this, "completed", _autoComplete, _autoError, _deps.length > 0)` right after `_status = "completed"`.
    - ERROR branch — same shape, classifying `"errored"`.
    - INVALIDATE branch — fires `onNonVacuousInvalidate(this, this._cached)` BEFORE the `_cached = undefined` reset, guarded on `_cached !== undefined` so vacuous invalidates (diamond fan-in second arrival, never-populated mid-chain derived) don't fire. This placement replaced an earlier (first-draft) hook at `_onDepMessage`'s INVALIDATE branch — that site only covered the cascade path; the rewrite to `_updateState` covers both cascade AND direct `.down([[INVALIDATE]])`, which the first-draft test cases exposed by failing with a vacuous witness log.
  - **Fast-check mirrors (4 new invariants in [_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts), registry 53 → 57):**
    - **#54 `cleanup-witness-in-value-domain`** (mirrors TLC #19) — state → derived. Drive N cycles of `s.emit(v)` → `d.down([[INVALIDATE]])` → second (vacuous) invalidate; assert exactly N witnesses on `d`, all with non-`undefined` `prevValue`.
    - **#55 `cleanup-witness-not-sentinel`** (mirrors TLC #24) — interleaved invalidates across both source and derived across ≥1 emit cycle; sweep every witness entry and assert `prevValue !== undefined`. Structural sibling to #54 — whereas #54 asserts count + value-domain, #55 exercises more paths and asserts value-domain only.
    - **#56 `cleanup-witness-accounting`** (mirrors TLC #26) — quantitative law: witness-log length per node equals the number of non-vacuous invalidates driven (populated → cleared transitions). Catches off-by-N regressions between the runtime's `_cached === undefined` guard and the hook fire.
    - **#57 `no-dep-cascade-terminal-when-gate-false`** (mirrors TLC #25) — derived with `completeWhenDepsComplete: false` / `errorWhenDepsError: false` over two state sources; drive both deps to COMPLETE (or ERROR); sweep `log.terminals` and assert no entry with `kind === "completed" && autoComplete === false && hasDeps` (same for errored). Load-bearing: a refactor that drops the `_autoComplete` check inside `_maybeAutoTerminalAfterWave` trips this.
  - **Dropped TLC mirrors** (documented inline in `_invariants.ts` under "Dropped TLC mirrors"):
    - **#18 `MultiSinkIterationCoherent` / #27 `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean`** — TLA+ ghost `pendingExtraDelivery` has no runtime analogue. The runtime's `_deliverToSinks` iterates sinks synchronously over a single shared `messages` reference; there is no mid-iteration pending queue to sweep. A mirror would be tautological vs. the runtime's `_updateState` writing the exact values the messages carry.
    - **#20 `ReplayBufferBounded` / #23 `LateSubscriberReceivesReplay`** — §2.5 `replayBuffer: N` is not implemented in the TS runtime yet. Mirror ships with the runtime feature if/when it lands.
    - **#22 `MetaTeardownObservedPreReset`** — §2.3 meta companion TEARDOWN tier-5 modeling is not in the TS runtime. Mirror ships with the runtime feature.
    - **#28 `TerminatedImpliesBatchIdle`** — TLA+ models `batchActive` as an *idle-gate* on terminal actions (`Terminate` / `Teardown` / `SinkNestedEmit` require `batchActive.status = "idle"`). The TS runtime allows terminal transitions inside an active `batch()` because `_frameBatch` + `downWithBatch` tier-sort delivery so DATA always ships before COMPLETE in the flushed wave — the TLA+ guard's purpose (preventing stale DATA after terminal) is enforced by the tier-sort at delivery time rather than by an idle-gate at action-application time. A runtime mirror would either be tautological (checking the tier-sort rather than the claimed property) or fire false positives on every legitimate inside-batch terminal emit. Classified as a TLA+ modeling law, not a runtime invariant.
  - **Design decision — config-hosted vs. per-node slot.** First-draft design proposed a per-`NodeImpl` `__rigor` field with an `instrumentNode()` helper. User called for central-singleton-config placement instead (mirroring the precedent already set by `globalInspector` / `inspectorEnabled`); the final shape matches that precedent exactly. Benefits: zero `NodeImpl` field growth, test isolation per-config (each property run constructs its own `GraphReFlyConfig`), production zero-cost via the `cfg.rigorRecorder != null` branch — same cost profile as the existing `inspectorEnabled` / `globalInspector` gate.
  - **Verification:** 2154 TS tests pass (was 2150 before the substrate wiring; +4 from the new invariants — no-delta `config.rigorRecorder` unset path means existing 95 test files unaffected). Lint clean (biome reformatted the import sort once). Build green. `assertBrowserSafeBundles` unaffected (no new `node:*` imports — the recorder + call sites are universal-safe). 3/3 serial stability check on the property suite (no flakes — the invariants drive deterministic graph topologies with fast-check integer seeds). Total fast-check invariant count: 57 (up from 53). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (narrower post-batch-20):
    - Fast-check mirrors for TLC #20 / #22 / #23 — gated on the corresponding runtime features landing (`replayBuffer: N`, meta-companion TEARDOWN). Defer until concrete need surfaces.
    - #18 / #27 / #28 — classified as TLA+-only; no runtime analogue by design.
    - Compound-topology / lifecycle-edge invariants (`windowTime`, `bufferCount + completeOnPartialTail`, `rescue + source resubscribable`, `switchMap + pause/resume`) — still bug-report-driven per batch 19's note.
  - **Batch 20 QA pass (same day).** Adversarial review surfaced one class of test-authoring fragility worth hardening: the new invariants filter the witness / terminal log by node identity (`w.node === d`), which silently returns `[]` if a future `Node<T>` refactor wraps the returned node in a Proxy / façade — turning a regression into a vacuous pass. Fix applied to all 4 invariants (#54 / #55 / #56 / #57) at [_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts): a non-vacuous pre-check on the raw log length (#54 / #56: `log.witnesses.length >= expected`; #55: `log.witnesses.length >= emits.length`; #57: `log.terminals` contains >= 2 source-side terminal entries matching the driven path) runs BEFORE the identity-filter equality check. If the premise of the property ever stops holding (premise = "the test's setup actually exercises the code path it claims to"), the new check fires loud. Other Blind Hunter findings rejected: `equals: () => false` on sources is intentional to isolate the INVALIDATE/terminal code paths from equals dedup; error swallowing around recorder calls matches the precedent set by `globalInspector` (instrumentation must not break data plane); config-freeze-not-triggered is the deliberate operational-slot semantic; `#57 drainExtra` post-terminal emit is safe because terminated non-resubscribable sources filter COMPLETE/ERROR out at the `_emit` terminal filter (`node.ts:1963-1966`). Post-fix: 2154 TS tests pass (unchanged count), lint clean, build green, 3/3 serial stability confirmed.

title: Wave B harness implementation landed (2026-04-24) — follow-ons:
body:
- **Wave B harness implementation landed (2026-04-24) — follow-ons:**
  - **`gateGraph.mount` vs hub queue topics.** Unit 17 B's intended shape `gateGraph.mount(\`queue/\${route}\`, topic)` is inapplicable once Unit 20 C put queue topics inside the hub — a topic can't be mounted in two parents. The harness falls back to `gateGraph.add(hub.topic(route).latest, { name: ... })`, which re-introduces the wrapper-node shape Unit 17 B was aiming to eliminate. Not a bug (both paths produce identical runtime behavior) but the double-registration is visible in `describe()`. Options if this becomes load-bearing: (a) teach `gate()` to accept a foreign node, (b) move the gateGraph inside the hub, (c) let gate resolve across graph boundaries via a path like `queues::${route}::latest`.
  - **JobQueueGraph EXECUTE is audit-layer only.** Per Unit 21's locked decision ("executor interface unchanged"), the per-route `JobQueueGraph`s under `harness.jobs.<route>` mirror the queue topics as a shadow observability surface — `depth` / `pending` / `jobs` readouts are live — but the dataflow (`merge(queueTopics.latest) → executor`) is unchanged. Full claim/ack/nack integration into the primary dataflow would require changing the executor contract and was explicitly ruled out in Unit 21. Revisit if backpressure-driven scheduling (priority-ordered consumption, per-queue concurrency caps) becomes a requirement. **QA round (2026-04-24) added `JobQueueGraph.remove(id)`** so the audit layer can terminate jobs by id instead of FIFO-claim; the mirror uses a `WeakSet` to detect new items so it survives ring-buffer truncation under `retainedLimit`.
  - **`priorityScore` head-of-queue wiring.** `HarnessGraph.priorityScores` exposes per-route score nodes that read `topic.latest` as the "head" item. Topic latest is the most RECENT item, not the highest-priority — so this reflects "current pressure at head" rather than "best item to pick next". A proper priority-ordered queue (heap-backed topic variant) is tracked separately under queue-layer enhancement; the score node is useful for dashboards in the meantime.
  - **`gate.reject()` snapshot outside `batch()` — cosmetic.** `const snapshot = [...queue]` in [src/patterns/orchestration/index.ts](../src/patterns/orchestration/index.ts) is captured before the `batch(() => { emit(...); dequeue(count) })` scope. Single-threaded JS guarantees no mid-snapshot mutation; the snapshot and batch body read/write the same `queue` reference in sequence. Moving the clone inside the batch would make the invariant explicit but changes nothing runtime. Low priority: fold into any future `gate()` refactor.
  - **`topicBridge` retention fragility on `triage-output`.** Every triaged item fans to 5 bridges (4 routes + `__unrouted`) via independent `SubscriptionGraph` cursors. `triage-output` itself retains every publish up to `retainedLimit` (default 1000). Late-attached bridges (none today, but plausible for a future hot-swap of routing) would only see the current retained window — anything older gets ring-buffer trimmed. Mitigations if this surfaces: (a) set `triage-output`'s `retainedLimit: 0` since all bridges attach eagerly at construction and have no reason to back-read, (b) add a `messaging.fanOut()` sugar that wires N bridges + asserts all at cursor 0 before the first publish, (c) surface a warning when any bridge's cursor lags the source log length. Systemic messaging-layer concern; not unique to harness.

title: Rigor-infra batches 12–19 QA pass (landed 2026-04-24) — assertion-strengthening sweep:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batches 12–19 QA pass (landed 2026-04-24) — assertion-strengthening sweep:**
  Adversarial review surfaced a class of "weak invariant" findings across the 36 new operator-layer invariants: docstrings claimed behaviors the property bodies never exercised, and equals-absorption at the source quietly masked duplicate-value shrinker inputs. 10 fixes landed, all at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). Deltas:
  - **Group A (auto-applied):**
    - **#25 debounce / #27 throttle** — added `{ equals: () => false }` to the respective sources so fast-check's `[5, 5]`-style shrunk counter-examples aren't Object.is-absorbed before the operator sees them. Previously a regression that passes every emit (instead of coalescing) would still look like "one DATA passed" on any duplicate-value input.
    - **#30 sample** — "source-completed leaks" claim in the docstring was untested. Added a post-loop `src.down([[COMPLETE]])` and assert that subsequent `ticker.emit(...)` calls produce no additional DATA (sample must clear its held value on source COMPLETE and not re-emit on notifier ticks).
    - **#24 repeat** — over-repeat check: after the N-th COMPLETE drives `completes === 1`, send one extra `s.down([[COMPLETE]])` and assert `completes` stays at 1 (catches a regression where `remaining` miscounts and repeat resubscribes infinitely).
    - **#39 take / #47 takeWhile / #51 find / #52 elementAt** — each of these operators uses `completeWhenDepsComplete: false` to emit its own threshold COMPLETE. Added a post-terminal `s.down([[COMPLETE]])` in each invariant + assert `completes` stays at 1 (catches a regression that lets the source COMPLETE bubble through as a second downstream COMPLETE).
    - **#43 merge** — staged-COMPLETE check: merge must NOT forward downstream COMPLETE until every inner source has completed. Complete N-1 sources and assert `completes === 0`; complete the last and assert `completes === 1`. Previously the "premature COMPLETE on one-source-complete" claim in the docstring had no assertion.
  - **Group B — decisions applied:**
    - **B1 (a) — #23 race** rewritten to use captured-push `producer` sources instead of `state`. Previous form selected the "winner" by push-on-subscribe order on cached-state DATAs — an artifact of §2.2 push-on-subscribe rather than race's actual first-DATA-arrival semantic. New form explicitly pushes `WINNER_TAG_1` into `sources[0]` first, then loser tags into the rest, then `WINNER_TAG_2` into `sources[0]` again; asserts `emitted` is exactly `[WINNER_TAG_1, WINNER_TAG_2]` (no loser leak, winner-continues). More honest test of the contract.
    - **B3 (a) — #49 tap** gained a second property arm covering side-effect / emit decoupling. Arm 2: source has `equals: () => false`, tap does NOT, all emits are identical so tap's Object.is absorbs everything after the first DATA. Asserts `sideEffects.length === repeats` (fn fires per source DATA) while `emittedCount === 1` (tap's output absorbs). Locks in the documented ordering: `fnOrObserver(v)` is called BEFORE `a.emit(v)` per iteration, independent of downstream absorption — catches a refactor that moves the fn call after `a.emit`.
  - **Group B — decisions deferred:**
    - **B2 — #46 combine first-run-gate arm** deferred. `combine` already defaults to `partial: false` via `derived`; the finding was that the TEST doesn't exercise the gate because both `state` deps are cached. Adding a producer-dep arm would verify the gate actively, but this surface is already covered by fast-check #10 `multi-dep-initial-pair-clean` at the core layer. Add if a concrete `combine`-partial regression surfaces.
    - **B4 — #26 timeout ERROR payload** deferred. Minor — the current assertion checks `m[0] === ERROR` but not `m[1]` (the Error instance / message). The operator's default error payload is stable; revisit if the payload shape is ever formalized.
  - **Rejected findings (blind-hunter noise):**
    - **#35 bufferTime initial-empty-flush** — impl guards with `if (buf.length > 0)`; no empty emit possible.
    - **#36 interval starting-counter** — impl's `setInterval` fires at t=period not t=0, counter starts at 0 → matches test.
    - **#53 windowCount lazy-vs-proactive** — impl IS lazy (`openWindow` on first DATA), matches test.
    - **#46 combine "custom element-wise tuple-equals not in test"** — the custom equals is set INSIDE `combine()`'s impl, not expected to be passed by callers. Test is correct.
  - **Verification:** 2117 TS tests pass (unchanged count — all changes are inside existing invariants + one new property arm in #49). Lint clean, build green. 10/10 serial stability check on the property suite. Fast-check invariant count unchanged at 53; individual invariants are structurally stronger.

title: Rigor-infra batch 19 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion closes: takeUntil / find / elementAt / windowCount:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 19 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion closes: takeUntil / find / elementAt / windowCount:**
  Final operator-layer batch. Four invariants close out the `src/extra/operators.ts` surface — at 53 fast-check invariants total, every shipped primitive operator (map/filter/scan/reduce/take/skip/first/last/find/elementAt/tap/distinctUntilChanged/pairwise/merge/concat/race/zip/combine/repeat/delay/timeout/throttle/debounce/audit*/sample/buffer/bufferCount/bufferTime/windowCount/interval/rescue/switchMap/mergeMap/concatMap/exhaustMap/takeWhile/takeUntil) has at least one contract invariant in the registry. (* `audit` currently debounce-like — invariant skipped pending RxJS-semantics fix.) Deltas:
  - **#50 `takeuntil-terminates-on-notifier-data`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` (src) + captured-trigger `producer<number>` (notifier stays silent until `triggerNotifier()` is called, ruling out cached-DATA termination during subscribe — otherwise takeUntil would complete immediately since the notifier `subscribe` happens after src `subscribe` and any cached notifier DATA would fire the stop branch). Emit M src values, then trigger notifier → one COMPLETE. K more src values ignored. Catches: notifier ignored, premature termination from unrelated messages, late src DATA leak after `stopped`, double-COMPLETE.
  - **#51 `find-emits-first-matching-and-completes`** — `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `find(s, v => v > 100)`. Cached DATA(0) + `nonMatches` more values `1..nonMatches` (all < 100) all skipped. `s.emit(500)` (match) → one DATA = 500, one COMPLETE. Also exercises the `find = take(filter(src, predicate), 1)` composition — a broken `take` or `filter` would fail here on a concrete domain semantic.
  - **#52 `elementat-emits-nth-and-completes`** — `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` + `elementAt(s, index)` with `index ∈ 0..3`. Source emits `[0, 1, ..., index]`; the index-th value is `index` itself. Assert `emitted = [index]`, `completes = 1`. Also verifies `elementAt = take(skip(src, index), 1)` composition.
  - **#53 `windowcount-sub-nodes-per-chunk`** — `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `windowCount(s, N)`. First cached DATA lazily opens window 1 (via `openWindow`); for `N*M` source DATAs total, assert exactly M sub-nodes emitted, each with N DATAs and a COMPLETE, and the flattened sub-node sequence matches the source DATA order `[-1, 1, ..., N*M-1]`. First **compound-topology** invariant in the registry — my test's subscriber synchronously subscribes to each emitted sub-node so that the sub-node's producer fn activates (captures `winDown`) before `windowCount`'s source callback calls `winDown?.([[DATA, value]])`. That synchronous activation ordering is the key load-bearing protocol contract this invariant verifies.
  - **Imports added:** `elementAt`, `find`, `takeUntil`, `windowCount` from `extra/operators`.
  - **Verification:** 2117 TS tests pass (was 2113 — +4 invariants; 53 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean. Build green. 10/10 serial stability check. Total fast-check invariant count: 53 (up from 49). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Operator-layer sweep complete** (batches 12–19, 2026-04-24): the registry now has at least one contract invariant per shipped operator. Any future operator-layer invariants target specific bug classes (concurrency boundaries, lifecycle edge cases, compound-topology interactions) rather than the basic contract surface.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons:**
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (`replayBuffer`, `cleanupWitness`, `teardownWitness`, `terminatedBy`, `nonVacuousInvalidateCount`, `_pendingExtraSinks`). ~200 LOC runtime surgery on `NodeImpl` plus a stability story for the hooks. Separate session.
    - **Compound-topology / lifecycle-edge invariants** — candidates as bugs surface: `windowTime` (compound topology like `windowCount` but with setInterval — one more moving part), `bufferCount + completeOnPartialTail` (COMPLETE-side emit of a sub-N tail), `rescue + source resubscribable` (live-after-rescue), `switchMap + pause/resume interaction` (§32 state-mirror hazards under compound topology). Each is a focused ~50-100 LOC add and should be triggered by a concrete bug report, not authored speculatively.

title: Rigor-infra batch 18 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: combine / takeWhile / last / tap:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 18 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: combine / takeWhile / last / tap:**
  Follow-on to batch 17. Four more operator-layer invariants covering multi-dep snapshot, predicate termination, latest-on-terminal, and identity-with-side-effect. Deltas:
  - **#46 `combine-emits-latest-tuple-on-any-dep-settle`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` + `state<number>(100, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `combine(a, b)`. combine is `derived([a, b], vals => vals)` with a custom tuple-element equals. Initial subscribe produces one tuple `[0, 100]` (first-run gate `partial: false` default). Alternate `a.emit(i*10)` and `b.emit(1000+i)` for `steps ∈ 2..4`; each emit produces one new tuple tracking the freshest value per dep. Assert total count = `1 + 2*steps`. Catches: first-run gate missed, stale-value leak, duplicate emission.
  - **#47 `takewhile-terminates-on-first-non-matching`** — `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `takeWhile(s, v => v < 100)`. Cached DATA(0) matches. Emit `N - 1` more values `[1..N-1]` (all match). Then `s.emit(500)` (non-match) → one COMPLETE, zero new DATAs. Extras after termination ignored. Catches: non-matching value forwarded as DATA, missing COMPLETE on first non-match, double-COMPLETE, late DATA leak after `done`.
  - **#48 `last-emits-latest-on-complete`** — `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `last(s)`. Emit `values[1..]` silently. `s.down([[COMPLETE]])` fires the terminal branch: one DATA = `values[values.length - 1]`, one COMPLETE. Catches: premature emit, wrong-value, missing terminal emit, reversed DATA/COMPLETE order.
  - **#49 `tap-is-identity-with-side-effect`** — `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` + `tap(s, v => sideEffects.push(v), { equals: () => false })`. Both `emitted` and `sideEffects` must equal `values` exactly (same length, order, content). Catches: side-effect skip, double-invocation, value mutation, RESOLVED / DATA conflation.
  - **Imports added:** `combine`, `last`, `takeWhile`, `tap` from `extra/operators`.
  - **Verification:** 2113 TS tests pass (was 2109 — +4 invariants; 49 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean. Build green. 10/10 serial stability check. Total fast-check invariant count: 49 (up from 45). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (getting thin — at this point the operator-layer coverage is ~comprehensive over `src/extra/operators.ts` surface):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — residual: `takeUntil` (notifier-driven termination — variant of `takeWhile`), `find` / `elementAt` (position-gated single-emit — small semantics delta from `first`), `windowCount` / `windowTime` (sub-node emission + per-window COMPLETE — compound topology, each needs ~double the LOC of a flat invariant). After these, operator-layer coverage effectively closes — any further invariant targets specific bug classes (concurrency boundary, lifecycle edge case) rather than operator contracts.

title: Rigor-infra batch 17 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: reduce / merge / filter / map:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 17 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: reduce / merge / filter / map:**
  Follow-on to batch 16 covering accumulator-on-terminal, fan-in, predicate gating, and pure transform. Deltas:
  - **#42 `reduce-emits-once-on-complete`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `reduce(s, (acc, v) => acc + v, 0)`. Emit `values[1..]` silently at reduce's output (store.acc accumulates, no DATA). Then `s.down([[COMPLETE]])` fires the terminal branch: exactly one DATA = `sum(values)`, exactly one COMPLETE. Catches: premature emit, missing terminal emit, wrong accumulator, COMPLETE-before-DATA in the terminal wave.
  - **#43 `merge-forwards-data-from-all-sources`** — N sources (`N ∈ 2..4`) each `state<number>(-1-i, { equals: () => false })`. `merge(...sources)`. Initial subscribe delivers N cached DATAs in subscribe order (`[-1, -2, ..., -N]`). Then K rounds round-robin (`K ∈ 1..3`), each source emits `i * 1000 + k` (globally unique). Assert: total emitted count = `N + N*K`, emitted set matches the canonical union. Catches: inner-source drop, duplicate fan-out, subscribe-order miss, premature COMPLETE on one-source-terminal.
  - **#44 `filter-forwards-only-matching-predicate`** — `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` + `filter(s, v => v % 2 === 0, { equals: () => false })`. The **double-axis-disable** is load-bearing: without `{ equals: () => false }` on filter, `values = [2, 2]` collapses to one emit at filter's own output (Object.is absorption), conflating operator semantics with protocol-level absorption. With both disabled, downstream = `values.filter(even)` exactly. Catches: predicate false-positive / false-negative, batch-iteration bug, RESOLVED vs DATA conflation on non-matches.
  - **#45 `map-transforms-values-one-to-one`** — `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` + `map(s, v => v * 2 + 7, { equals: () => false })`. Project is injective; `equals: () => false` on map lets identical-project outputs pass when inputs repeat. Downstream = `values.map(project)` exactly. Catches: dropped emit, duplicated emit, batch-iteration bug, project not applied.
  - **Imports added:** `filter`, `map`, `merge`, `reduce` from `extra/operators`.
  - **Verification:** 2109 TS tests pass (was 2105 — +4 invariants; 45 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean. Build green. 10/10 serial stability check. Total fast-check invariant count: 45 (up from 41). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (even narrower now):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — residual candidates: `combine` / `combineLatest` (multi-dep snapshot), `takeWhile` / `takeUntil` (predicate / notifier termination), `last` (latest-on-complete + default), `find` / `elementAt` (position-gated single-emit), `tap` (side-effect-only passthrough), `windowCount` / `windowTime` (sub-node emission + per-window COMPLETE — compound topology, larger than a single invariant).

title: Rigor-infra batch 16 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: concat / take / skip / scan:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 16 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: concat / take / skip / scan:**
  Follow-on to batch 15. Four more operator-layer invariants covering phase-transition sequencing, termination by count, position-gated forwarding, and accumulator folding. Deltas:
  - **#38 `concat-phase-transition-a-completes-before-b`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` (a) + `state<number>(-2, { equals: () => false })` (b) wrapped in `concat(a, b)`. Concat subscribes secondSrc first (pending captures b's cached DATA(-2)), firstSrc second (a's cached DATA(-1) forwards immediately). Emit M into a (all forwarded), then K into b (all buffered in `pending`; output unchanged). `a.down([[COMPLETE]])` triggers phase=1 flush of pending. Assert: output = `[-1, 1..M, -2, 101..100+K]`. Catches: second-source leak into phase 0, pending-drop on COMPLETE, flush reordering, stuck-phase.
  - **#39 `take-terminates-after-n-datas`** — `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `take(s, N)` with `N ∈ 1..5`. Cached DATA(0) is the first take; emit N-1 more values. Assert `emitted = [0, 1, ..., N-1]` + exactly one COMPLETE. Emit `extras ∈ 1..3` more DATAs; none reach output, COMPLETE count stays 1. Catches: off-by-one in count, missing COMPLETE on threshold, late DATA leak after `done`, multiple COMPLETEs.
  - **#40 `skip-drops-first-n-datas`** — `state<number>(0, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `skip(s, N)` with `N ∈ 0..3`, `extras ∈ 1..5`. Total source DATAs = `1 + extras`. Assert downstream length = `max(0, 1 + extras - N)`, values = `[0, 1, ..., extras].slice(N)`. Catches: count mis-tracking, forwarding a skipped DATA, dropping a non-skipped DATA after the window closes.
  - **#41 `scan-folds-accumulator-per-data`** — sum reducer over `fc.array(fc.integer({ min: 1, max: 100 }), { minLength: 2, maxLength: 6 })`. **Surfaced a subtle contract nuance during implementation:** scan's `initial: seed` on its output node means subscribe delivers `DATA(seed)` FIRST, before the fn runs on the source's cached DATA. So the observed trace is `[seed, acc_1, acc_2, ...]`, not just the prefix sums. Documented inline in the expected-computation loop. Catches: seed not applied, accumulator reset across waves, skipped value in the fold, reversed chain. Also now serves as a cross-reference for the "operator's inner node can push a prefix DATA" gotcha future authors will hit when they add a new folding operator.
  - **Imports added:** `concat`, `scan`, `skip`, `take` from `extra/operators`.
  - **Verification:** 2105 TS tests pass (was 2101 — +4 invariants; 41 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean. Build green. 10/10 serial stability check on the property suite. **Fix-in-flight:** initial draft of #41 expected `[acc_1, acc_2, ...]`; fast-check counter-example `[1, 1]` flagged the missing seed-prefix within 1 test — another "the invariant just caught a test-design regression" moment. Total fast-check invariant count: 41 (up from 37). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (the list is getting short):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — remaining candidates: `reduce` (single-emit-on-COMPLETE sibling of `scan`), `merge` (all-inners-forward with interleaved ordering), `combine` / `combineLatest` (multi-dep snapshot contract), `takeWhile` / `takeUntil` / `first` / `last` (termination-point variants), `find` / `elementAt` (position-gated single-emit), `filter` / `map` / `tap` (pure-transform contracts — straightforward), `windowCount` / `windowTime` (sub-node emission + per-window COMPLETE — complex topology), `pausable` identity (currently a deprecated no-op; skip unless operator evolves).

title: Rigor-infra batch 15 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: buffer / bufferTime / interval / rescue:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 15 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: buffer / bufferTime / interval / rescue:**
  Follow-on to batch 14, continuing the operator-layer invariant sweep across notifier-driven windowing, time-driven windowing, periodic sources, and error-recovery. Deltas:
  - **#34 `buffer-notifier-flushes-accumulated`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` × 2 (src + notifier) wrapped in `buffer(src, notifier)`. Subscribe order: src first → cached DATA(-1) → buf=[-1]; notifier second → cached DATA(-1) triggers flush → emit([-1]). Then R × K shape: emit K distinct values into src (buf grows, no emission), tick notifier (flush K items as one array). Assert R+1 arrays emitted; each round-array has K sequential values at positions `[r*K, r*K+K-1]`. Catches: buffer-drop, flush-on-source (emit per source DATA), non-empty-gate missing (flushing `[]`), reset-miss (values leaking across rounds).
  - **#35 `buffertime-flushes-on-window-boundary`** — captured-push `producer<number>` (`equals: () => false`) wrapped in `bufferTime(src, 100)`. Under fake timers, `fc.array(fc.array(int, {minLength:1, maxLength:4}), {minLength:2, maxLength:4})` generates non-empty windows. For each window: push values, `advanceTimersByTime(100)` to fire the setInterval tick. Assert downstream trace is exactly `windows[i]` per position. Catches: window-boundary drift, empty-buffer emission, across-window leak.
  - **#36 `interval-emits-monotonic-counter-per-period`** — `interval(50)` under fake timers. `advanceTimersByTime(50 * N)` yields exactly N DATAs = `[0, 1, ..., N-1]`. Catches: counter initialization drift, double-fire per period, period-miss. (Counter values are monotonic distinct ints so `Object.is` equals-absorption at the interval node is harmless — no `equals: () => false` needed on this one.)
  - **#37 `rescue-replaces-error-with-value`** — `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `rescue(s, () => recoveryValue)` where `recoveryValue ∈ 100..999` (disjoint from initial -1, so the recovery DATA can't be absorbed by `rescue`'s own Object.is cache). Initial DATA(-1) passes. `src.down([[ERROR, errTag]])` → rescue's recover path fires → emits DATA(recoveryValue). Assert: trace is `[-1, recoveryValue]`, zero ERRORs, zero COMPLETEs forwarded to output. Catches: ERROR leak, recovery-fn not called, double-emission.
  - **Imports added:** `buffer`, `bufferTime`, `interval`, `rescue` from `extra/operators`.
  - **Verification:** 2101 TS tests pass (was 2097 — +4 invariants; 37 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean (biome reformatted once — long multi-arg `fc.array(fc.array(...))` wrapped), build green. 10/10 serial stability check on the property suite (no flakes — the batch-14 per-hop absorption lesson generalized: whenever the operator's own inner node could absorb duplicates of a recovery/re-emit value, the test's generator bounds or source equals config already disjoint the values). Total fast-check invariant count: 37 (up from 33). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (now narrower):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — remaining candidates: `windowCount` / `windowTime` (sub-node emission + per-window COMPLETE — more complex topology than plain `bufferCount` / `bufferTime`), `concat` (phase-transition contract — first source fully drains before second starts), `merge` (all-inners-forward with interleaved ordering), `combine` / `combineLatest` (multi-dep snapshot contract — emit only after all deps have settled), `take` / `takeUntil` / `first` / `last` (termination-point contracts), `skip` / `find` / `elementAt` (position-gated forwarding), `scan` / `reduce` (accumulator contract — seed + fold). The enumerable-contract-surface argument starts to pay off once these are filled in: an LLM composing a pipeline can look up each operator's invariant and know what the substrate guarantees without reading implementation.

title: Rigor-infra batch 14 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: sample / pairwise / bufferCount / delay:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 14 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: sample / pairwise / bufferCount / delay:**
  Follow-on to batch 13 continuing the systematic operator-layer sweep. Four new fast-check invariants covering notifier-sampling, adjacent-pair windowing, fixed-size chunking, and time-shift. Deltas:
  - **#30 `sample-emits-source-latest-on-notifier`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). `state<number>(-1)` (src) + `state<number>(-1)` (ticker) wrapped in `sample(src, ticker)`. Initial subscribe: sample subscribes src → callback captures `lastSourceValue = -1`; sample subscribes ticker → callback fires with cached DATA(-1) → emits `-1`. Then for each `i ∈ 1..N`, mutate src to `i * 100` and tick with `i`; output carries the latest src value positionally. Catches: held-value staleness, notifier DATA swallowed without emit, source-completed leaks.
  - **#31 `pairwise-emits-adjacent-tuples`** — `state<number>(values[0], { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `pairwise(s)`. Emit `values[1..]`; assert downstream is exactly `[(values[0], values[1]), (values[1], values[2]), …]`. The `{ equals: () => false }` is load-bearing: it lets consecutive identical values pass source-level absorption so pairwise's own dedup / windowing is what's under test. Catches: first-DATA tuple leak, prev-state corruption across waves, tuple-order flip.
  - **#32 `buffer-count-chunks-exactly-n`** — `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` wrapped in `bufferCount(s, N)`. Cached DATA(-1) on subscribe counts as the first source DATA. Emit `N*M - 1` more values; assert exactly M arrays of size N, concatenated trace equal to `[-1, 1, 2, …, N*M-1]`. Catches: chunk-size drift, chunk-boundary miss, partial-tail leak without a COMPLETE gate.
  - **#33 `delay-preserves-values-and-order`** — captured-push `producer<number>` configured with `{ equals: () => false }` so duplicate generated values don't get upstream-absorbed, **and** `delay(s, 100, { equals: () => false })` so delay's own node doesn't absorb the timer-fired duplicate re-emit. Under fake timers, fire N values at t=0. Assert no DATA at t=0 and at t=99. After `advanceTimersByTime(2)` (crossing the 100ms window), all N DATAs delivered in source-arrival order. Catches: timer-drop, out-of-order delivery, eager emission. **Fix-in-flight during implementation:** initial draft omitted the `equals: () => false` on both the producer AND the delay node; fast-check counter-examples `[1, 1]` surfaced the Object.is absorption twice — first at the source (~47 runs), then at the delay node itself (flaky ~16-62 runs) when the timer-fired re-emit hit the delay node's cache. Applying the axis-disable at both layers stabilized the property to 10/10 green across serial runs. Classic "the invariant just caught a test-design regression" — and highlights that each reactive hop is its own absorption checkpoint.
  - **Imports added:** `bufferCount`, `delay`, `pairwise`, `sample` from `extra/operators`. (All others already imported from batches 12–13.)
  - **Verification:** 2097 TS tests pass (was 2093 — +4 invariants; 33 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean (biome reformatted on first pass — multi-arg producer with options became multiline), build green (`assertBrowserSafeBundles` unaffected). Total fast-check invariant count: 33 (up from 29). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (smaller still than batch 13):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — remaining candidates: `buffer` (notifier-driven flush contract, complement to `bufferCount`), `bufferTime` / `windowTime` (time-window flush contracts), `windowCount` (sub-node emission + per-window COMPLETE), `interval` (regular time-driven emit count), `audit` (currently behaves debounce-like — ship an invariant only if/when the operator is fixed to RxJS semantics), `rescue` / `catchError` (ERROR-to-value mapping + live-after-rescue). Add as concrete bugs surface or when the per-operator coverage becomes worthwhile enumerable surface for the LLM contract.

title: Rigor-infra batch 13 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: timeout / throttle / zip / distinctUntilChanged:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 13 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: timeout / throttle / zip / distinctUntilChanged:**
  Follow-on to batch 12, continuing systematic operator-layer invariant coverage. Four new fast-check invariants extending the surface introduced in batch 9/10/12. No TLA+ analogues — all four operators are producer/derived compositions over `setTimeout` + `monotonicNs()` or synchronous queue-management, none of which corresponds to protocol-layer state. Deltas:
  - **#26 `timeout-errors-on-idle`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). Silent `producer<number>` (never emits) wrapped in `timeout(silent, ms)` with `ms ∈ 20..200`. Under `vi.useFakeTimers()`, `advanceTimersByTime(ms - 1)` — no terminal yet. `advanceTimersByTime(2)` — exactly one ERROR, no preceding DATA / COMPLETE. Catches: watchdog-timer off-by-one, double-ERROR (timer re-firing after emit), or spurious DATA/COMPLETE during the timeout cascade.
  - **#27 `throttle-leading-passes-first-suppresses-rest`** — captured-push `producer<number>` wrapped in `throttle(src, 100, { trailing: false })`. Under fake timers, emit each generated value (`2..5`) with a 5ms gap (all within the 100ms window). Exactly one DATA reaches downstream, equal to `values[0]`. Catches: leading-edge gate bypass, trailing-leak with `trailing: false`, or `lastEmitNs` reset inside the window.
  - **#28 `zip-strict-pair-per-position`** — `zip(state(0), state(100))` over `N ∈ 1..4`. Initial subscribe delivers `[0, 100]` (both cached DATAs queue, `tryEmit` pairs them). Emit N values into `a` — no new tuples (queue_b empty). Emit N values into `b` — exactly N new tuples, `emitted[k] === [k, 100 + k]` for k = 1..N. Catches: queue-bypass (emit-per-source without pairing), FIFO violation in `tryEmit`, or `active` counter divergence letting one source race past the other.
  - **#29 `distinct-until-changed-dedups-consecutive`** — `state<number>(-1, { equals: () => false })` so the source admits every emit as DATA (no upstream equals-absorption), wrapped in `distinctUntilChanged(s)`. Emit `2..8` generated values from the alphabet `{0, 1, 2, 3}` (small alphabet guarantees frequent consecutive duplicates). Assert downstream trace matches the canonical dedup of `[-1, ...values]`. Catches: dedup state corruption across waves, first-DATA bypass, or `equals` fn misapplication. Also proves the operator is load-bearing on top of node-level equals (source-level absorption disabled → dedup must happen at the operator).
  - **Imports added:** `producer` from `core/sugar`; `distinctUntilChanged`, `throttle`, `timeout`, `zip` from `extra/operators`. (`race` / `repeat` / `debounce` were already imported in batch 12.)
  - **Verification:** 2093 TS tests pass (was 2089 — +4 new invariants; 29 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean, build green (`assertBrowserSafeBundles` unaffected). Total fast-check invariant count: 29 (up from 25). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (narrower than batch 12):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (`replayBuffer`, `cleanupWitness`, `teardownWitness`, `terminatedBy`, `nonVacuousInvalidateCount`, batch-11's `_pendingExtraSinks` + cache-read ordering). Requires ~200 LOC runtime surgery on `NodeImpl`. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — remaining candidates: `audit` (currently debounce-like — ship if/when the operator is fixed to RxJS semantics), `sample` (notifier-driven latest-value sampling — tractable, no timer), `pairwise` (tuple-over-adjacent-pair contract), `buffer` / `bufferCount` / `bufferTime` (windowing size/reset contracts), `delay` (wave-delay count + value-preservation). Add as concrete bugs surface, per the batch-9 G pattern.

title: Rigor-infra batch 12 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: race / repeat / debounce:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 12 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion: race / repeat / debounce:**
  Follow-on to batch 11 QA, closing the "tractable incremental additions" bullet on the batch-11 remaining-follow-ons list. Three new fast-check invariants in the operator-layer surface area (no TLA+ analogues — race / repeat / debounce are all thin composition over `node` / `producer` and `setTimeout`, nothing to model at the protocol layer). Deltas:
  - **#23 `race-first-wins-only`** at [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts). Generates `2..5` sources each cached at a unique negative initial, wraps them in `race(...sources)`. Subscribe order makes `sources[0]` the winner (its cached DATA arrives first). Emits a uniquely-tagged sentinel `LOSER_TAG + idx` into every loser post-subscribe — none may reach the trace. Emits a `WINNER_TAG` DATA into `sources[0]` — MUST reach the trace. Catches: winner-lock regressions (e.g. `winner` reset on each batch, or losers re-entering after an unrelated event) and short-circuit bypass (a loser forwarding DATA despite `winner !== null`).
  - **#24 `repeat-count-respected`** — `state(0, { resubscribable: true })` wrapped in `repeat(s, N)` with `N ∈ 1..5`. Drives `N-1` COMPLETE signals via raw `s.down([[COMPLETE]])`; downstream MUST NOT have emitted COMPLETE yet. The N-th COMPLETE signal MUST produce exactly one downstream COMPLETE. Catches: off-by-one in `remaining`, premature downstream COMPLETE (forwarding the first inner COMPLETE), or over-repeat (resubscribing after the final COMPLETE).
  - **#25 `debounce-coalesces-rapid-emits`** — Under `vi.useFakeTimers()`, emits `2..6` generated values into `debounce(state, 50)` with a 10ms gap between each (< ms, so every new DATA resets the pending timer). No DATA may reach the trace during the burst. After `vi.advanceTimersByTime(60)` (crosses the post-last-emit quiet window at t+50), the trace MUST contain exactly one new DATA equal to the LAST emitted value. `try/finally` restores real timers — the toggle is per-property so the enclosing suite's other invariants stay on real timers. Catches: coalesce-bypass regressions (emit-per-input), timer-reset drops (failing to `clearTimeout` on each DATA), or pending-value staleness (flushing an earlier value instead of the latest).
  - **Imports added to `_invariants.ts`:** `race`, `repeat`, `debounce` from `extra/operators`; `vi` from `vitest`. (`vi` was not previously imported — acceptable since `_invariants.ts` already lives inside `src/__tests__/`.)
  - **Verification:** 2089 TS tests pass (was 2086 — +3 new invariants; 25 total in the property-based suite). Lint clean, build green (`assertBrowserSafeBundles` unaffected — test files never hit the universal-entry walker). Total fast-check invariant count: 25 (up from 22). Total TLC invariant count unchanged at 28.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (narrower than batch 11 QA):
    - **Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27** — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (`replayBuffer`, `cleanupWitness`, `teardownWitness`, `terminatedBy`, `nonVacuousInvalidateCount`, plus batch-11's `_pendingExtraSinks` + cache-read ordering for `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean`). Requires ~200 LOC runtime surgery on `NodeImpl` to expose internals plus a stability story for the hooks. Separate session.
    - **Additional operator-layer invariants** — next candidates if concrete bugs surface: `timeout` fires exactly once on idle window expiration; `audit` trailing-edge latches only one value per window; `throttle` leading/trailing interaction; `zip` strict-pairing across dep subscription order. Add as bugs surface, per the batch-9 G pattern.

title: Rigor-infra batch 11 QA (landed 2026-04-24) — zombie-batchActive fix + MaxEmits reservation + stale-code sweep:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 11 QA (landed 2026-04-24) — zombie-batchActive fix + MaxEmits reservation + stale-code sweep:**
  Adversarial review of batch 11 surfaced a latent correctness hole in the `batchActive` ghost lifecycle and a bounded-budget accounting issue, plus doc drift from the refactor. Deltas:
  - **F-A. `TerminatedImpliesBatchIdle` (#28) + five `batchActive` resets.** QA found that `Teardown(parent)` / `DeliverTeardown(p, c)` had no `batchActive.status = "idle"` guard and didn't clear the ghost on `status → "terminated"`; `Resubscribe(sid)` explicitly kept `batchActive` in UNCHANGED, so a resubscribed source could inherit a zombie `[status: "running", pending: <stale vs>]` from its prior lifecycle. Under the batch-11 Begin+Step state machine that zombie would let `BatchEmitStep(sid)` fire post-resubscribe and drain stale pending values into a freshly-reset trace — violating `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState`'s spirit. Currently unreachable in any shipped MC (no MC combines non-empty `BatchSeqs` with non-empty `MetaCompanions` on the same source), but a genuine modeling hole. Fix: (a) added `batchActive' = [... idle ...]` reset to `Terminate(src)`, `Teardown(parent)`, `DeliverTeardown(p, c)`, `DeliverTerminal(p, c)` gate=TRUE branch, and `Resubscribe(sid)`; (b) added `batchActive[...].status = "idle"` entry guards to `Teardown` + `DeliverTeardown` (mirroring the existing `Terminate` / `Emit` / `SinkNestedEmit` guards); (c) shipped new invariant `TerminatedImpliesBatchIdle` (#28) as the structural regression catcher: `\A n : status[n] = "terminated" => batchActive[n].status = "idle"`. Wired into all 17 cfg files.
  - **F-B. `emitCount` reservation moved to `BatchEmitBegin`.** QA found a latent starvation: `BatchEmitBegin` bound-checked `emitCount + Len(vs) <= MaxEmits` but didn't reserve; a concurrent `Emit(other-src, v)` between Begin and the final Step could consume a slot, leaving `BatchEmitStep` blocked forever on `emitCount < MaxEmits` and stranding the batch's last step with `batchActive[src].status = "running"`. Fix: move `emitCount' = emitCount + Len(vs)` to `BatchEmitBegin` (atomic reservation matching runtime's `batch()` quota claim). Removed per-step `emitCount' + 1` from `BatchEmitStep` — `emitCount` bound now enforced at Begin, not per step. `perSourceEmitCount` stays in Step because `EqualsFaithful` (#5) ties it to settlement delivery. No impact on MCs that passed before (state counts unchanged; the fix closes an unreachable path).
  - **F-C. Dead-helper sweep.** Six recursive helpers — `BuildSettleSeq`, `FinalCache`, `CountDataEmits`, `BuildSettleItemsRec`, `BuildBatchPendingItems`, `ApplyBatchToRing` — were originally called from the atomic `BatchEmitMulti`. Batch 11 replaced the atomic action with the Begin+Step state machine, which computes settle/cache/version per-step inline. The helpers have been dead since batch 11 I landed; removed in the QA pass (~75 LOC). `DirtySeqOf` stays (still used by `BatchEmitBegin`). `AppendToReplayBuffer` stays (used by `Emit` / `SinkNestedEmit` / `DeliverSettle` / `BatchEmitStep`).
  - **F-D. Stale-doc sweep.** Updated three stale references to the pre-refactor world: (a) `AllExtraPendingEmpty` docblock now lists `BatchEmitBegin` / `BatchEmitStep` instead of the vanished `BatchEmitMulti`; (b) the former `BatchEmitMulti` preamble comment now documents the Begin+Step split motivation and points at the per-action docstrings; (c) the `#21 — MultiSinkIterationDriftClean — DEFERRED` block (written pre-batch-11) replaced with a short `**RESOLVED batch 11 I**` pointer to `#27`; (d) the `AllExtraPendingEmpty` rationale comment at §2.4 now reads "ships as `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#27), batch 11 I" instead of "not shipped today."
  - **F-E. Batch-10 doc cross-ref.** Added an inline pointer to the batch-10 entry's "Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons" bullet that previously listed `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` as a `#21` deferral — clarifying that the invariant shipped as `#27` in batch 11 I and the `#21` number slot was retired.
  - **F-F. `EqualsPairs` purity note.** Added a paragraph to the `EqualsPairs` constant docstring explicitly stating the purity / determinism assumption and acknowledging that asymmetric / non-reflexive / non-transitive relations are permitted (no `ASSUME` enforces equivalence-relation laws). Stateful `equals` fns in the runtime are a user-facing footgun outside the TLA+ model's expressiveness.
