  - **Verification:** all 15 clean MCs green with state-space counts unchanged from batch 11 (base 105715, nested 273515, custom_equals 4359, etc.). `wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed. 2086 TS tests pass, lint clean, build green (`assertBrowserSafeBundles` passes). `TerminatedImpliesBatchIdle` (#28) is vacuous in every MC today (no MC terminates a source with an active batch), matching the "latent hole" classification — the invariant ships as a regression catcher for future MCs that cross these axes. Total TLC invariant count: 28 (up from 27); fast-check count unchanged at 22.
  - **Rejected findings** (from QA review): Pause/Resume/UpPause/UpResume/Invalidate-without-idle-guard (treated as acceptable modeling looseness — the runtime's `batch()` allows these ops mid-batch and the TLA+ interleaving stays conservative); `BatchEmitStep` lacks `NoDirtyAnywhere` (tier ordering preserved at dequeue via `DeliverSettle`'s gate; action-level looseness doesn't change downstream correctness). Both decisions documented in `archive/optimizations/resolved-decisions.jsonl` candidates for future cross-language-notes entries if PY port revisits.

title: Rigor-infra batch 11 (landed 2026-04-24) — `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor + `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#27):
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 11 (landed 2026-04-24) — `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor + `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#27):**
  Closes the single largest remaining rigor-infra follow-on — the atomic `BatchEmitMulti` action that had blocked the strict drift-catching invariant since batch 3. Deltas:
  - **Refactor.** Atomic `BatchEmitMulti(src, vs)` replaced by a two-action state machine:
    - `BatchEmitBegin(src, vs)` — enqueues the K-DIRTY tier-1 prefix to primary + extras at pre-batch cache, sets `batchActive[src] = [status: "running", pending: vs]`. Does NOT touch cache / version / counters.
    - `BatchEmitStep(src)` — processes one value from `batchActive[src].pending`, routes through `IsAbsorbed`, enqueues one settle to primary + extras (with post-emit cache snap), advances `cache` / `version` / `replayBuffer`, increments `emitCount` + `perSourceEmitCount`, decrements pending. Transitions back to "idle" on empty pending. Gated on `AllExtraPendingEmpty` + `NoInvalidateAnywhere` so each step's extra-sink items fully drain before the next step fires.
    - New ghost `batchActive: NodeIds -> [status: {"idle", "running"}, pending: Seq(Values)]` + initializer + TypeOK check + `batchActive` added to every other action's UNCHANGED clause (20 sites).
    - Guards on `Emit(src, v)`, `Terminate(src)`, `SinkNestedEmit(_, target, _)`: all require `batchActive[...].status = "idle"` — single-emit actions can't interleave with an active batch on the same source (matches runtime's "no re-entrant emit during in-flight batch()" semantic).
    - `Next` disjunction: `\E src, vs \in BatchSeqs : BatchEmitBegin(src, vs) \/ \E src : BatchEmitStep(src)` replaces the atomic `BatchEmitMulti`.
  - **New invariant `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#27).** `\A n, i, j : pendingExtraDelivery[n][i][j].msg.type = DATA => pendingExtraDelivery[n][i][j].msg.value = cache[n]`. Previously unshippable because atomic `BatchEmitMulti` left intermediate pending items stamped at non-final values while `cache[n]` jumped to the batch-final value. Under the Begin+Step refactor with inter-step drain gating, cache advances monotonically one step at a time and pending is drained before the next step — strict equality holds at every delivery boundary. Wired into all 17 cfg files (16 MCs + base). Load-bearing: reverting `BatchEmitStep`'s `AllExtraPendingEmpty` gate trips the invariant in `multisink_batch_MC` within ~6 steps (verified by temporary regression).
  - **Adjusted invariant: `BalancedWaves` (#2).** Added `NoActiveBatch` precondition alongside `AllQueuesEmpty /\ AllBuffersEmpty`. Reason: under the refactor, the transient state between `BatchEmitBegin` (K DIRTYs enqueued to trace) and all `BatchEmitStep`s (K settles enqueued) has `DIRTY count > settlement count` at the primary sink. Queues drain independently via `DeliverDirty`, so `AllQueuesEmpty` can be TRUE mid-batch — the old precondition was insufficient. Added helper `NoActiveBatch == \A n \in NodeIds : batchActive[n].status = "idle"` and ANDed it into the precondition. Invariant still fires on all the pre-refactor cases (no active batch → same behavior as before); the new precondition just waits out the batch window.
  - **State-space deltas** (baseline state counts that were `0` indicate no change; MCs with `BatchSeqs = {}` or `BatchSeqs` not exercising the step actions are unchanged):
    - base MC: 76984 → 105715 (+37%) — BatchSeqs triples interleave through the new step actions.
    - `nested_MC`: 123732 → 273515 (+121%) — BatchSeqs × SinkNestedEmit cross-interleaving. Biggest growth; still under 5s TLC runtime.
    - `equals_false_MC`: 2391 → 2833 (+18%).
    - `multisink_batch_MC`: 1322 → 1525 (+15%).
    - `custom_equals_MC`: 3409 → 4359 (+28%).
    - `replay_MC`: 2265 → 3005 (+33%).
    - Other MCs unchanged (no BatchSeqs, or BatchSeqs doesn't trigger the interleaving pressure).
    - Total sweep runtime still well under 30s across all 16 MCs.
  - **Verification:** all 15 clean MCs green + new invariant asserted on all 16 cfg files. `gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed (no false-positive from the new invariant). 2086 TS tests pass, lint clean.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (now narrower):
    - Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#27 — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks. `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` has no direct runtime analogue either; would need the runtime to expose its `_pendingExtraSinks` queue + cache-read ordering.
    - ~~Additional operator-layer invariants (race-first-wins, repeat-count-respected, debounce-coalesce)~~ **SHIPPED batch 12 (2026-04-24) as fast-check #23–#25.**

title: Rigor-infra batch 10 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion + batch-9 doc-drift cleanup:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 10 (landed 2026-04-24) — operator-layer fast-check expansion + batch-9 doc-drift cleanup:**
  Follow-on batch after 9: extends the operator-layer invariant surface with two more properties and closes out stale `EqualsAbsorbs` references that survived the batch-9 rename. Deltas:
  - **H1. `concatmap-serial-inner-subscription` (#21 fast-check).** concatMap's serialized-subscription contract: `project` is called for outer N+1 only after inner N completes. Catches queue-bypass regressions (project called eagerly on every outer emit) and FIFO violations (outer N+2 subscribes before N+1). Implementation: memoise inner `state` per outer value, count `project` calls, drive outer through a distinct sequence, then complete each inner in order and assert each completion triggers exactly one new `project` call for the next queued outer.
  - **H2. `mergemap-concurrency-bound-respected` (#22 fast-check).** With `{concurrent: K}`, at most K project calls are pending at once; excess outers queue until a prior inner completes. Property-tested across `K \in {1, 2}` with outer sequence length 3–5. Catches concurrency-bound bypass and drain-counter divergence. Fast-check registry now has 22 invariants (up from 20 in batch 9).
  - **J. Batch-9 doc-drift cleanup.** Seven `wave_protocol_*_MC.tla` files updated: `\* ReplayBufferSize = 0 and EqualsAbsorbs = TRUE by default` → `EqualsPairs = identity`. `wave_protocol_replay_MC.tla` docstring replaced its "EqualsAbsorbs deferred" aside with a current-state reference to `wave_protocol_equals_false_MC` + `wave_protocol_custom_equals_MC`. `wave_protocol_equals_false_MC.tla` purpose block rewritten to use the `EqualsPairs[n] = {}` vocabulary while preserving the Package 3 / batch 9 E historical breadcrumb trail. Errata added inline to the batch 6 F active-work entry flagging that the "parameterizes Emit / BatchEmitMulti / SinkNestedEmit / DeliverSettle" claim was actually inaccurate until batch 9 E closed the gap. Total: 9 files touched, no behavior changes.
  - **Verification:** 2086 TS tests pass (was 2084 — +2 new invariants; no fast-check flakes across 100 runs/invariant default). Lint clean. All TLC MCs green post-docstring edits (smoke-tested via `equals_false_MC` since only its docstring had the largest rewrite). TLA+ invariant count unchanged at 26; fast-check invariant count 22 (up from 20).
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (unchanged category-wise from batch 9):
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21 in TLC, distinct from fast-check #21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor. Separate batch if/when attempted. *(Updated 2026-04-24 batch 11 I: shipped as TLC invariant **#27** via the Begin+Step state-machine refactor; the `#21` number slot was retired in favor of `#27` in `wave_protocol.tla`.)*
    - Fast-check mirrors for TLC #18–#26 — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure. The protocol-layer runtime hooks needed (`replayBuffer`, `cleanupWitness`, `teardownWitness`, `terminatedBy`, `nonVacuousInvalidateCount`) don't exist in the TS runtime yet.
    - Additional operator-layer invariants (race, repeat, audit, etc.) — tractable incremental additions; add as specific bugs surface.

title: Rigor-infra batch 9 (landed 2026-04-24) — custom `EqualsPairs` relation + Package 7 stale-doc cleanup + operator-layer fast-check invariants:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 9 (landed 2026-04-24) — custom `EqualsPairs` relation + Package 7 stale-doc cleanup + operator-layer fast-check invariants:**
  Three-part batch closing the last explicit rigor-infra coverage gap (custom equals axis) + a doc cleanup that retires an already-shipped gap entry + a new fast-check surface area (operator-layer lifecycle). Deltas:
  - **E. `EqualsAbsorbs: NodeIds -> BOOLEAN` → `EqualsPairs: NodeIds -> SUBSET (Values \X Values)`.** The batch-3 boolean axis could only express all-absorb (identity equality) or never-absorb (`equals: () => false`); custom equality fns (e.g. "0 and 1 are interchangeable; 2 is distinct") were unexpressible. Generalization: a per-node relation over value pairs, with canonical encodings for the legacy cases (`{<<v, v>> : v \in Values}` = identity diagonal; `{}` = never; arbitrary subset = custom). New helper `IsAbsorbed(n, prev, next) == <<prev, next>> \in EqualsPairs[n]` threaded through every emission site: **Emit**, **BatchEmitMulti** (plus its `BuildSettleSeq` / `FinalCache` / `CountDataEmits` / `BuildSettleItemsRec` / `ApplyBatchToRing` helpers), **SinkNestedEmit**, **DeliverSettle**, and **DeliverTerminal**'s rescue-recompute branch. **Surfaced and closed a pre-existing modeling gap**: before batch 9, only `Emit` was gated by the axis — the other three sites used raw `=` so `BatchEmitMulti` / `SinkNestedEmit` / `DeliverSettle` silently bypassed `EqualsAbsorbs[n]` entirely, contradicting the batch-6 F entry's claim that all four were parameterized. The generalization closes this by routing every site through `IsAbsorbed`. New exercise MC `wave_protocol_custom_equals_MC` (2-node chain `A → B`, `Values = {0, 1, 2}`, `EqualsPairs[A] = identity ∪ {<<0,1>>, <<1,0>>}` so 0 and 1 are interchangeable at A but 2 is strictly distinct, `BatchSeqs = {<<0, 1>>, <<1, 0>>, <<2, 0>>}`). 3409 distinct states. Load-bearing: reverting `BuildSettleSeq`'s `IsAbsorbed` to raw `=` while leaving `FinalCache` / `CountDataEmits` intact trips `VersionPerChange` (#6) in custom_equals_MC because the bundle emits DATA where the relation dictated RESOLVED, but the version counter still reflected the relation's decision — verified by temporary regression.
  - **F. Package 7 stale-doc cleanup.** The "Remaining rigor-infra coverage gaps" list entry for §2.3 meta companion TEARDOWN was written pre-batch-3, but batch 3 (axis, witness, invariant) + batch 3 QA round 2 (full parent state transition) + batch 4 (`DeliverTeardown` cascade) collectively shipped the entire scope. The `Teardown(parent)` action's own docstring in [wave_protocol.tla](../../graphrefly/formal/wave_protocol.tla) also still said "MVP: does NOT perform the parent's own state transition" — stale since batch 3 QA round 2. Both updated: docstring now reflects the shipped cascade + hard-reset + `terminatedBy` classification; gap-list entry marked resolved with the batches-to-credit breakdown.
  - **G. Operator-layer fast-check invariants (3 new).** Fast-check-only, separate surface from protocol-layer invariants. Tests composed-behavior correctness of higher-order operators — lifecycle semantics of which inner sources contribute to downstream. Added to [src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts](../src/__tests__/properties/_invariants.ts):
    - **#18 `switchmap-stale-inner-suppressed`** — after switchMap supersedes to a new inner, emits into a prior (superseded) inner source never reach downstream. Memoises inner `state` per outer value so the test can reach in and emit a unique sentinel post-supersession. Catches the switchMap-inner teardown bug class flagged in the `refineExecutor` / `evalVerifier` hardening item.
    - **#19 `mergemap-all-live-inners-contribute`** — every DATA emitted into a live inner reaches downstream; no silent drops. Catches inner-subscription leaks.
    - **#20 `exhaustmap-drops-while-active`** — while the first outer's inner is still live, subsequent outer emits are dropped; `project` is only called once (for the first outer value). Catches gate-bypass regressions where the drop-while-active semantic is lost.
    Fast-check registry now has 20 invariants (up from 17); total test count 2084 (up from 2081).
  - **Verification:** all 15 clean MCs green with state-space counts unchanged from batch 8 (base 76984, nested 123732, up 159993, pause 22761, rescue 464, invalidate_diamond 2555, etc.) + new custom_equals_MC at 3409 states. `wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed. 2084 TS tests pass, lint clean. Total TLC invariant count: 26 (unchanged from batch 8 — E is a generalization, not a new invariant). Total fast-check invariant count: 20 (up from 17).

title: Rigor-infra batch 8 (landed 2026-04-24) — Package 5 rescue semantic invariant + INVALIDATE accounting law:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 8 (landed 2026-04-24) — Package 5 rescue semantic invariant + INVALIDATE accounting law:**
  Two TLA+-only extensions closing two of the four explicit "Remaining rigor-infra coverage gaps" items (§2.5 `completeWhenDepsComplete: false` / `errorWhenDepsError: false` Package-5 invariant, and a stricter Package-6 counting-law companion to `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel`). Deltas:
  - **A. `NoDepCascadeTerminalWhenGateFalse` (#25, §2.5 Package 5).** New ghost `terminatedBy: NodeIds -> {"none","self-source-terminate","self-teardown","teardown-cascade","dep-cascade-complete","dep-cascade-error"}`. Set at the four status→"terminated" transition sites: `Terminate` / `Teardown` use "self-*", `DeliverTeardown` uses "teardown-cascade", `DeliverTerminal`'s gate=TRUE branch classifies by `m.type` into "dep-cascade-complete" / "dep-cascade-error". `Resubscribe(sid)` clears the slot to "none" (fresh lifecycle). Invariant asserts: `terminatedBy[n] = "dep-cascade-complete" => AutoCompleteOnDepsComplete[n]` AND `terminatedBy[n] = "dep-cascade-error" => AutoErrorOnDepsError[n]`. Load-bearing in `wave_protocol_rescue_MC` (`AutoCompleteOnDepsComplete[B] = FALSE`): dropping the `IF gate` wrapper on `terminatedBy'` in `DeliverTerminal` trips the invariant at B because the gate-FALSE absorb path would then classify itself as `"dep-cascade-complete"`. Verified by temporary regression (reverted after confirmation). Vacuous in all MCs where both gate maps are TRUE everywhere (legacy baseline).
  - **D. `CleanupWitnessAccounting` (#26, §1.4 Package 6 counting law).** New ghost counter `nonVacuousInvalidateCount: NodeIds -> Nat` incremented by `Invalidate` / `DeliverInvalidate` only when `cache[n] # DefaultInitial` at action time (mirror of the same `wasReset` guard that gates the `cleanupWitness` append added in batch 5 B). `Resubscribe(sid)` zeroes the counter in lockstep with the existing `cleanupWitness[sid] = <<>>` clear. Invariant asserts `Len(cleanupWitness[n]) = nonVacuousInvalidateCount[n]` for every node. Complements `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel` (#24, value domain) with a quantitative law — off-by-N regressions that diverge the witness guard from the counter guard get caught. Load-bearing in `wave_protocol_invalidate_diamond_MC`: dropping the `wasReset` guard on the counter only (witness guard intact) produces `Len(cleanupWitness[D]) = 1 /\ nonVacuousInvalidateCount[D] = 2` at the diamond join node, tripping the equality. Verified by temporary regression.
  - **Verification:** all 14 clean MCs green, `wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed (new invariants do not false-positive against the substrate-faithful gap model). State-space counts unchanged from batches 5–7 — the new ghosts expand the state tuple but are either constant-valued (counter stays 0 in non-invalidate MCs) or take one value per terminated node (terminatedBy's domain is finite and most MCs terminate ≤ 1 node). Total TLC invariant count: 26 (was 24). `wave_protocol.cfg` + all 15 MC `.cfg` files updated in lockstep.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra coverage gaps** (down from 4 to 2):
    - **§2.5 `equals` variance — custom `EqualsFn`** (Package 3 deepening, still deferred). `EqualsAbsorbs: BOOLEAN` boolean axis shipped batch 3 + load-bearing MC batch 6; custom per-value-pair `EqualsFn: NodeIds -> (Value, Value) -> BOOLEAN` still deferred pending a consumer that needs deep-equality modeling.
    - **§2.3 meta companion TEARDOWN fan-out ordering** (Package 7, partial). `MetaTeardownObservedPreReset` (#22) shipped batch 3; full parent state-transition modeling still deferred because it would disturb `EqualsFaithful` / `VersionPerChange`.
    - *Closed by this batch:* §2.5 `completeWhenDepsComplete: false` / `errorWhenDepsError: false` (Package 5 invariant) via invariant A; §2.4 INVALIDATE counting-law companion via invariant D.
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (unchanged from batch 7):
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor.
    - Fast-check mirrors for #18–#26 — most still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (no runtime counterparts for `replayBuffer` / `cleanupWitness` / `teardownWitness` / `replaySnapshot` / `terminatedBy` / `nonVacuousInvalidateCount`).
    - Operator-layer invariants (switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, exhaustMap) — fast-check-only, separate surface area.
  - **TS runtime parity:** the `terminatedBy` classification and `nonVacuousInvalidateCount` accounting are pure TLA+ ghost state; no runtime analogues exist (or need to exist) because the behaviors they gate — `DeliverTerminal` gate branches and the `wasReset` INVALIDATE witness skip — both already ship in the runtime (batch 5 B) and are covered by existing unit tests (`lifecycle.test.ts` diamond fan-in regression).

title: Rigor-infra batches 5–7 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23) — runtime fix + invariant rename + defensive hardening + caveat docs:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batches 5–7 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23) — runtime fix + invariant rename + defensive hardening + caveat docs:**
  Adversarial review of batches 5–7 surfaced one real runtime bug (batch 5 B parity, fixed same session — see entry below) plus a handful of smaller improvements. Deltas:
  - **Runtime fix (B-runtime, same day).** See the batch-5 entry below for the full description. Guard added at [src/core/node.ts](../src/core/node.ts) `_onDepMessage`'s INVALIDATE branch + regression test at [src/__tests__/core/lifecycle.test.ts](../src/__tests__/core/lifecycle.test.ts) verified load-bearing.
  - **BH-3 rename.** Renamed `CleanupWitnessNonTrivial` → `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel` in [wave_protocol.tla](../../graphrefly/formal/wave_protocol.tla) + all 15 `.cfg` files + README + optimizations.md. The prior name implied a stronger semantic than the invariant actually checks; the new name honestly reflects "no witness entry equals `DefaultInitial`." Docstring updated.
  - **BH-4 + BH-5 defensive hardening.** Hoisted `baseLen >= 1` to the top of `StartHandshakeValid`'s conjunction (so downstream `\E i \in 1..baseLen` quantifiers never range over non-positive). Added explicit `baseLen >= 0` + per-index `baseLen + k <= hLen` guards to `LateSubscriberReceivesReplay` so pathological states with oversized `replaySnapshot` fail-fast instead of evaluating vacuously.
  - **BH-6 + ECH-7 comments.** Added rationale to `SubscribeSink`'s `replayVals` guard explaining why the literal "terminated-resubscribable" fix BH-6 suggested would introduce a `TerminalAbsorbing` (#3) violation (replay DATA after COMPLETE) and is intentionally not applied; noted that the `LateSubscriberReceivesReplay` vacuous-on-terminated branch is a feature, not a bug.
  - **BH-7 + ECH-9 comments.** Added scope notes to `ObserverCallbackActive` documenting the atomic-trace-append assumption and confirming compound-batch (`<<DIRTY, DATA, DIRTY, DATA>>`) firings are correctly explored by TLC's action interleaving.
  - **Option B caveat (BH-2/ECH-2) on TS runtime.** The `_cached === undefined` guard overloads "cache was reset this wave" with "cache was never populated." For a mid-chain derived that received DIRTY but not DATA yet (or a node with no `initial`), a first-time INVALIDATE arrival is silently elided. Accepted as a design simplification (per user: "invalidate and undefined are almost equal in real life") rather than introducing a per-wave `_invalidatedThisWave` flag. Documented at [src/core/node.ts](../src/core/node.ts) in the INVALIDATE branch so future readers aren't surprised.
  - **ECH-8 cross-reference.** `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel`'s docstring now explicitly notes the "emit-default-then-invalidate" caveat is TLA+-model-only — the TS runtime rejects `DATA(undefined)` at the `_emit` boundary per spec §1.2, so no runtime analogue exists.
  - **Verification:** all 15 clean MCs green (unchanged state-space counts — renames + comments don't affect state space), gap MC still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed. 2081 TS tests pass, lint clean, build green (assertBrowserSafeBundles unaffected).
  - **Rejected (false positives):** BH-1 `_dirtyDepCount` leak (incorrect analysis — counter is cleared by future DATA/RESOLVED/terminal arrivals); ECH-3 cleanup-hook-before-cache-reset (confirmed safe); ECH-6 inactive-node INVALIDATE (not a bug); ECH-10 `resetOnTeardown: false` + INVALIDATE (pre-existing, not caused by this change).
  - **Deferred:** BH-8/BH-9/BH-10 test hardening suggestions (low ROI); ECH-4/ECH-5 observability semantic change (1 INVALIDATE instead of N per diamond) — matches the TLA+ model; a §1.4 spec amendment codifying "INVALIDATE coalesced per-wave per-node" is tracked as a separate item below.

title: Rigor-infra batch 7 (landed 2026-04-23) — `SinkNestedEmit` tightened to the §32 callback-active window:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 7 (landed 2026-04-23) — `SinkNestedEmit` tightened to the §32 callback-active window:**
  Single focused TLA+-only refinement per the 2026-04-23 nested-drain strategy update's top-priority item. Delta:
  - **H. `ObserverHasReceivedData` → `ObserverCallbackActive`.** `SinkNestedEmit(observer, target, v)` previously fired any time observer's trace contained a DATA — including long after the sink callback completed. Tightened to require `trace[observer][Len(...)].type = DATA` (the LAST trace entry is DATA), matching the §32 bug semantic: the nested `batch(() => target.emit(v))` call happens INSIDE the sink callback that the DATA delivery just invoked. Under the old loose guard, TLC also fired the action at arbitrary later states that don't correspond to any JS runtime scenario. Effect on `wave_protocol_nested_MC`: 125216 → 123732 distinct states (~1.2% shrink) — the action still fires meaningfully (nested-wave × outer-wave interleaving coverage preserved), but only in the legitimate window.

Confirms the protocol-layer tier ordering (`NoDirtyAnywhere` gate on `DeliverSettle`) is sound even within the tightened §32 window — the simple-topology model is clean. Compound-topology repros (switchMap resubscribe) remain tracked as a separate operator-layer surface.

title: Rigor-infra batch 6 (landed 2026-04-23) — §2.5 `EqualsAbsorbs = FALSE` exercise MC + `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState` extended to cover `replaySnapshot` clear:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 6 (landed 2026-04-23) — §2.5 `EqualsAbsorbs = FALSE` exercise MC + `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState` extended to cover `replaySnapshot` clear:**
  Two small, self-contained TLA+ additions that each make a previously-vacuous axis load-bearing. Deltas:
  - **F. Dedicated `EqualsAbsorbs = FALSE` exercise MC.** Batch 3 Package 3 shipped the `EqualsAbsorbs: NodeIds -> BOOLEAN` constant **claimed to parameterize** `Emit` / `BatchEmitMulti` / `SinkNestedEmit` / `DeliverSettle` equality checks, but no existing MC flipped the axis off (`replay_MC` explicitly notes "left TRUE to keep RESOLVED-absorbing semantics normal; equality variance MC deferred"). The whole `equals: () => false` code path was therefore unexercised. New MC `wave_protocol_equals_false_MC` (2-node chain `A → B`, `EqualsAbsorbs[A] = FALSE`, `BatchSeqs = {<<0,0>>, <<1,1>>}` to include same-value repeats) forces every emit at A to produce DATA (never RESOLVED). `EqualsFaithful` (#5) is now load-bearing via the DATA path — a regression that ignores the `EqualsAbsorbs[n]` flag would produce a RESOLVED where DATA is contractually required and trip the invariant. 2391 distinct states, < 1s runtime. **Errata (batch 10, 2026-04-24):** the batch-3 claim that Package 3 parameterized all four emission sites was inaccurate — only `Emit` was actually gated by `EqualsAbsorbs`; the other three sites (`BatchEmitMulti`, `SinkNestedEmit`, `DeliverSettle`) used raw `=` until batch 9 E's `IsAbsorbed` threading closed the gap. Under this MC specifically the inaccuracy was invisible — `BatchSeqs` exercise `BatchEmitMulti`'s `Emit`-like settle path via the recursive helpers, but with value alphabet `{0, 1}` and empty `EqualsPairs[A]` the cache advances on every emit regardless of whether absorption was gated per-site or only at the top-level `Emit`. Batch 9 E discovered the gap while generalizing to `EqualsPairs` and shipped the fix; the batch 6 claim was retroactively correct once batch 9 landed.
  - **G. `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState` (#13) extended with `replaySnapshot[sid] = <<>>`.** Batch 5 A added the `replaySnapshot' = <<>>` clear to `Resubscribe` but the structural invariant didn't check it — a future refactor dropping the clear would silently regress without tripping any MC. Added the check in lockstep with the existing `cleanupWitness` / `teardownWitness` / `replayBuffer` post-resubscribe clauses (batch 3 QA round 2 item 3 pattern). The existing `wave_protocol_resubscribe_MC` has `ReplayBufferSize = 0` everywhere so the check is vacuous there — added new `wave_protocol_replay_resubscribe_MC` (2-node chain `A → B`, `ReplayBufferSize[B] = 1`, `ResubscribableNodes = {B}`) to cross-cut the two axes so pre-resubscribe `replaySnapshot[B]` can be non-empty. 550 distinct states, < 1s runtime. Verified load-bearing: reverting the `Resubscribe` clear on `replaySnapshot` trips the invariant in ~8 steps.
  - **Verification:** all 15 MCs green, `wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed. Existing MC state-space counts unchanged (both new axes default off in legacy MCs).
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (none blocking; `activeSinkDelivery` closed in batch 7):
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor.
    - Fast-check mirrors for #18–#24 — blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (runtime features for `replayBuffer` / `cleanupWitness` / `teardownWitness` / `replaySnapshot` don't exist yet).
    - Operator-layer invariants (switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, exhaustMap) — fast-check-only, separate surface area.

title: Rigor-infra batch 5 (landed 2026-04-23) — §2.5 replayBuffer consumption side + §1.4 diamond-`DeliverInvalidate` witness guard:
body:
- **Rigor-infra batch 5 (landed 2026-04-23) — §2.5 replayBuffer consumption side + §1.4 diamond-`DeliverInvalidate` witness guard:**
  Two TLA+-only extensions closing one explicit "Remaining rigor-infra coverage gaps" item and one batch-4 QA deferred latent bug. Deltas:
  - **A. §2.5 replayBuffer consumption side.** `SubscribeSink(sid)` now appends the contents of `replayBuffer[sid]` as DATA messages after the base handshake (non-terminated sinks only). New ghost `replaySnapshot: NodeIds -> Seq(Values)` captures the buffer at subscribe time; `Resubscribe(sid)` clears it alongside the other per-sid lifecycle resets. New invariant **#23 `LateSubscriberReceivesReplay`** verifies the handshake tail matches the snapshot positionally: for every activated sink, the last `Len(replaySnapshot[sid])` handshake entries are DATA messages carrying exactly the captured values in ring order. `StartHandshakeValid` (#7) was extended with a `baseLen = Len(H) - Len(replaySnapshot[sid])` decomposition so its shape check runs over the base-handshake prefix and the replay tail is validated separately. Exercised actively in `wave_protocol_replay_MC` — state space grew from ~1.9K → ~2.3K distinct states as TLC explores interleavings where `Emit` / `BatchEmitMulti` fire before `SubscribeSink(A)` (the new snapshot ghost captures distinct ring contents per ordering).
  - **B. Diamond `DeliverInvalidate` witness guard.** Both `Invalidate(n)` and `DeliverInvalidate(p, c)` now skip the `cleanupWitness` append when `cache[n] = DefaultInitial` / `cache[c] = DefaultInitial` — the cache is already at the reset sentinel, so a second cascade arrival at a fan-in node no longer appends a spurious sentinel witness. Mirrors the runtime's `cleanupHook` no-op contract on already-reset `prevData`. New invariant **#24 `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel`**: every witness entry is non-default. Closes the batch-4 QA deferred finding (`docs/optimizations.md` "Remaining rigor-infra coverage gaps" → diamond-topology `DeliverInvalidate` post-reset witness). New MC `wave_protocol_invalidate_diamond_MC` (4-node diamond `A → {B, C} → D`, `InvalidateOriginators = {A}`, `MaxInvalidates = 1`) makes the guard load-bearing — 2555 distinct states, < 1s runtime. Verified: reverting the guard on `DeliverInvalidate` immediately trips `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel` with a concrete trace; restoring the guard returns the MC to green.
  - **B-runtime (landed 2026-04-23, same day).** The TLA+ modeling exposed a real latent bug in the TS runtime: `_onDepMessage`'s INVALIDATE branch at [src/core/node.ts:1455](../src/core/node.ts:1455) unconditionally fired `_emit(INVALIDATE_ONLY_BATCH)` on every arrival. In a diamond `A → {B, C} → D` with `graph.invalidate("A")`, D received INVALIDATE from both paths — the second arrival's `_emit` walk re-fired the object-form `invalidate` cleanup hook and re-broadcast INVALIDATE to D's children (cascading amplification through deeper subgraphs). Live consumer: [patterns/ai/prompts/frozen-context.ts:81](../src/patterns/ai/prompts/frozen-context.ts:81) uses `{ invalidate }` on a single-dep chain today (unaffected), but any future diamond-fan-in use of the hook would have silently misfired. **Fix:** guard the `_emit` call with `if (this._cached === undefined) return` — skip cleanup + forwarding when the cache was already reset. Matches the TLA+ guard exactly. Regression test `diamond fan-in: INVALIDATE cascade fires invalidate hook ONCE at the join` at [src/__tests__/core/lifecycle.test.ts](../src/__tests__/core/lifecycle.test.ts) (topology `A → {B, C} → D → E`, asserts `invalidates === 1` at D AND `invalidatesAtE === 1` at the downstream child). Verified load-bearing by reverting the guard → test fails with `expected 2 to be 1`; restoring → 2081 tests green. PY parity deferred per standing policy.
  - **Verification:** all 14 MCs ran post-batch-5 — 13 green, 1 expected failure (`wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean`, intentional regression guard for the substrate fix landed in batch 2). State-space deltas vs. batch-4:
    - `wave_protocol_MC`: 77K → 77K (unchanged — new ghost stays empty when `ReplayBufferSize = 0`).
    - `wave_protocol_replay_MC`: 1912 → 2265 (+353, from `replaySnapshot` capture).
    - Other MCs: unchanged distinct-state counts.
    - `wave_protocol_invalidate_diamond_MC`: 2555 distinct states (new).
  - **Remaining rigor-infra follow-ons** (smaller scope than prior "next session" items):
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor.
    - `activeSinkDelivery` context tightening for `SinkNestedEmit` (Option C in the batch-5 recommendation) — optional next-session item (~30 LOC TLA+). Would tighten the §32 nested-drain model so the action fires only mid-`_deliverToSinks`-iteration.
    - Fast-check mirrors for #18–#24 — still blocked on runtime ghost-state exposure hooks (`replayBuffer`, `cleanupWitness`, `teardownWitness`, `replaySnapshot`). Runtime features don't exist yet in TS for §2.5 `replayBuffer` + §1.4 `INVALIDATE` + §2.3 meta companions.
    - Operator-layer invariants (switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, exhaustMap) — fast-check-only, separate surface area.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 4 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23):
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 4 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23):**
  Adversarial review surfaced 5 patchable findings + 1 deferred latent. All 5 fixes applied. Deltas:
  - **F1 (CRITICAL fix):** silent state-space prune via deadlock between DATA and INVALIDATE in the same queue. Repro: `Emit(A, v)` enqueues `[DIRTY, DATA(v)]`; `DeliverDirty` consumes DIRTY; `Invalidate(A)` appends INVALIDATE → queue = `[DATA(v), INVALIDATE]`. `DeliverSettle` blocked by `NoInvalidateAnywhere`; `DeliverInvalidate` requires head = INVALIDATE but head is DATA — deadlock with `CHECK_DEADLOCK FALSE` hiding it. **Fix:** added `NoInvalidateAnywhere` conjunct to `Emit` / `BatchEmitMulti` / `SinkNestedEmit`. Tier-3 cannot be enqueued while tier-1 is pending. Matches spec §1.4 "tier-1 drains globally before tier-3" both producer- and consumer-side.
  - **F2 (MAJOR fix):** `Teardown(parent)` was gated on `MetaCompanions[parent] # {}`, contradicting spec §1.4/§2.6 which treats TEARDOWN as a lifecycle primitive independent of meta. Runtime doesn't require meta either. Fix: dropped the gate. `MaxTeardowns` still bounds state space; witness mapping no-ops for parents with no meta.
  - **BH-Gate (MAJOR fix):** `Invalidate` / `DeliverInvalidate` / `Teardown` / `DeliverTeardown` all mutate `queues` but were ungated on `AllExtraPendingEmpty`. Inconsistent with other queue-writing actions (Terminate, DeliverTerminal etc. all gated). Added the gate to all four.
  - **F5 (MINOR fix):** `Teardown` and `DeliverTeardown` now clear `dirtyMask` at the torn-down node. Mirrors runtime's `_deactivate` on TEARDOWN. Prevents stale mask entries on terminated nodes.
  - **Docblock update:** `AllExtraPendingEmpty` exemption list corrected — INVALIDATE/TEARDOWN actions are now gated (moved from Exempt to Gated). Tier-drain predicates (`NoDirtyAnywhere`, `NoInvalidateAnywhere`, `NoSettleAnywhere`) moved earlier in the file so emit-side actions can reference them (TLA+ requires operators defined before use).
  - ~~**Deferred:** diamond-topology `DeliverInvalidate` post-reset witness~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-23 in batch 5 B.** Both `Invalidate(n)` and `DeliverInvalidate(p, c)` now guard the witness append on `cache # DefaultInitial` — a second cascade arrival at an already-reset fan-in node skips the append. New invariant `CleanupWitnessNotSentinel` + new MC `wave_protocol_invalidate_diamond_MC` (4-node diamond `A → {B, C} → D`) make the guard load-bearing.
  - **Verification post-fix:** all 13 MCs green. Expected state-space shifts: `invalidate_MC` 1815→1671 distinct states (F1 eliminated invalid interleavings), `meta_teardown_MC` 1356→2832 (F2 lets Teardown fire at any node, not just meta-parents). Baseline MCs unchanged. 2080 TS tests pass, lint + build clean.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 4 (landed 2026-04-23) — full INVALIDATE + TEARDOWN cascades + tier-1 ordering:
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 4 (landed 2026-04-23) — full INVALIDATE + TEARDOWN cascades + tier-1 ordering:**
  Next-batch push after the batch 3 QA round 2. Deltas:
  - **A. Full INVALIDATE cascade:** `DeliverInvalidate(p, c)` now forwards INVALIDATE to every child of c (previously one-step only). Full subgraph cache-bust reachable from any `InvalidateOriginators` node. Bounded by graph depth × `MaxInvalidates`.
  - **B. Tier-1 ordering — `NoInvalidateAnywhere`:** new guard added to `DeliverSettle` + `DeliverTerminal`. INVALIDATE (tier-1 per spec §1.4) must drain globally before tier-3 (DATA/RESOLVED) or tier-4 (COMPLETE/ERROR) delivery. Vacuous when `InvalidateOriginators = {}` (baseline MCs). In `invalidate_MC`: state space dropped from 2139 → 1815 distinct states — the guard eliminates the invalid interleavings.
  - **C. Full TEARDOWN cascade + `DeliverTeardown`:** `Teardown(parent)` now also enqueues TEARDOWN to DAG children via `queues`. New action `DeliverTeardown(p, c)` consumes TEARDOWN, records witness at c's meta companions with pre-reset cache/status, transitions c to "terminated", clears locks/buffer/upQueues, forwards TEARDOWN to c's DAG children. `MetaTeardownObservedPreReset` now exercised uniformly across the reached subgraph, not just origin-only.
  - **D. Extended exercise MCs:** both `invalidate_MC` and `meta_teardown_MC` promoted from 2-node to 3-node / 4-node topologies to actually walk the cascade:
    - `invalidate_MC`: A → B → C chain, `Invalidate(A)` cascades through both hops. 1815 distinct states.
    - `meta_teardown_MC`: A → B chain with `MetaCompanions[A] = {M}` AND `MetaCompanions[B] = {N}`. Teardown(A) cascades through `DeliverTeardown(A, B)`, exercising BOTH M and N witnesses. 1356 distinct states.
  - **Verification:** all 13 MCs green, 2080 TS tests pass, lint + build clean. Baseline MC state-space counts unchanged (new actions/guards are gated off on `InvalidateOriginators = {}` / `MetaCompanions = [n |-> {}]`). Total state space: ~420K distinct states across 13 MCs in ~40s.
  - **Remaining follow-ons** (fewer now):
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor.
    - Tier-5 ordering for TEARDOWN (`NoTeardownAnywhere` guards on DeliverSettle/DeliverTerminal) — deferred; runtime behavior is that TEARDOWN fires synchronously in `_emit`, so there's no "tier-5 in queue while tier-3 drains" scenario to guard. Action-level interleaving in TLC covers the legitimate cases.
    - Operator-layer invariants (switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, exhaustMap) — fast-check-only, separate surface area.
    - Fast-check mirrors for #18–#22 — requires runtime ghost-state exposure hooks.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 3 QA round 2 extended (landed 2026-04-23) — `replayBuffer` full threading + full `Invalidate`/`Teardown` + extended `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState` + 4 dedicated exercise MCs:
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 3 QA round 2 extended (landed 2026-04-23) — `replayBuffer` full threading + full `Invalidate`/`Teardown` + extended `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState` + 4 dedicated exercise MCs:**
  Follow-on push after the QA fixes. Deltas:
  - **Item 1 (`replayBuffer` full threading):** `Emit` was the only site appending to the ring. Extended to `BatchEmitMulti` (new `ApplyBatchToRing` recursive helper — iterates vs, appends each DATA-producing emit, skips RESOLVED), `DeliverSettle` (append on derived DATA emissions), `SinkNestedEmit` (append on nested source DATA). The ring now reflects last-N DATA at ANY node, not just source-side `Emit`.
  - **Item 5 (full `Invalidate`):** prior MVP was witness-only. Now models the full §1.4 one-step contract: `Invalidate(n)` records pre-reset `cache[n]` to `cleanupWitness[n]`, resets cache to `DefaultInitial`, enqueues INVALIDATE to every child. New action `DeliverInvalidate(p, c)` consumes the INVALIDATE head, records pre-reset `cache[c]`, resets cache[c]. One-step cascade (doesn't forward to grandchildren) — keeps state bounded; full cascade tracked as a follow-on.
  - **Item 6 (full `Teardown`):** prior MVP was witness-only. Now transitions parent status to "terminated" AFTER witness recording, clears `pauseLocks[parent]`/`pauseBuffer[parent]`, clears outbound `upQueues[<<parent, _>>]`. Mirrors `Terminate`'s hard-reset semantic per §2.6 "Teardown". Cache preserved (TEARDOWN is lifecycle disposal; cache bust is INVALIDATE's job).
  - **Item 3 (extended `ResubscribeYieldsCleanState`):** invariant #13 now also checks `cleanupWitness[sid] = <<>>`, `teardownWitness[sid] = <<>>`, `replayBuffer[sid] = <<>>` post-resubscribe. Locks in the A4 fix structurally — any future refactor dropping those resets will trip TLC with a concrete counter-example.
  - **Item 2 (4 exercise MCs):** each package axis now has a dedicated MC making its invariant load-bearing instead of vacuous:
    - `wave_protocol_rescue_MC` (464 states) — `AutoCompleteOnDepsComplete[B] = FALSE` exercises Package 5 + D1 rescue recompute.
    - `wave_protocol_meta_teardown_MC` (350 states) — `MetaCompanions[A] = {M}` exercises Package 7 + `MetaTeardownObservedPreReset`.
    - `wave_protocol_replay_MC` (1912 states) — `ReplayBufferSize[A] = 2` + `BatchSeqs = {<<1, 0>>}` exercises Package 3 + ring drop-oldest + all 4 emission sites.
    - `wave_protocol_invalidate_MC` (2139 states) — `InvalidateOriginators = {A}` exercises Package 6 + `DeliverInvalidate` one-hop cascade.
  - **Verification:** all 13 MCs green, 2080 TS tests pass, lint + build clean. Baseline MCs' state-space counts unchanged (new actions are action-level disjuncts in `Next`, gated off in baseline MCs → no state explosion). Total state space: ~420K distinct states across 13 MCs in ~40s.
  - **Remaining follow-ons** (none blocking; each is its own focused session):
    - Full INVALIDATE cascade via `DeliverInvalidate` forwarding to grandchildren (requires tier ordering adjustment — INVALIDATE interleaving with DATA/RESOLVED).
    - `DeliverTeardown(p, c)` for DAG-child TEARDOWN propagation + tier-5 prefix draining.
    - `MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` (#21) — still blocked on `BatchEmitMulti` step-action refactor.
    - Operator-layer invariants (switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, exhaustMap) — fast-check-only.
    - Fast-check mirrors for #18–#22.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 3 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23):
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 3 QA pass (landed 2026-04-23):**
  Adversarial review surfaced 5 findings; 4 auto-fixed, 1 architectural addition with user approval. Deltas:
  - **A1 (CRITICAL fix):** `Emit` action had `replayBuffer' = IF ... AppendToReplayBuffer ...` AND `replayBuffer` in its UNCHANGED list — a silent contradiction when `ReplayBufferSize[src] > 0`. Vacuous in all current MCs (which have size = 0), but the entire Package 3 axis would have been unreachable if any consumer enabled it. Removed `replayBuffer,` from Emit's UNCHANGED list at [wave_protocol.tla:529-534](../../graphrefly/formal/wave_protocol.tla).
  - **A2 (MAJOR fix):** `DeliverUp`'s bufferAll-drain branches enqueue to `pendingExtraDelivery` (like other emission actions), but was NOT gated on `AllExtraPendingEmpty`. Added the gate and expanded the gate docblock to enumerate ALL exempt actions (`DeliverToExtraSink`, `SinkNestedEmit`, `Invalidate`, `Teardown`, `UpPause`, `UpResume`, `SubscribeSink`, `Resubscribe`) with reasons.
  - **A3 (cleanup):** Deleted dead helper `BuildBatchSettlePendingItems` — the captured-bufferAll path diverts settles to `pauseBuffer`, so the settle-only variant is never needed.
  - **A4 (consistency fix):** `Resubscribe(sid)` was clearing handshake/trace/extraSinkTrace/pendingExtraDelivery per-sid but NOT `cleanupWitness[sid]`, `teardownWitness[sid]`, `replayBuffer[sid]`. Added per-sid resets for all three — a resubscribed lifecycle now starts with empty witness history + empty ring buffer, matching the pauseLocks/pauseBuffer/handshake/trace reset semantics.
  - **D1 (architectural — option (a)):** When `AutoCompleteOnDepsComplete[c] = FALSE` absorbs the LAST dirty dep's terminal (i.e. `dirtyMask[c] \ {p} = {}` and `status[c] = "dirty"`), `DeliverTerminal` now performs a full recompute + settle — mirroring `DeliverSettle`'s all-settled branch (Compute(c, cache), emit DATA or RESOLVED, update cache/version/status). Models rescue/catchError's "emit recovery value on dep terminal" semantic. Previously the node sat in "dirty" with an empty mask and never re-emitted — a latent bug that would have surfaced the moment anyone exercised Package 5. Axis is still defaulted disabled in all MCs; ships ready for consumers.
  - **Verification post-fix:** all 9 TLC MCs still green (same state-space counts — the fixes touch only defaulted-disabled paths), 2080 TS tests pass, lint clean, build green (no `assertBrowserSafeBundles` violations).
  - **Deferred / non-findings:**
    - `replayBuffer` not updated in BatchEmitMulti/DeliverSettle/SinkNestedEmit — acknowledged MVP limitation, already tracked as a follow-on.
    - `AllExtraPendingEmpty` as a global (not node-local) gate — documented modeling decision; loosening to node-local is a follow-up design question.
    - Edge Case Hunter agent operated on the wrong baseline (missed the `formal/` repo changes entirely because it only checked graphrefly-ts HEAD~1..HEAD); its findings about fast-check invariants #15–#17 are not in this diff's scope.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 3 extended (landed 2026-04-23) — all remaining packages + gate + meta TEARDOWN + cross-axis MC:
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 3 extended (landed 2026-04-23) — all remaining packages + gate + meta TEARDOWN + cross-axis MC:**
  Extended follow-on pass landed after the initial batch-3 closure. Deltas:
  - **(a) `wave_protocol_multisink_batch_MC`** — dedicated 2-node MC with `ExtraSinks[A] = 1` and `BatchSeqs = { <<1, 0>> }`. Validates that the shipped `BuildBatchPendingItems` per-item-cache-snapshot fix is load-bearing on the `BatchEmitMulti` × extra-sinks cross-axis (the original multisink MC has `BatchSeqs = {}`, so invariant #18 was vacuous there). 1322 distinct states, <1s runtime. Reverting the fix trips `MultiSinkIterationCoherent` (#18) within 2 steps.
  - **(b) Package 6 (§1.4 INVALIDATE + cleanup witness)** — new `INVALIDATE` message type, `InvalidateOriginators`/`MaxInvalidates` constants, `invalidateCount`/`cleanupWitness` state, `Invalidate(n)` action, invariant #19 `CleanupWitnessInValueDomain`. MVP: witness-only, no queue propagation, no cache reset — models the hook-firing-pre-reset ordering without disturbing cache/version invariants. Default `InvalidateOriginators = {}` → vacuous in all existing MCs.
  - **(c) Package 5 (§2.5 `completeWhenDepsComplete` / `errorWhenDepsError`)** — `AutoCompleteOnDepsComplete`/`AutoErrorOnDepsError` per-node BOOLEAN constants. `DeliverTerminal` now gates on the flag: TRUE (default) = transition + forward + cleanup; FALSE = absorb the terminal, clear `dirtyMask[c] \ {p}`, stay live (rescue/catchError semantic). Default all-TRUE in existing MCs — no behavior change.
  - **(d) Package 3 (§2.5 `replayBuffer` + `equals` variance)** — `ReplayBufferSize`/`EqualsAbsorbs` per-node constants, `replayBuffer` state variable, `AppendToReplayBuffer` helper, invariant #20 `ReplayBufferBounded`. `Emit` updated to gate equality on `EqualsAbsorbs[src]` and append DATA emissions to the ring. MVP scope: `Emit` only (BatchEmitMulti / DeliverSettle / SinkNestedEmit deferred for follow-on extension).
  - **(e) Package 2 gate-ship + drift-deferred** — `AllExtraPendingEmpty` helper + emission gate shipped on 9 actions (Emit, BatchEmitMulti, Terminate, DeliverDirty, DeliverSettle, DeliverTerminal, Pause, Resume, DeliverPauseResume). Models runtime's atomic `_deliverToSinks` iteration. Stricter drift-catching invariant (`MultiSinkIterationDriftClean` — compares pending DATA to current `cache[n]`) authored and then REVERTED — false-positives on `BatchEmitMulti`'s atomic K-emit cache advance (the TLA+ model enqueues K pending items in one atomic step, but cache ends at final value, so intermediate DATAs look like drift). Clean fix requires refactoring `BatchEmitMulti` into K step actions — substantial restructuring that loses the atomicity simplification; deferred.
  - **(f) Package 7 (§2.3 meta companion TEARDOWN)** — new `TEARDOWN` message type, `MetaCompanions`/`MaxTeardowns` constants, `teardownCount`/`teardownWitness` state, `Teardown(parent)` action, invariant #22 `MetaTeardownObservedPreReset`. MVP: witness-only fan-out, no parent state transition (keeps `EqualsFaithful` / `VersionPerChange` undisturbed). Default `MetaCompanions = [n |-> {}]` → vacuous in all existing MCs.
  - **Verification:** all 9 MCs green. `wave_protocol_gap_MC` still counter-examples `MultiDepHandshakeClean` as designed. 2080 TS tests pass, lint clean. 21 live invariants (up from 18 post-batch-3-initial); 1 reserved-deferred (#21 drift). README.md in `~/src/graphrefly/formal/` updated.

title: Rigor-iterator batch 3 (landed 2026-04-23) — I4 per-source emit counter, BatchEmitMulti per-item snapshot, `resetOnTeardown` parameterization, up() runtime gap doc:
body:
- **Rigor-iterator batch 3 (landed 2026-04-23) — I4 per-source emit counter, BatchEmitMulti per-item snapshot, `resetOnTeardown` parameterization, up() runtime gap doc:**
  Sustained-session sweep of the rigor iterator's smaller / well-scoped items. Deltas:
  - **Package 1 (I4 fix) — `EqualsFaithful` per-source generalization.** New `perSourceEmitCount: NodeIds -> Nat` variable in [wave_protocol.tla](../../graphrefly/formal/wave_protocol.tla); incremented at the emitting source's slot by `Emit` / `BatchEmitMulti` / `SinkNestedEmit`. `EqualsFaithful` now compares `trace[s]` + buffered settlements against `perSourceEmitCount[s]` (was: global `emitCount`). **Re-enabled in `wave_protocol_nested.cfg`** where it was previously excluded for being single-source-implicit; the nested MC's two sources (A, B) now trip the multi-source axis correctly. All 8 MCs green post-fix; nested MC went from 12 invariants back to 13 with 125K distinct states / 9s runtime unchanged.
  - **Package 2 (multi-sink iteration — structural half).** Fixed `BatchEmitMulti`'s per-item cache snapshot: new `BuildBatchPendingItems(vs, cache, src)` helper stamps each item in the bundle with cache-at-its-own-emit-time (DIRTYs carry pre-batch cache; settles carry running-per-step cache). New `EnqueuePendingExtraItems` helper enqueues pre-built items. Added **invariant #18 `MultiSinkIterationCoherent`** (structural form): every pending DATA's `snap[n]` must equal `msg.value`. Catches the BatchEmitMulti "stamp-all-with-final-cache" bug with a concrete counter-example trace. Wired into all 8 cfg files. Green across the board.
  - **Package 4 (`resetOnTeardown` parameterization).** New `ResetOnTeardownNodes: SUBSET NodeIds` constant mirrors spec §2.5. `Resubscribe(sid)` now gates cache reset: `sid \in ResetOnTeardownNodes /\ Parents(sid) # {}` clears to `DefaultInitial`, otherwise preserves the last computed value across the terminal boundary. All existing MCs default `ResetOnTeardownNodesMC == NodeIdsMC \ SourceIdsMC` (matches prior "reset all derived" behavior exactly — state-space unchanged). Axis shipped; a preserve-all MC variant can be added when a concrete consumer needs it.
  - **Package 8 (up-direction PAUSE — option (c) shipped).** JSDoc on [src/core/node.ts](../src/core/node.ts) `up()` documents (a) the leaf-source no-op semantic (runtime does NOT apply PAUSE to its own `_pauseLocks` when `_deps.length === 0`; TLA+ `DeliverUp` encodes the aspirational target contract), and (b) the lockId collision-across-origins gotcha (distinct pauser identities must use distinct lockIds because `_pauseLocks` is a `Set`, not a multiset). Runtime unchanged per option (c) — no in-tree consumer today drives option (a).
  - **Package 9 (spec §2.7 amendment).** Verified: the authoritative "First-run gate (amended 2026-04-23)" block at GRAPHREFLY-SPEC §2.5 already documents both `partial: false` sugar gate and `partial: true` raw-node default post-substrate-fix. The stale "This is NOT a protocol invariant violation" paragraph is gone. No further edit needed.
  - **Still deferred (from batch 3 scope):**
    - **Package 2 drift-catching form** (`head.value = cache[n]` + emission-action gate on `AllExtraPendingEmpty` + optional `activeSinkDelivery` ghost). Blocked on a design call: the gate's abstraction-vs-runtime mapping needs thought (runtime's "atomic iteration" happens synchronously inside one `_emit`; TLA+ actions are atomic state transitions — the mapping is not purely mechanical). Today's structural form catches the BatchEmitMulti bug class; the drift form would additionally catch §32 peer-read bugs at the multi-sink primitive. Scope: ~8 actions gated on the helper + stricter invariant + probable state-space contraction. Next session.
    - **Package 3** (§2.5 `replayBuffer: N` + `equals: () => false`). Requires new `replayBuffer` per-node constant + replay delivery on `SubscribeSink` + equals-fn parameterization. Substantial — new actions, not just state. ~80 LOC TLA+ + fast-check mirror. Next session.
    - **Package 5** (`completeWhenDepsComplete: false` / `errorWhenDepsError: false`). Requires new parameterization on terminal auto-emit in `DeliverSettle` (when all deps terminate and the flag is false, the derived stays live instead of emitting terminal). ~50 LOC TLA+ + fast-check mirror. Next session.
    - **Package 6** (INVALIDATE + fn cleanup firing). Requires INVALIDATE action on source (not currently modeled) + cleanup-hook ghost witness + invariant that cleanup observed before `prevData` reset. ~40 LOC TLA+. Next session.
    - **Package 7** (meta companion TEARDOWN fan-out ordering). Requires modeling meta as a companion-node category + TEARDOWN tier-5 message + `TeardownOrdering` invariant (meta observes TEARDOWN before parent `cache`/`status` reset). Substantial new infrastructure — new node category, new action, new message type. Next session.
    - **Fast-check mirrors for 1 + 2.** The TLA+ changes above don't have runtime-equivalent fast-check properties yet: I4 is purely a model-bookkeeping fix (no observable runtime shape); `MultiSinkIterationCoherent` tests a ghost snapshot that fast-check doesn't currently model. Adding fast-check mirrors for §2.4 multi-sink iteration requires a runtime-observable analogue (e.g. inspecting the runtime's internal `_pendingExtraSinks` queue or wrapping adapters). Tracked as deferred.

- ~~**Up-direction PAUSE semantics — spec §1.4 ambiguity + TLA+/runtime gap**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-23 via option (c): document-only.** JSDoc on [src/core/node.ts](../src/core/node.ts) `up()` now documents the leaf-source no-op (runtime does NOT apply PAUSE to its own `_pauseLocks` when `_deps.length === 0`) and the lockId-collision-across-origins gotcha (`_pauseLocks: Set<unknown>` is NOT a multiset; distinct pauser identities must use distinct lockIds). TLA+ `DeliverUp` stays as the aspirational target contract. Option (a) deferred until a concrete consumer needs leaf-source upstream PAUSE — today no in-tree caller relies on this. Fast-check #16 + TLA+ `UpQueuesCarryControlPlane` / `UpPauseOriginatorBound` still pin the tier guard + originator accounting.

title: Router spread order defaults to intake-wins (done 2026-04-23, QA):
body:
- **Router spread order defaults to intake-wins (done 2026-04-23, QA):**
  `routerInput` effect in `harness/loop.ts` now computes `{ ...classification, ...intakeItem }` so intake fields override any stray classification fields an LLM returns. Kept as a log entry because the previous order (`{ ...intakeItem, ...classification }`) was a latent safety hole: a triage prompt update that started returning `affectsAreas` or `_reingestions` would have silently clobbered the harness's reingestion tracking.
