title: `fallbackAdapter` fixtures API — three fields instead of one union (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer QA):
body:
- **`fallbackAdapter` fixtures API — three fields instead of one union (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer QA):**
  **DONE.** Shipped in [src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts](../src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts). `fixtures: readonly FallbackFixture[]` (inline hand-authored, messages-keyed or hash-keyed; adapter auto-hashes at init), `fixturesDir: string` (cache-format files), `fixturesStorage: StorageTier` (advanced backends). Mutually exclusive — `TypeError` at init if more than one is set. Init-time validator on `fixturesDir` throws a clear `TypeError` on non-cache-format files (checks for `response.content` + `storedAtNs`, flags top-level `messages` as hand-authored-leak) — users drop hand-authored fixtures into a directory and silently miss no more. Tests: 17 in [fallback.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/fallback.test.ts) (+4 new: mutex validators, messages-keyed auto-hashing, record.dir inherit, record.storage/dir mutex). Below kept for history.
  **Original design shape (kept below for history):**
  Current `fixtures?: string | readonly FallbackFixture[] | StorageTier` stuffs three distinct concerns into one shape-discriminated option. Pre-1.0 refactor — split into three fields, mutually exclusive:
  ```ts
  fixtures?: readonly FallbackFixture[];   // hand-authored inline (messages-keyed OR hash-keyed)
  fixturesDir?: string;                    // directory of cache-format files — auto-namespaced
  fixturesStorage?: StorageTier;           // advanced: custom backend (sqlite/indexeddb/remote)
  ```
  **Why:** `fixtures` unambiguously means "inline". `fixturesDir` is the filesystem happy path with auto-namespacing baked in. `fixturesStorage` is the bring-your-own-backend escape hatch, clearly marked advanced.
  **Messages-keyed stays in `fixtures`** — hash-by-hand is dev-hostile. Hash-keyed lives in `fixturesDir` because that's the record-mode file format (copy-pastable filenames from prior record runs).
  **Directory mode: hash-keyed only** — a hand-authored `{messages, response}` file dropped into `fixturesDir` is silently missed today. Add a ~10-LOC init-time validator: scan the dir, parse a sample, throw a clear `TypeError` if shape doesn't match `CachedEntry`. Document clearly. If a user wants hand-authored in a directory, the fix is: hoist to `fixtures: [...]` and let the adapter compute hashes in memory.
  **Alternatives considered:** (A2) auto-convert messages-keyed files via pre-scan wrapper — breaks "file format matches withReplayCache" promise; write-conflict ambiguity with record mode. (A4) hybrid merged tier — same issues plus more code. (A3) two separate directories — more config surface.

title: `fallbackAdapter` + `withReplayCache` directory commingling — auto-namespace `fixturesDir` (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer QA):
body:
- **`fallbackAdapter` + `withReplayCache` directory commingling — auto-namespace `fixturesDir` (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer QA):**
  **DONE.** Shipped as B2 in the same session. `fixturesDir: "./dir"` now resolves internally to `fileStorage(join("./dir", keyPrefix))` — multiple adapters sharing a root directory get distinct subdirs per `keyPrefix`. Same auto-namespacing applied to `record.dir`. `fixturesStorage` (B5) preserved as the escape hatch for custom backends (sqlite/indexeddb/cascading) and for opting out of auto-namespace. `record.dir` defaults to `fixturesDir` when omitted — enables the "read baseline, append misses to same dir" pattern. Below kept for history.
  **Original design shape (kept below for history):**
  Two adapters pointed at the same `dir` with different `keyPrefix` (e.g. `fallback:` vs `llm-replay:`) produce distinct keys so lookups don't collide — but files commingle. Bulk-ops (rm, diff, backup scripts) can't tell tenants apart.
  **Recommendation: auto-namespace when `fixturesDir` / `record.dir` is a string → `fileStorage(join(dir, keyPrefix))`.** This becomes the default: physical separation, zero commingle risk.
  **`fixturesStorage` (StorageTier) is NOT just for back-compat** — it's the door for every non-filesystem backend (sqlite / indexeddb / remote / cascading), which a pure `fixturesDir` can't express. Users who want to OPT OUT of auto-namespacing (e.g., interop with an existing fixture set) can reach for `fixturesStorage: fileStorage(dir)` directly. 95% of users never need this; the `fixturesDir` default just works.
  **Alternatives considered:** (B1) do nothing — operational footgun remains. (B3) manifest file — race-prone. (B4) one-time warning on construction of two adapters sharing a dir — orthogonal; could ship as belt-and-suspenders alongside B2. (B5 as back-compat only) — rejected: B5 serves genuine custom-backend use cases.

- ~~**Node-only imports leaking into browser bundles via `patterns/ai`**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22 (D1, A+B combined).**
  **Option (A) shipped:** sha256 moved from `node:crypto` to `globalThis.crypto.subtle` via new [src/core/hash.ts](../src/core/hash.ts) `sha256Hex()` — universal in Node 18+ and every browser, no `node:*` import. `replay-cache.ts` / `fallback.ts` key computation is now async; `keyFn` widened to `(ctx) => string | Promise<string>` (legacy sync return still accepted). Inline `fallbackAdapter({ fixtures: [...] })` seeds the memory tier asynchronously via a one-shot `seedReady` awaited by `invoke`/`stream` — adapter construction stays synchronous.
  **Option (B) shipped:** filesystem-only fixture options (`fixturesDir`, `record.dir`, `validateDirShape`) split out of [fallback.ts](../src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts) into [fallback-node.ts](../src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback-node.ts). The base `fallbackAdapter` accepts only `fixtures` / `fixturesStorage` / `record.storage` (no `node:*` imports). Node-only convenience lives at new subpath `@graphrefly/graphrefly/patterns/ai/node-middleware` — import `fallbackAdapter` from there for `fixturesDir` / `record.dir`. `package.json` exports + `tsup.config.ts` entry added; tests updated to import the node variant.
  **Residual Node-only leak:** `extra/storage.ts` still co-bundles `memoryStorage` (browser-safe) with `fileStorage` / `sqliteStorage` (inherently Node). Full browser-safety requires splitting `extra/storage.ts` into a browser-safe core + Node extras — tracked as follow-up below.

- ~~**`extra/storage.ts` — split browser-safe core from Node-only backends**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22 (D1 follow-up).**
  Split into three modules with matching package subpaths:
  - [extra/storage-core.ts](../src/extra/storage-core.ts) — browser-safe: `StorageTier`, `StorageHandle`, `memoryStorage`, `dictStorage`, `sortJsonValue`, `stableJsonString`. Zero `node:*` imports. Exposed at `@graphrefly/graphrefly/extra/storage-core`.
  - [extra/storage-node.ts](../src/extra/storage-node.ts) — Node-only: `fileStorage`, `sqliteStorage`. Imports `node:fs` / `node:path` / `node:crypto` / `node:sqlite`. Exposed at `@graphrefly/graphrefly/extra/storage-node`.
  - [extra/storage-browser.ts](../src/extra/storage-browser.ts) — browser-only: `indexedDbStorage`, `fromIDBRequest`, `fromIDBTransaction`, `IndexedDbStorageSpec`. DOM-only. Exposed at `@graphrefly/graphrefly/extra/storage-browser`.
  - [extra/storage.ts](../src/extra/storage.ts) — back-compat barrel that re-exports all three. Existing `@graphrefly/graphrefly/extra` users keep working.
  `fallback.ts` / `replay-cache.ts` / in-tree browser-safe consumers migrated to import from `storage-core` directly. Verified: `dist/patterns/ai.js` and all its transitive chunks now have **zero** `from "node:..."` imports. `dist/extra/storage-core.js` / `dist/extra/storage-browser.js` likewise clean. Regression catcher lives in the `pnpm run build` chunk-grep check in this session's notes.

- ~~**Developer-defined cache key generator for `withReplayCache` / `fallbackAdapter`**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22** — widened `keyFn` to accept `(ctx: ReplayCacheKeyContext) => string` where `ctx = {messages, opts, context}` and `context` shortcuts `opts.keyContext`. Extended `LLMInvokeOptions` with `keyContext?: unknown` slot (stripped from the default canonical hash so it has no effect unless a custom `keyFn` opts in). Legacy 2-arg `keyFn(messages, opts?)` form still accepted — adapter dispatches via `Function.length`. `fallbackAdapter` gained `keyFn?` pass-through option. Regression coverage in [middleware.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/middleware.test.ts) under "B4" (ctx form, legacy form, default-ignore).

- ~~**`graph.add(node, opts)` — signature flip**~~ **DONE 2026-04-22.** Shipped at [src/graph/graph.ts:1138](../src/graph/graph.ts:1138). New signature: `graph.add(node, { name?, annotation? })` — name lives in opts, falls back to `node.name` from the node's own options; throws on non-empty-name miss. **Migrated 608 call sites across 39 files** via two TypeScript AST codemods (archived to `TRASH/`): v1 for `.add("LITERAL", node)` (582 sites, 38 files), v2 for variable/template-literal first-arg (`.add(name, node)` where `name` is a local — 25 sites, 7 files); plus 3 hand-touched edge cases (nestjs/explorer.ts, harness/loop.ts, snapshot-rehydrate). Legacy string-first signature removed entirely — no back-compat. Tests: 1997 passing, lint clean, build green.

- ~~**Inspection-surface consolidation pass #2**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22 (D2; QA-hardened same day).**
  - **`explain` / `reactiveExplainPath`** → `graph.explain(from, to, { reactive: true, ... })` overload (landed earlier). QA pass added a `disposed` flag so queued flush-hook firings after `dispose()` don't resurrect a torn-down derived.
  - **`describe` / `graphLens`** → `graph.describe({ reactive: true })` now returns `ReactiveDescribeHandle<GraphDescribeOutput | string>` — `{ node, dispose }` where `node` emits a fresh `GraphDescribeOutput` (or format string, if `format` is set) every time the graph settles. Same coalescing as the reactive explain (one recompute per outermost drain via `registerBatchFlushHook`). QA pass (2026-04-22): recursive topology subscription — a parent's `topology` is own-graph only, so `_describeReactive` now walks `_mounts` at subscribe time + listens for `nodeKind: "mount"` events to subscribe to newly-mounted children transitively. Also added `disposed` flag. `graphLens` stays alongside for users who want `.flow` / `.why` / `.stats` — the new reactive-describe is the minimal primitive.
  - **`harnessTrace` → `observe({ format: "stage-log" })`** — `ObserveOptions.format` extended with `"stage-log"` + a `stageLabels?: Record<string, string>` option. The format auto-enables `timeline: true` and renders `[Xs] STAGE ← preview` / `✗ err` / `■ complete` lines — domain-agnostic, works for any graph. `patterns/harness/trace.ts` rewritten as thin sugar over `graph.observe({ format: "stage-log", stageLabels: HARNESS_STAGES })`. QA pass: silent `catch {}` on missing stage paths replaced with synthetic `observe-unavailable` error trace events — dry-run equivalence preserved.

- ~~**Stdout-native DAG flowchart renderer: library feature**~~ **SHIPPED (TS) 2026-04-23.**
  `graph.describe({ format: "ascii" })` implemented as full Sugiyama (not Sugiyama-lite — **virtual-node splitting + per-gutter track assignment are both in**, so crossing count is minimal and the renderer stays correct at 100+ nodes, not just 50-ish).
  **Files:** [src/graph/_layout-sugiyama.ts](../src/graph/_layout-sugiyama.ts) (layout pipeline: layer assignment → virtual-node splitting for multi-layer edges → barycenter crossing minimization with transposition polish → median-aligned coordinate assignment → per-gutter cross-axis track-assigned orthogonal routing), [src/graph/_ascii-grid.ts](../src/graph/_ascii-grid.ts) (char-grid blitter with Unicode `─│┌┐└┘┬┴├┤┼▶▼` glyphs and an `asciiCharset: "ascii"` fallback using `-|+>v`), [src/graph/describe-ascii.ts](../src/graph/describe-ascii.ts) (top-level renderer, option parsing, direction normalization), [src/graph/_ascii-width.ts](../src/graph/_ascii-width.ts) (shared `cellWidth`/`countCells`/`truncateToCells` utility — extracted from `patterns/reactive-layout/measurement-adapters.ts` so CJK/combining-mark cell math has a single source of truth; the CLI measurement adapter now imports from this utility).
  **Options added to `GraphDescribeOptions`:** `format: "ascii"`, `direction: "LR" | "TD"` (BT/RL throw with a clear message — ASCII grid semantics are only meaningful for LR/TD), `maxLabelWidth?: number` (default 24), `asciiCharset?: "unicode" | "ascii"`. Reactive overload (`describe({ reactive: true, format: "ascii" })`) picks up for free via the existing generic overload.
  **Wide-character handling:** grid is cell-indexed; a 2-cell CJK char occupies one grid slot and a `""` continuation slot so `row.join("")` produces a single visible wide glyph without spurious adjacent narrow-space leakage.
  **Arrow-convergence convention:** when multiple edges terminate at the same entry cell (e.g. `c→d` + `a→d` via virtuals, both ending at `(d.x-1, d.yMid)`), they share the `▶` tip glyph — one arrow per target entry point, not one per edge. Visually correct; tests assert ≥3 tips on a 4-edge diamond-through-virtuals fixture rather than 4.
  **Invariants check:** pure string math, no `node:*` / DOM imports, universal tier (no new subpath — extends existing `describe` surface). `tsup.config.ts assertBrowserSafeBundles` unaffected. No polling, no imperative, no async. Spec §3.6 `describe` shape unchanged.
  **Tests:** 14 in [src/__tests__/graph/describe-ascii.test.ts](../src/__tests__/graph/describe-ascii.test.ts) — single-node box, LR + TD chain shape, diamond, long edge spanning 3 layers (virtual-node routing), ASCII charset fallback, label truncation with ellipsis, CJK width, subgraph-qualified paths (`sub::a`), invalid-direction throw, logger callback, reactive handle recomputes on graph change, 100-node wide+deep DAG in <2s, LR/TD label-set parity. 2064 tests pass, lint clean, build green.
  **Follow-ups:** subgraph bounding-boxes (mounts currently render as `sub::child` labels with no enclosing rectangle — adds ~80 LOC for placement that avoids overlapping inner routing; defer until a dogfood session hits it). Edge labels (dep index / annotation on arrows) deferred.  `"sixel"` format deferred to a separate task (different rendering model — pixel-encoded vs character grid). `[py-parity-describe-ascii]` — port the same pipeline to graphrefly-py per the standing PY deferral policy; blocked on rigor infrastructure per top-level note.

title: `fallbackAdapter` — library provider with `provider: "fallback"` (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer session):
body:
- **`fallbackAdapter` — library provider with `provider: "fallback"` (opened + resolved 2026-04-21, inbox-reducer session):**
  **DONE.** Shipped at [src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts](../src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts), wired into `createAdapter({ provider: "fallback", ... })`, re-exported via `@graphrefly/graphrefly/patterns/ai`. 13 tests at [src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/fallback.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/fallback.test.ts). Covers: inline-map / directory / array fixture sources; `onMiss: "throw" | "respond"`; canned degraded response when no `respond` supplied; record mode persisting invoke + stream transcripts (with per-chunk delay capture); `fixtures + record` combined (replay-then-record-on-miss); stream replay respects `replaySpeed`; invoke-only fixture synthesizes a single-chunk stream. Key shape matches `withReplayCache` — fixture files are interchangeable. Duplicated `canonicalJson` from replay-cache.ts; flagged to dedupe when a second consumer needs it.
  **Original design shape (kept below for history):**
  A first-class `LLMAdapter` alongside `anthropicAdapter` / `openAICompatAdapter` / `googleAdapter` / `ollamaAdapter` / `dryRunAdapter`, but whose role is to serve pre-recorded or canned responses when real providers aren't reachable. `adapter.provider === "fallback"` makes its role self-documenting in logs, stats, and cost tables (it charges $0).
  **Not a composer.** Users install it as a tier via the existing mechanisms — no new wrapper needed:
  ```ts
  cascadingLlmAdapter([
    { name: "primary",  adapter: anthropicAdapter(...) },
    { name: "fallback", adapter: fallbackAdapter({ fixtures: "./fixtures" }) },
  ]);
  // or via the resilientAdapter sugar:
  resilientAdapter(anthropicAdapter(...), {
    fallback: fallbackAdapter({ fixtures: "./fixtures" }),
  });
  ```
  **Fix shape:** `fallbackAdapter(opts)` returning an `LLMAdapter`, where `opts` supports:
  - `fixtures: string | { [key: string]: LLMResponse }` — directory of recorded `{request, response}` JSON files, OR an inline map keyed on prompt+model hash. Streaming fixtures record chunks + cadence and replay at original pace (or accelerated via `replaySpeed`).
  - `respond?: (messages, opts?) => string | LLMResponse` — scripted fallback for cases without a fixture. Same shape as `dryRunAdapter`'s `respond` callback.
  - `onMiss?: "throw" | "respond" | "degrade"` — miss policy. `"degrade"` returns a canned "service unavailable" response marked with `metadata.degraded: true` so callers can render a UX hint.
  - `record?: { adapter: LLMAdapter, dir: string }` — record mode: wrap a real adapter, persist every `{request, response}` pair to `dir` as it flows through, keyed on a stable hash (same pattern as `withReplayCache`). Running `fallbackAdapter` in record mode against real traffic populates a fixture set that later runs can replay offline.
  **Three use cases, one adapter:**
  1. **Users' apps** — install as the `fallback` tier in `cascadingLlmAdapter` / `resilientAdapter` so their app degrades gracefully when cloud provider fails or network is offline.
  2. **Tests** — `record-missing` style: first CI run records, committed fixtures drive deterministic replays thereafter. Miss in read-only mode fails loudly.
  3. **Evals offline fallback** — replaces the ad-hoc `evals/lib/replay-cache.ts` implementation. `fallbackAdapter` fed a fixtures dir produces identical eval traces with no API spend. (Today's `evals/lib/replay-cache.ts` is essentially this pattern with its own key function + storage shape — consolidate.)
  **Shared implementation.** Obviates the "should `mock-llm.ts` be a public test helper?" question from roadmap Phase 7.6 — this adapter IS the shared mechanism. `src/__tests__/helpers/mock-llm.ts` can be retired or simplified to thin wrapper on top.
  **Scope:** `src/patterns/ai/adapters/providers/fallback.ts` — the adapter itself. Fixture file format shared with `withReplayCache` (JSON Lines, sha256 key) so fixtures produced by either tool work with the other. Streaming support mandatory. ~250 LOC, no new deps. `createAdapter({ provider: "fallback", … })` dispatches to it so users can configure via the same preset flow as other providers.

- ~~**Dry-run should exercise every observability code path the real run exercises**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22 (D4; extended in QA pass).** Shipped `validateGraphObservability(graph, { paths?, pairs?, requireFound?, formats? })` in [src/graph/validate-observability.ts](../src/graph/validate-observability.ts) — adapter-agnostic utility that walks `describe()` (checks dangling deps), `describe({ format })` for each requested format (mermaid / pretty / json / d2 / mermaid-url), `graph.resolve(path)` for observe-resolvability (does NOT subscribe — avoids waking `fromTimer` / `fromPromise` side effects), and `explain(from, to)` (checks chain.found). Returns `{ ok, checks, failures, summary() }` — non-throwing so callers can exit non-zero with a diagnostic. `examples/inbox-reducer/index.ts` migrated from ad-hoc dry-run checks to the utility; the local `mermaidLiveUrl` helper was deleted (`describe({ format: "mermaid-url" })` covers it). Regression tests in [validate-observability.test.ts](../src/__tests__/graph/validate-observability.test.ts) incl. "D1 formats" coverage.

- ~~**`withBudgetGate` `onExhausted` fires on every blocked attempt, not on the open→closed edge**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22** — `onExhausted` is now edge-triggered via a dedicated `isOpen.subscribe` watcher that fires exactly once on each open→closed transition; `buildClosedError` no longer fires the callback on per-attempt rejections. JSDoc updated. Cross-cutting convention for breakers: the `CircuitBreaker` primitive already has no imperative callbacks (state is a reactive node / getter), so the same level-triggered bug shape cannot occur there — any future breaker callback must follow the same edge-trigger rule. Regression test in [qa-regressions.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/qa-regressions.test.ts) under "B8 withBudgetGate".


- ~~**Rate-limiter sharing across multiple `resilientAdapter` instances**~~ **RESOLVED 2026-04-22** — `WithRateLimiterOptions.limiter?: AdaptiveRateLimiterBundle` landed. When supplied, `withRateLimiter` reuses the passed bundle rather than constructing a fresh one; `rpm`/`tpm`/`adaptation`/`burstMultiplier`/`name` are ignored in that case (the shared bundle owns those). Tests in [middleware.test.ts](../src/__tests__/patterns/ai/adapters/middleware.test.ts) cover both shared-bundle reuse and independent-limiter default.

title: `patterns/memory` Wave A landed (2026-04-25, public-face primitives; QA-hardened 2026-04-26):
body:
- **`patterns/memory` Wave A landed (2026-04-25, public-face primitives; QA-hardened 2026-04-26):** [src/patterns/memory/index.ts](../src/patterns/memory/index.ts) rewritten per `archive/docs/SESSION-public-face-blocks-review.md` Units 1–5. Five primitives — `decay` (JSDoc only), `lightCollection`, `collection`, `vectorIndex`, `knowledgeGraph` (renamed from `KnowledgeGraphGraph`). All four data primitives now reactive-only on the read side: `itemNode` / `hasNode` / `searchNode` / `relatedNode` are the canonical reads; imperative `get / has / peek / getItem / search / related` dropped per the no-imperative-reads policy. Storage migrated to `reactiveMap` everywhere (single source of truth for LRU + retention). `collection.ranked` is fully reactive on time via a `fromTimer`-driven `refresh_tick_ns` source when `decayRate > 0`; ranked is lazy (no internal keepalive — call `keepalive(coll.ranked)` for eager activation). `vectorIndex` becomes `VectorIndexGraph<TMeta>` with reactive `searchNode`, `reindex()`, retention-driven HNSW eviction sync, optional adapter `dispose()`, and strict-dimension default. `knowledgeGraph` ships symmetric `adjacencyOut` + `adjacencyIn` deriveds (full O(E) rebuild per mutation — see JSDoc), triple-keyed `reactiveMap` edges (O(1) link/unlink at the edge map; adjacency rebuild is the bottleneck), `entityCount` / `edgeCount` observability deriveds, and `orphanGC: "keep" | "remove"` option (now applied to BOTH `unlink` cascades AND `removeEntity` cascades). Every imperative mutation wrapped via `lightMutation` from `_internal/imperative-audit.ts`; each primitive ships a public `events: ReactiveLogBundle<...AuditRecord>` audit log (bounded 1024). Retention-driven evictions on `vectorIndex` now emit a synthetic `"evict"` audit record (with `seq` advance) so replay consumers can reconstruct the live snapshot from `events` alone — `lightCollection` and `collection` use built-in `reactiveMap` LRU which has no `onArchive` hook today; that gap is documented and queued for the same `evict`-record extension when an `onArchive`-on-built-in-LRU surface lands. `domainMeta("memory", kind)` tagging on all internal nodes for mermaid grouping. **PY parity deferred** per the broader PY parity stance (rigor infrastructure first); when the parity pass runs, mirror Wave A in `graphrefly-py` `src/graphrefly/patterns/memory/`.

  **QA fixes (2026-04-26, post-/qa adversarial review):** (A1) `searchNode` equality now compares `id` + `score` + `meta` per position — previous id-only comparator silently dropped score-only changes from re-upserts that preserved ranking order. (B1) `vectorIndex.upsert / remove / clear` reversed mutation order — HNSW commits BEFORE the reactive `entries` write so an adapter throw aborts cleanly without leaving entries diverged from the index. (C1) `orphanGC: "remove"` extended to `removeEntity` cascades so cascading entity removal can't leave dangling orphans. (D1) Orphan-remove audit records now bump `seqCursor` (previously bare `events.append(...)` skipped the cursor advance, leaving gaps in replay sequence). (E1) `vectorIndex` retention-driven evictions emit synthetic `"evict"` audit records via `bumpCursor`. (F1) Defense-in-depth `keepalive(entries)` registered inside `vectorIndex` so downstream sync consumers (`patterns/ai/memory/runRetrieval`) reading `.cache` don't depend on external subscription. (G) `vectorIndex.clear()` skips the explicit `hnsw.clear()` when retention is configured — the per-entry `onArchive` already drained the adapter; suppressing via a `clearInProgress` flag. **Auto-fixes:** dimension/k/empty guards on `searchNode` (degrade to `[]` instead of throwing or returning meaningless all-zero scores); `Math.max(0, Math.floor(k))` instead of `| 0` truncation; HNSW return defensively copied; `cosineSimilarity` returns `0` on non-finite scores; `inferredDimension` reset on `clear()`; `entityHasReferences` uses O(1) `adjacency.cache` lookups instead of O(E) edge scan (also closes the latent post-`deleteMany` cache-timing dependency); `lightCollection.upsertImpl` reads prior `createdAtNs` via the snapshot Node (no LRU-touch side effect); `link` audit record now includes `weight`; `rankedEqual` also compares `value` reference; `vectorIndex.upsert` defensively shallow-copies `meta` (Array spread or object spread depending on shape); JSDoc tightening on `lightCollection` (audit freeze contract, audit no-op records), `collection` (rescore pre-batch ordering caveat), and `knowledgeGraph` (adjacency O(E)-per-mutation cost; NUL separator caller responsibility). **Tracking item:** `decay` PY sibling — port when a 2nd caller emerges (e.g. `harness.strategy.successRate`).

title: Pre-1.0 breaking change — DB read adapters emit `Node<T[]>` (Wave 5, 2026-04-16):
body:
- **Pre-1.0 breaking change — DB read adapters emit `Node<T[]>` (Wave 5, 2026-04-16):**
  `fromSqlite`, `fromPrisma`, `fromDrizzle`, `fromKysely` now emit **one** `[[DATA, rows[]]]` then `COMPLETE`, replacing the old "N per-row DATA + COMPLETE" shape. Matches how the drivers natively return results and makes the adapters flatMap-ready (`source | switchMap(fromSqlite(...))` yields array-shaped DATAs that downstream can `from(...)` / iterate). **Migration:** downstream consumers expecting per-row streaming must flatten via a new operator (e.g. `mergeAll` over the array) or use the new `fromSqliteCursor` for row-by-row streaming. Release note must call this out in the Wave 5 changelog.

title: Pre-1.0 breaking change — `fromHTTP` default no longer emits `COMPLETE` (Wave 5, 2026-04-16):
body:
- **Pre-1.0 breaking change — `fromHTTP` default no longer emits `COMPLETE` (Wave 5, 2026-04-16):**
  Default `fromHTTP(url)` now emits `DATA` and stays live (cached replay to late subscribers via push-on-subscribe §2.2). Users who want the old one-shot semantic opt in with `{ completeAfterFetch: true }`. Users who need a companion `"done"` signal under the new default should use that option until the `fetched: Node<boolean>` companion ships (see Active item above).

title: Pre-1.0 breaking change — first-run gate is now universal at `node()` (Wave 6, 2026-04-23):
body:
- **Pre-1.0 breaking change — first-run gate is now universal at `node()` (Wave 6, 2026-04-23):**
  Every compute node — sugar `derived` / `effect` AND raw `node([deps], fn)` — now defaults to `partial: false` (gate ON). Multi-parent activation produces the clean `[[START]], [[DIRTY]], [[DATA, fn(init...)]]` handshake instead of the pre-fix sequential `[[START]], [[DIRTY]], [[RESOLVED]], [[DIRTY]], [[DATA, ...]]`. Operators that deliberately fire on partial deps opt out with `partial: true`:
  - **`withLatestFrom`**, **`valve`** ([src/extra/operators.ts](../src/extra/operators.ts)) — use the new `partialOperatorOpts` helper.
  - **worker-bridge / worker-self aggregators** ([src/extra/worker/bridge.ts](../src/extra/worker/bridge.ts), [src/extra/worker/self.ts](../src/extra/worker/self.ts)) — `{ name, partial: true }` at construction.
  - All `merge` / `concat` / `race` / `zip` / `sample` / `buffer*` / `window*` operators are unaffected (they use the producer pattern, which has no declared deps for the gate to act on).
  - Zero-dep producer-pattern factories (`stratify`, `budgetGate`, `distill`, `verifiable`) are unaffected for the same reason.
  **Migration for external consumers:** external code calling raw `node([dep1, dep2], fn)` and expecting the fn to fire on partial deps must add `{ partial: true }` to the opts. External code calling `derived` / `effect` with multi deps previously got the gap-aware `[DIRTY], [RESOLVED]` intermediate pair on activation; now sees only the final `[DIRTY], [DATA]`. Counters of "activation settled" markers must switch to counting DATA, not DIRTY/RESOLVED.
  **`autoTrackNode` inline sentinel guard was also removed in the same pass** — it replicated sugar's `sentinelGuard` semantics over the lazy dep set and now delegates to the core gate. Net behavior change: autoTrackNode no longer re-gates fn after an INVALIDATE clears a tracked dep's `prevData`. Consumers that relied on this re-gate must handle `undefined` / `null` dep values in their fn body (track() returns `ctx.prevData[idx]` which is `undefined` for a freshly invalidated dep). See spec §2.5 INVALIDATE amendment + §2.7 for the unified gate semantics.

title: Pre-1.0 breaking change — `[ERROR, payload]` requires non-`undefined` payload (Wave 6, 2026-04-23):
body:
- **Pre-1.0 breaking change — `[ERROR, payload]` requires non-`undefined` payload (Wave 6, 2026-04-23):**
  Spec §1.2 amended: `[[ERROR, undefined]]` and bare `[[ERROR]]` are now protocol violations, parallel to `[[DATA, undefined]]` / bare `[[DATA]]`. `_emit` enforces the rule with a `TypeError` at the dispatch boundary; the message points callers at `new Error("reason")` or domain-tag values. **Why:** `DepRecord.terminal === undefined` encodes "dep is live" — an `ERROR(undefined)` would be indistinguishable from no termination at every downstream `_maybeAutoTerminalAfterWave` check, swallowing the error silently. The collision was previously documented as a soft user contract; now enforced. **Migration:** any external producer that emitted `ERROR` with an `undefined` payload (rare — typically a faulty Promise.reject(undefined)) must wrap with a meaningful payload. In-tree: zero call sites affected; `_depSettledAsTerminal`'s "Edge case" JSDoc updated to reference the new guard.

title: Operator-layer: `filter` mixed-batch RESOLVED forwarding (2026-04-24)
body: - **Operator-layer: `filter` mixed-batch RESOLVED forwarding (2026-04-24).** When `filter`'s source emits a batch with mixed pass/fail values (e.g. `[null, "x", null]`), `filter` only emits the passing values and does NOT forward a `RESOLVED` for the dropped batch entries. Tier-3 counters that track "batch fully drained" via per-message RESOLVED tagging could drift in this case — though no current consumer relies on per-item RESOLVED accounting. Surfaced by Edge Case Hunter during Wave A Unit 4 QA review of [src/patterns/ai/prompts/streaming.ts](../src/patterns/ai/prompts/streaming.ts) where `filter` was newly introduced as load-bearing for the `gatedStream` null-drop. Decision: defer to a focused operator-layer review that decides (a) whether mixed-batch emission semantics need normalization across `filter` / `map` / `take` / `skip`, (b) whether per-item RESOLVED tagging is even part of the operator contract, (c) whether tier-3 counters should rely on it. Scope is operator-layer-wide, not pattern-specific.
resolution: RESOLVED by spec §1.3.3 'Tier-3 wave exclusivity' + COMPOSITION-GUIDE §41 (both authored post-2026-04-24, post-dating this deferral). Protocol-level rule: within any single wave at any single node, DATA and RESOLVED are mutually exclusive — RESOLVED only when the entire wave produces zero DATA, never per-dropped-item, never trailing a wave that already emitted DATA. (a) Emission semantics ARE normalized across `filter`/`map`/`take`/`skip`/`takeWhile`/`distinctUntilChanged`/`pairwise`/`scan`/`reduce` — all conform to wave-exclusivity. (b) Per-item RESOLVED tagging is REJECTED at the protocol level. (c) Tier-3 counters MUST count upstream of operators, not on operator output — `filter` JSDoc (transform.ts:51-60) codifies this caller responsibility. Walked as DS-11.10 on 2026-04-30; finding was that the deferral pre-dated the spec rule that resolves it. Action items at recognition: archive this entry, mark DS-11.10 ✅ RESOLVED in implementation-plan, optionally add JSDoc cross-reference parity to the other operators (deferred — not strictly necessary for protocol correctness).

title: Surface `restoreSnapshot` rejects `mode: "diff"` records — revisit after §8.7 WAL replay (opened 2026-04-19, §9.3 QA)
body:
- **Surface `restoreSnapshot` rejects `mode: "diff"` records — revisit after §8.7 WAL replay (opened 2026-04-19, §9.3 QA):**
  `src/patterns/surface/snapshot.ts` `unwrapCheckpoint` throws `restore-failed` when the tier's latest record is a `mode: "diff"` `GraphCheckpointRecord`. Today that's unreplayable (no WAL history storage), so callers must either use `saveSnapshot` (always writes full) or configure `attachStorage({compactEvery: 1})`. **Fix shape:** once Phase 8.7 (Delta checkpoints & WAL) lands, `restoreSnapshot` should read the baseline + replay WAL entries up to the target seq. The StorageTier contract will need to grow a `listByPrefix(prefix)` / `readWAL(key)` to expose the history. **Coordinate with §8.7 design** so the surface and auto-checkpoint paths share the same replay implementation.
